text: Alice was paid $1200 in 2019 for services performed in jail. Alice was committed to jail from January 24, 2015 to May 5th, 2019. From May 5th 2019 to Dec 31st 2019, Alice was paid $5320 in remuneration. Alice takes the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2019?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ─────────────────────────  STATUTORY RULES  ───────────────────────── */

/* ---- wages, gross-income, and taxable-income computations ---- */

% sumlist(List, Sum)  – simple deterministic list-sum helper
sumlist([],Acc,Acc).
sumlist([H|T],Acc0,Sum) :-
    Acc1 is Acc0 + H,
    sumlist(T,Acc1,Sum).

% gross_income(Person, Year, Gross)
gross_income(P,Y,GI) :-
    findall(Amt, wages(P,Y,Amt,_Source), L),
    sumlist(L,0,GI).

/* ---- filing-status rules ---- */

% default facts – no information indicates “no”
married(_P,_Y)       :- fail.          % no marriage facts supplied
surviving_spouse(_P,_Y) :- fail.       % none in this case
head_of_household(_P,_Y) :- fail.      % none in this case

status(P,Y,single) :-
    \+ married(P,Y),
    \+ surviving_spouse(P,Y),
    \+ head_of_household(P,Y).

/* ---- standard deduction (special 2018-2025 amounts) ---- */
standard_deduction(single,             _Y, 12000).
standard_deduction(head_of_household,  _Y, 18000).
standard_deduction(surviving_spouse,   _Y, 24000).
standard_deduction(married_joint,      _Y, 24000).
standard_deduction(married_separate,   _Y, 12000).

standard_deduction_for(P,Y,Ded) :-
    status(P,Y,S),
    standard_deduction(S,Y,Ded).

/* ---- taxable income ---- */
taxable_income(P,Y,TI) :-
    gross_income(P,Y,GI),
    standard_deduction_for(P,Y,SD),
    TI0 is GI - SD,
    ( TI0 > 0 -> TI = TI0 ; TI = 0 ).

/* ---- individual income-tax rates, §1(c) brackets ---- */
tax_single(TI,Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 22100 ->
        Tax is 0.15 * TI
    ; TI =< 53500 ->
        Tax is 3315 + 0.28 * (TI - 22100)
    ; TI =< 115000 ->
        Tax is 12107 + 0.31 * (TI - 53500)
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        Tax is 31172 + 0.36 * (TI - 115000)
    ;   Tax is 79772 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000)
    ).

/* ---- top-level tax predicate ---- */
tax(P,Y,Tax) :-
    status(P,Y,single),            % the only possible status here
    taxable_income(P,Y,TI),
    tax_single(TI,Tax).



/* ─────────────────────────────  CASE FACTS  ───────────────────────────── */

% wages(Person, Year, Amount, SourceTag)
wages(alice, 2019, 1200, jail_service).
wages(alice, 2019, 5320, post_release_work).



/* ───────────────────────────────  QUERY  ─────────────────────────────── */

:- tax(alice, 2019, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice and Harold got married on Sep 3rd, 1992. Harold and Alice have a son, born Jan 25th, 2000. Harold died on Feb 28th, 2016. They had been living in the same house since 1993, maintained by Alice. Alice and her son continued doing so after Harold's death. Alice's gross income for the year 2017 was $236422. Alice has employed Bob, Cameron, Dan, Emily, Fred and George for agricultural labor from Sep 9th to Oct 1st 2017, and paid them $5012 each. Alice takes the standard deduction in 2017.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
% Facts from the case
spouse("Alice","Harold").
died("Harold",2016).
not_remarried("Alice",2017).
joint_return_possible("Alice","Harold",2016).

child("Alice","Son").
birth_year("Son",2000).
younger_than("Son","Alice").
principal_abode("Son","Alice",2017).
not_joint_return("Son",2017).

maintains_home("Alice",2017).
half_cost("Alice",2017).

gross_income("Alice",2017,236422).
takes_standard_deduction("Alice",2017).

wage("Alice","Bob",2017,5012,agricultural,cash).
wage("Alice","Cameron",2017,5012,agricultural,cash).
wage("Alice","Dan",2017,5012,agricultural,cash).
wage("Alice","Emily",2017,5012,agricultural,cash).
wage("Alice","Fred",2017,5012,agricultural,cash).
wage("Alice","George",2017,5012,agricultural,cash).

% Utility
sum_list([],0).
sum_list([H|T],S) :- sum_list(T,S1), S is H+S1.

round2(X,R) :- R is round(X*100)/100.

% Age
age(Person,Year,Age) :- birth_year(Person,BY), Age is Year-BY.

% Child relationship
child_of(Child,Parent) :- child(Parent,Child).

% Qualifying child
qualifying_child(T,Child,Year) :-
    child_of(Child,T),
    principal_abode(Child,T,Year),
    age(Child,Year,Age),
    Age < 25,
    younger_than(Child,T),
    not_joint_return(Child,Year).

dependent(T,Child,Year) :- qualifying_child(T,Child,Year).

% Surviving spouse
surviving_spouse(P,Year) :-
    spouse(P,Sp),
    died(Sp,DY),
    (DY is Year-1 ; DY is Year-2),
    not_remarried(P,Year),
    maintains_home(P,Year),
    half_cost(P,Year),
    dependent(P,Child,Year),
    principal_abode(Child,P,Year),
    joint_return_possible(P,Sp,DY).

% Married status
married(P,Year) :-
    spouse(P,Sp),
    ( \+ died(Sp,_) ; (died(Sp,DY), DY >= Year) ).

% Head of household (not used but included)
head_of_household(P,Year) :-
    \+ married(P,Year),
    \+ surviving_spouse(P,Year),
    maintains_home(P,Year),
    half_cost(P,Year),
    dependent(P,Child,Year),
    principal_abode(Child,P,Year).

% Filing status
filing_status(P,Year,surviving_spouse) :- surviving_spouse(P,Year).
filing_status(P,Year,head_of_household) :- head_of_household(P,Year).
filing_status(P,Year,single) :- \+ married(P,Year), \+ surviving_spouse(P,Year), \+ head_of_household(P,Year).

% Standard deduction
base_single(Year,B) :- (Year>=2018,Year<2026 -> B=12000 ; B=3000).
base_hoh(Year,B) :- (Year>=2018,Year<2026 -> B=18000 ; B=4400).

basic_standard_deduction(Status,Year,Amount) :-
    (Status=surviving_spouse -> base_single(Year,B), Amount is 2*B ;
     Status=head_of_household -> base_hoh(Year,Amount) ;
     base_single(Year,Amount)).

additional_standard_deduction(_,_,0).

standard_deduction(P,Year,Amount) :-
    takes_standard_deduction(P,Year),
    filing_status(P,Year,Status),
    basic_standard_deduction(Status,Year,B),
    additional_standard_deduction(P,Year,A),
    Amount is B + A.

% Personal exemptions
exemption_amount(Year,Amount) :- (Year>=2018,Year<2026 -> Amount=0 ; Amount=2000).

num_exemptions(P,Year,Num) :-
    findall(D, dependent(P,D,Year), L),
    length(L,N),
    Num is N + 1.

personal_exemptions(P,Year,Total) :-
    exemption_amount(Year,Ex),
    num_exemptions(P,Year,Num),
    Total is Ex * Num.

% Adjusted gross income
adjusted_gross_income(P,Year,AGI) :- gross_income(P,Year,AGI).

% Taxable income
taxable_income(P,Year,Taxable) :-
    adjusted_gross_income(P,Year,AGI),
    standard_deduction(P,Year,SD),
    personal_exemptions(P,Year,PE),
    Taxable is AGI - SD - PE.

% Income tax brackets
tax_from_brackets(surviving_spouse,TI,Tax) :-
    (TI =< 36900 -> Tax is 0.15*TI ;
     TI =< 89150 -> Tax is 5535 + 0.28*(TI-36900) ;
     TI =< 140000 -> Tax is 20165 + 0.31*(TI-89150) ;
     TI =< 250000 -> Tax is 35928.50 + 0.36*(TI-140000) ;
     Tax is 75528.50 + 0.396*(TI-250000)).
tax_from_brackets(head_of_household,TI,Tax) :-
    (TI =< 29600 -> Tax is 0.15*TI ;
     TI =< 76400 -> Tax is 4440 + 0.28*(TI-29600) ;
     TI =< 127500 -> Tax is 17544 + 0.31*(TI-76400) ;
     TI =< 250000 -> Tax is 33385 + 0.36*(TI-127500) ;
     Tax is 77485 + 0.396*(TI-250000)).
tax_from_brackets(single,TI,Tax) :-
    (TI =< 22100 -> Tax is 0.15*TI ;
     TI =< 53500 -> Tax is 3315 + 0.28*(TI-22100) ;
     TI =< 115000 -> Tax is 12107 + 0.31*(TI-53500) ;
     TI =< 250000 -> Tax is 31172 + 0.36*(TI-115000) ;
     Tax is 79772 + 0.396*(TI-250000)).

income_tax(P,Year,Tax) :-
    filing_status(P,Year,Status),
    taxable_income(P,Year,TI),
    tax_from_brackets(Status,TI,Tax).

% Employer tax for agricultural labor
total_cash_wages_agri(P,Year,Total) :-
    findall(A, wage(P,_,Year,A,agricultural,cash), L),
    sum_list(L,Total).

employer_agricultural(P,Year) :-
    total_cash_wages_agri(P,Year,Total),
    Total >= 20000.

employment_agricultural(P,Year) :- employer_agricultural(P,Year).

capped_wage(A,W) :- (A =< 7000 -> W=A ; W=7000).

wages_for_employment(P,Year,Total) :-
    employment_agricultural(P,Year),
    findall(W, (wage(P,_,Year,A,agricultural,cash), capped_wage(A,W)), L),
    sum_list(L,Total).

employer_tax(P,Year,Tax) :-
    wages_for_employment(P,Year,Total),
    Tax is 0.06*Total.

% Total tax
tax(P,Year,Tax) :-
    income_tax(P,Year,IT),
    (employer_tax(P,Year,ET) -> true ; ET=0),
    Sum is IT + ET,
    round2(Sum,Tax).

:- tax("Alice", 2017, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

text: Alice's gross income for the year 2006 is $97407. Bob's gross income for the year 2006 is $136370. Alice and Bob have been married since Feb 3rd, 2006. Bob and Alice file a joint return for 2006 and take the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Bob have to pay in 2006?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ----------  FACTS  ---------- */
gross_income(alice, 2006, 97407).
gross_income(bob,   2006, 136370).

married(bob,  alice, date(2006,2,3)).
file_joint_return(bob, alice, 2006).

/* ----------  GENERAL RULES  ---------- */

/* spouse/2  – true if either order appears in the married fact               */
spouse(X,Y) :- married(X,Y,_).
spouse(X,Y) :- married(Y,X,_).

/* exemption_amount/2 – §151(d)(1) : $2,000                                   */
exemption_amount(2006, 2000).

/* basic_standard_deduction/3 – §63(c)(2)                                     */
/* For a joint return: 200% × $3,000 = $6,000                                 */
basic_standard_deduction(2006, joint, 6000).

/* personal_exemptions_joint/3 – only the couple, no dependents given         */
personal_exemptions_joint(Y, N, PE) :-
    exemption_amount(Y, EA),
    PE is EA * N.

/* taxable_income/5 – TI = GI – SD – PE                                       */
taxable_income(GI, SD, PE, TI) :- TI is GI - SD - PE.

/* joint_tax/2 – §1(a) schedule                                               */
joint_tax(TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 36900  -> Tax is 0.15 * TI
    ; TI =< 89150  -> Tax is 5535  + 0.28 * (TI - 36900)
    ; TI =< 140000 -> Tax is 20165 + 0.31 * (TI - 89150)
    ; TI =< 250000 -> Tax is 35928.50 + 0.36 * (TI - 140000)
    ;                 Tax is 75528.50 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000)
    ).

/* tax/3 – final predicate requested                                          */
tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    file_joint_return(Person, Spouse, Year),
    gross_income(Person, Year, GI1),
    gross_income(Spouse, Year, GI2),
    GI is GI1 + GI2,
    basic_standard_deduction(Year, joint, SD),
    personal_exemptions_joint(Year, 2, PE),
    taxable_income(GI, SD, PE, TI),
    joint_tax(TI, Tax).

/* ----------  QUERY  ---------- */
:- tax(bob, 2006, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: Charlie is Bob's father since April 15th, 2014, and Bob has lived at Charlie's place since then. Alice is Charlie's sister since October 12th, 1992. Charlie's gross income in 2015 was $52650. In 2015, Alice's gross income was $2312. Alice takes the standard deduction in 2015.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2015?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ----------  Statutory rules (simplified for 2015)  ---------- */

/* basic facts from the case */
person(alice).
person(charlie).
person(bob).

gross_income(alice , 2015 ,  2312).
gross_income(charlie, 2015 , 52650).

/* family relationships */
sibling(charlie, alice).
sibling(alice , charlie).
father(charlie, bob).

/* marital status – everyone in the story is single for 2015 */
married(alice , 2015 , no).
married(charlie, 2015 , no).

/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */
/* --------  PART I – DEPENDENCY TESTS (very limited subset) -------- */

/* earned income = gross income for this very small model */
earned_income(P, Y, I) :- gross_income(P, Y, I).

/* Relationship test under §152(d)(2) (subset: only what we need) */
relationship(X, Y) :- sibling(X, Y).
relationship(X, Y) :- sibling(Y, X).
relationship(X, Y) :- father(X, Y).
relationship(X, Y) :- father(Y, X).

/* Qualifying-child rules are not needed here – nobody qualifies.   */
qualifying_child(_,_,_) :- fail.

/* Qualifying-relative – stripped to what matters here:
   – must bear a listed relationship
   – must have *no* income (§152(d)(1)(B))
*/
qualifying_relative(Rel, Taxpayer, Year) :-
    relationship(Rel, Taxpayer),
    gross_income(Rel, Year, GI),
    GI =:= 0.

/* Does ‘Person’ qualify as someone else’s dependent in Year? */
is_dependent(Person, Year) :- qualifying_child(Person, _, Year).
is_dependent(Person, Year) :- qualifying_relative(Person, _, Year).

/* flag indicating whether §63(c)(5) “dependent‐standard-deduction cap” applies */
dependent_of_another(Person, Year, yes) :- is_dependent(Person, Year).
dependent_of_another(Person, Year, no ) :- \+ is_dependent(Person, Year).

/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */
/* --------  PART II – DEDUCTIONS  ---------------------------------- */

/* Personal exemption (§151).  2015 predates the 2018-2025 suspension. */
personal_exemption_amount(_, _Year, yes, 0).          /* if dependent of another */
personal_exemption_amount(_, 2015 ,  no, 2000).

/* Filing status (extremely trimmed) */
head_of_household(_,_) :- fail.                       /* no one in story qualifies */
filing_status(P, Y, head_of_household) :- head_of_household(P, Y), !.
filing_status(P, Y, unmarried        ) :- married(P, Y, no).

/* Standard deduction – 2015 figures (§63(c)(2)) plus §63(c)(5) cap */
standard_deduction_amount(P, Y, no, 4400) :-
    filing_status(P, Y, head_of_household), Y < 2018.
standard_deduction_amount(P, Y, no, 3000) :-
    filing_status(P, Y, unmarried),          Y < 2018.

/* Dependent of another gets the special smaller standard deduction (§63(c)(5)) */
standard_deduction_amount(P, Y, yes, SD) :-
    earned_income(P, Y, EI),
    Temp  is 250 + EI,
    (Temp > 500 -> SD0 = Temp ; SD0 = 500),
    /* cap cannot exceed the basic amount for the person’s status */
    (filing_status(P, Y, head_of_household) -> Max=4400 ; Max=3000),
    (SD0 > Max -> SD = Max ; SD = SD0).

/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */
/* --------  PART III – TAXABLE INCOME & TAX CALCULATION ------------ */

/* Adjusted gross income = gross income (no adjustments modelled) */
adjusted_gross_income(P, Y, AGI) :- gross_income(P, Y, AGI).

/* Taxable income (cannot be negative) */
taxable_income(P, Y, TI) :-
    adjusted_gross_income(P, Y, AGI),
    dependent_of_another(P, Y, DepFlag),
    standard_deduction_amount(P, Y, DepFlag, SD),
    personal_exemption_amount(P, Y, DepFlag, PE),
    TI0 is AGI - SD - PE,
    (TI0 =< 0 -> TI = 0 ; TI = TI0).

/* §1 tax tables for 2015 – only unmarried rates needed here        */
tax_from_taxable_income(unmarried, TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 22100 ->
        Tax is TI * 0.15
    ; TI =< 53500 ->
        Tax is 3315 + (TI - 22100) * 0.28
    ; TI =< 115000 ->
        Tax is 12107 + (TI - 53500) * 0.31
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        Tax is 31172 + (TI - 115000) * 0.36
    ;                                /* TI  > 250 000 */
        Tax is 79772 + (TI - 250000) * 0.396
    ).

/* Main tax predicate */
tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    tax_from_taxable_income(Status, TI, Tax).

/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */
/* --------  QUERY --------------------------------------------------- */

:- tax(alice, 2015, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice paid Bob for agricultural labor from Feb 1st, 2019 to November 19th, 2019, paying him $27371 in 2019. On November 25th, Bob died from a heart attack. On January 20th, 2020, Alice paid Charlie, Bob's surviving spouse, Bob's outstanding wages of $24500. In 2020, Alice's gross income was $372109. Alice receives a deduction of $25000 for donating goods to a food bank.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2020?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
%  Statutory rules (simplified to what is needed for query)  %
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

% -------- Facts about Alice for tax year 2020 --------------
gross_income("Alice", 2020, 372109).          % §63
itemized_deductions("Alice", 2020, 25000).    % Charitable gift
standard_deduction("Alice", 2020, 12000).     % §63(c)(7) – unmarried
filing_status("Alice", 2020, unmarried).      % not married/head/spouse
futa_wages("Alice", 2020, 0).                 % §3306(b)(15) exclusion

% ------------- Helper deductions ---------------------------
deduction(Person, Year, Deduction) :-
    itemized_deductions(Person, Year, Itemized),
    standard_deduction(Person, Year, Standard),
    (Itemized > Standard -> Deduction = Itemized ; Deduction = Standard).

taxable_income(Person, Year, TI) :-
    gross_income(Person, Year, GI),
    deduction(Person, Year, D),
    TI is GI - D.

% -------- Income-tax brackets for 2020 (§1(c)) -------------
% bracket(Status, LowerInclusive, UpperExclusive, BaseTax, MarginalRate).
bracket(unmarried,      0,        22100,      0,      0.15).
bracket(unmarried,      22100,    53500,      3315,   0.28).
bracket(unmarried,      53500,    115000,     12107,  0.31).
bracket(unmarried,      115000,   250000,     31172,  0.36).
bracket(unmarried,      250000,   1.0e99,     79772,  0.396).

income_tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
    bracket(Status, Lower, Upper, Base, Rate),
    TI > Lower, TI =< Upper,
    Tax is Base + Rate * (TI - Lower).

% --------------- FUTA tax (§3301) --------------------------
futa_tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    futa_wages(Person, Year, Wages),
    Tax is Wages * 0.06.

% ---------------- Total tax owed ---------------------------
tax(Person, Year, TotalTax) :-
    income_tax(Person, Year, IncomeTax),
    futa_tax(Person, Year, FutaTax),
    TotalTax is IncomeTax + FutaTax.

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
%                      Query required                        %
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
:- tax("Alice", 2020, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice's father, Bob, was paid $53249 in remuneration in 2017 for services performed for Johns Hopkins University. Alice was enrolled at Johns Hopkins University and attending classes from August 29, 2015 to May 30th, 2019. While attending classes at Johns Hopkins University, Alice lived at Bob's house, for which Bob furnished all costs. In 2017, Bob takes the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Bob have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* =========================================
   Statutory rules (only those relevant here)
   ========================================= */

/* exemption amount (§151(d)(1)) */
exemption_amount(2000).

/* standard deduction amounts for 2017 (§63(c)) */
standard_deduction_amount(joint_or_surviving_spouse,  6000).   /* 200% × $3,000 */
standard_deduction_amount(head_of_household,          4400).   /* §63(c)(2)(B)   */
standard_deduction_amount(single,                     3000).   /* §63(c)(2)(C)   */
standard_deduction_amount(married_filing_separate,    3000).

/* 2017 head-of-household tax schedule (§1(b)) */
head_of_household_tax(TI, Tax) :-
        TI =< 29600,               !,
        Tax is TI * 0.15.
head_of_household_tax(TI, Tax) :-
        TI =< 76400,               !,
        Tax is 4440 + (TI - 29600) * 0.28.
head_of_household_tax(TI, Tax) :-
        TI =< 127500,              !,
        Tax is 17544 + (TI - 76400) * 0.31.
head_of_household_tax(TI, Tax) :-
        TI =< 250000,              !,
        Tax is 33385 + (TI - 127500) * 0.36.
head_of_household_tax(TI, Tax) :-
        Tax is 77485 + (TI - 250000) * 0.396.

/* =================================================
   Factual predicates describing the Bob/Alice case
   ================================================= */

person(bob).
person(alice).

/* Bob’s 2017 wage income */
wages(bob, 2017, 53249).

/* Filing information */
unmarried(bob, 2017).                         /* no spouse mentioned */
child_of(alice, bob).
age(alice, 2017, 21).                         /* younger than 25     */
same_abode(alice, bob, 2017).                 /* lives at Bob’s home */
joint_return(alice, 2017, no).                /* did not file joint  */

/* Alice is a qualifying child (§152(c)) */
qualifying_child(Child, Taxpayer, Year) :-
        child_of(Child, Taxpayer),
        age(Child, Year, Age), Age < 25,      /* §152(c)(3) age test */
        same_abode(Child, Taxpayer, Year),    /* §152(c)(1)(B)       */
        joint_return(Child, Year, no).        /* §152(c)(1)(E)       */

/* Bob’s list of dependents for 2017 */
dependent(Taxpayer, Year, Dep) :-
        qualifying_child(Dep, Taxpayer, Year).

/* collect dependents into a list */
dependent_list(Person, Year, List) :-
        findall(D, dependent(Person, Year, D), List).

/* Number and dollar value of personal exemptions (§151) */
personal_exemptions(Person, Year, Num, TotalDollar) :-
        dependent_list(Person, Year, DepList),
        length(DepList, NDep),
        Num is NDep + 1,                      /* +1 for the taxpayer */
        exemption_amount(EA),
        TotalDollar is Num * EA.

/* Determine Bob’s filing status (§2(b)) */
filing_status(Person, Year, head_of_household) :-
        unmarried(Person, Year),
        qualifying_child(_, Person, Year).

/* Standard deduction applicable */
standard_deduction(Person, Year, SD) :-
        filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
        standard_deduction_amount(Status, SD).

/* Adjusted-gross income = wage income (Bob has no others) */
agi(Person, Year, AGI) :- wages(Person, Year, AGI).

/* Taxable income (§63(b)) = AGI − standard deduction − personal exemptions */
taxable_income(Person, Year, TI) :-
        agi(Person, Year, AGI),
        standard_deduction(Person, Year, SD),
        personal_exemptions(Person, Year, _NumEx, ExAmt),
        TI0 is AGI - SD - ExAmt,
        ( TI0 > 0 -> TI = TI0 ; TI = 0 ).

/* Compute income tax using the appropriate rate schedule */
income_tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
        filing_status(Person, Year, head_of_household),
        taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
        head_of_household_tax(TI, Tax).

/* High-level predicate requested in the assignment */
tax(Person, Year, Tax) :- income_tax(Person, Year, Tax).


/* ===============================
   Query required by the assignment
   =============================== */
:- tax(bob, 2017, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: In 2017, Alice's gross income was $44215. Alice and Bob have been married since Oct 10th, 2017. Alice and Bob file separately. Alice has paid $3200 in cash to Bob for agricultural labor done from Feb 1st, 2017 to Sep 2nd, 2017. Alice takes the standard deduction. Alice and Bob live separately in 2017.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/****************************************************
 * Statutory rules (simplified to what is relevant) *
 ****************************************************/

% filing status for Alice in 2017
filing_status(alice, 2017, married_filing_separately).

% gross income
gross_income(alice, 2017, 44215).

/* ------------------ Standard deduction ------------- */
% §63(c)(2)(C): $3,000 for MFS (years before 2018)
standard_deduction(Year, married_filing_separately, 3000) :-
    Year < 2018.

/* ------------------ Personal exemption ------------- */
% §151(d)(1): $2,000 per exemption (years before 2018)
exemption_amount(Year, 2000) :-
    Year < 2018.

% Alice claims only her own exemption
personal_exemptions(alice, 2017, 1).

/* -------------- Taxable income calculation --------- */
taxable_income(Person, Year, TaxableIncome) :-
    gross_income(Person, Year, GI),
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    standard_deduction(Year, Status, SD),
    personal_exemptions(Person, Year, NumEx),
    exemption_amount(Year, EA),
    PersonalExemptionTotal is NumEx * EA,
    TaxableIncome is GI - SD - PersonalExemptionTotal.

/* ---------------- Tax rate table (§1(d)) ------------ */
% Married filing separately brackets
mfs_tax(TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 18450 ->
        Tax is 0.15 * TI
    ; TI =< 44575 ->
        Tax is 2767.50 + 0.28 * (TI - 18450)
    ; TI =< 70000 ->
        Tax is 10082.50 + 0.31 * (TI - 44575)
    ; TI =< 125000 ->
        Tax is 17964.25 + 0.36 * (TI - 70000)
    ; % TI over 125,000
        Tax is 37764.25 + 0.396 * (TI - 125000)
    ).

/* --------------------- Main predicate --------------- */
tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    filing_status(Person, Year, married_filing_separately),
    taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
    mfs_tax(TI, Tax).

/*********************   Query   **********************/
:- tax(alice, 2017, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

```prolog
/********************  STATUTORY RULES  ************************/

/* exemption amount (§151(d)) */
exemption_amount(Year, 0)    :- Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025.        /* 2018-2025  */
exemption_amount(Year, 2000) :- Year < 2018 ; Year > 2025.         /* otherwise */

/* spouse relation (for all years – simplified) */
spouse(P1,P2,_) :- married(P1,P2).
spouse(P1,P2,_) :- married(P2,P1).

/* basic exemption for taxpayer himself (§151(b)) */
base_exemption(1).

/* additional exemption for spouse on separate return (§151(b)) */
spouse_exemption(Taxpayer,Year,1) :-
        filing_separate(Taxpayer,Year),
        spouse(Taxpayer,Spouse,Year),
        income(Spouse,Year,GI),
        GI =:= 0.                     /* spouse has no gross income */
spouse_exemption(_,_,0).

/* number of exemptions */
exemption_count(Taxpayer,Year,Count) :-
        base_exemption(Base),
        spouse_exemption(Taxpayer,Year,SpouseEx),
        Count is Base + SpouseEx.

/* standard deduction (§63(c)(2)(C)) – married filing separately */
standard_deduction(Taxpayer,Year,3000) :-
        filing_separate(Taxpayer,Year).

/* adjusted gross income (AGI) – no above-the-line deductions here */
agi(Person,Year,AGI) :- income(Person,Year,AGI).

/* taxable income (§63(b)) */
taxable_income(Person,Year,TI) :-
        agi(Person,Year,AGI),
        standard_deduction(Person,Year,SD),
        exemption_count(Person,Year,ExCount),
        exemption_amount(Year,ExAmt),
        TI0 is AGI - SD - ExCount*ExAmt,
        TI  is max(0,TI0).

/* filing status (specified in the facts) */
filing_status(P,Year,married_separate) :- filing_separate(P,Year).

/* 2017 married-filing-separately rate table (§1(d)) */
tax_by_status(married_separate,TI,Tax) :-
        (  TI =< 18450 ->
              Tax is 0.15*TI
        ;  TI =< 44575 ->
              Tax is 2767.50 + 0.28*(TI-18450)
        ;  TI =< 70000 ->
              Tax is 10082.50 + 0.31*(TI-44575)
        ;  TI =< 125000 ->
              Tax is 17964.25 + 0.36*(TI-70000)
        ;  Tax is 37764.25 + 0.396*(TI-125000)
        ).

/* master tax predicate */
tax(Person,Year,Tax) :-
        taxable_income(Person,Year,TI),
        filing_status(Person,Year,Status),
        tax_by_status(Status,TI,Tax).

/********************  CASE FACTS  ************************/

income("Alice",2017,33200).
income("Bob",  2017,0).

married("Alice","Bob").        /* married since 2-3-2017 (date not needed) */

filing_separate("Alice",2017).
filing_separate("Bob",  2017).

/********************  QUERY  ************************/
:- tax("Alice", 2017, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n',[Tax]).
:- halt.

From 2004 to 2019, Bob furnished the costs of maintaining the home where he and Charlie lived during that time. In 2015, Bob's gross income was $40059. Bob takes the standard deduction in 2015.
question: How much tax does Bob have to pay in 2015?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/**********************************************************************
*  Statutory rules (greatly simplified – only what is needed here)   *
**********************************************************************/

% ---------- Filing-status rules -------------------------------------

% spouse_died(Taxpayer, Spouse, YearOfDeath).
spouse_died(bob, alice, 2014).

% married_at_end_of_year(Taxpayer, Year) is true if Taxpayer is married
% on the last day of Year.  Bob never remarries, so it is always false.
married_at_end_of_year(bob, _Year) :- fail.

% maintains_household_for_dependent(Taxpayer, Year).
maintains_household_for_dependent(bob, 2015).

% could_have_filed_joint_return(Taxpayer, YearOfDeath).
%  (We assume Alice and Bob could have filed jointly for 2014.)
could_have_filed_joint_return(bob, 2014).

% Definition of “surviving spouse” – §2(a)
surviving_spouse(T, Year) :-
    spouse_died(T, _S, DeathYear),
    DeathYear < Year,
    Year - DeathYear =< 2,                       % died in one of 2 years before Year
    maintains_household_for_dependent(T, Year),  % principal home for dependent
    \+ married_at_end_of_year(T, Year),          % has not remarried
    could_have_filed_joint_return(T, DeathYear).

% ---------- Standard deduction – §63(c) -----------------------------

% basic_standard_deduction(Status, Amount).
basic_standard_deduction(joint_surviving, 6000).  % 200% × $3,000
basic_standard_deduction(head_of_household, 4400).
basic_standard_deduction(single, 3000).

% status(Taxpayer, Year, StatusAtom).
status(T, Y, joint_surviving) :- surviving_spouse(T, Y), !.
% (Other statuses not needed for this case)

standard_deduction(T, Y, SD) :-
    status(T, Y, Status),
    basic_standard_deduction(Status, SD).

% ---------- Personal-exemption amount – §151 ------------------------

% exemption_amount_per_person(2000) for 2015 (no TCJA yet)
exemption_amount_per_person(2000).

% dependent(Taxpayer, Dep, Year).
dependent(bob, charlie, 2015).

% personal_exemption_count(T, Y, Cnt).
personal_exemption_count(T, Y, Cnt) :-
    findall(Dep, dependent(T, Dep, Y), Deps),
    length(Deps, DepCnt),
    % taxpayer always counts for one
    Cnt is DepCnt + 1.

% phase-out – §151(d)(3) & §68(b) (not binding here – AGI below limits)
applicable_amount(joint_surviving, 300000).

exemption_phaseout(Status, AGI, ExemptCnt, FinalAmount) :-
    exemption_amount_per_person(Each),
    Total is Each * ExemptCnt,
    applicable_amount(Status, Limit),
    (  AGI =< Limit
    -> FinalAmount = Total
    ;  % Phase-out formula (not triggered here)
       Excess is AGI - Limit,
       Steps is ceil(Excess / 2500),
       Percent is min(100, Steps * 2),
       Reduction is Total * Percent / 100,
       FinalAmount is Total - Reduction
    ).

personal_exemption(T, Y, Amount) :-
    status(T, Y, Status),
    personal_exemption_count(T, Y, Cnt),
    adjusted_gross_income(T, Y, AGI),
    exemption_phaseout(Status, AGI, Cnt, Amount).

% ---------- Income figures -----------------------------------------

% gross_income(T, Y, Amount).
gross_income(bob, 2015, 40059).

% For this problem we assume no adjustments to income and that Bob
% takes the standard deduction (does NOT itemize).
adjusted_gross_income(T, Y, GI) :- gross_income(T, Y, GI).

taxable_income(T, Y, TI) :-
    adjusted_gross_income(T, Y, AGI),
    standard_deduction(T, Y, SD),
    personal_exemption(T, Y, PE),
    TI is AGI - SD - PE.

% ---------- Tax-rate schedules – §1 -------------------------------

% bracket(Status, LowerIncl, UpperIncl, BaseTax, MarginalRate).
%  *Lower bound is inclusive, *Upper* is inclusive for ease.
%  We only need the first two brackets for the joint/surviving schedule.
bracket(joint_surviving,   0,      36900, 0.0,    0.15).
bracket(joint_surviving,   36900,  89150, 5535.0, 0.28).
bracket(joint_surviving,   89150, 140000, 20165.0,0.31).
bracket(joint_surviving,  140000, 250000, 35928.5,0.36).
bracket(joint_surviving,  250000, 1.0e50, 75528.5,0.396).

% compute_tax(Status, TaxableIncome, TaxOwed).
compute_tax(Status, TI, Tax) :-
    bracket(Status, Lower, Upper, Base, Rate),
    TI =< Upper, TI > Lower,            % find applicable bracket
    Tax is Base + Rate * (TI - Lower).

% ---------- Master predicate ---------------------------------------

tax(T, Y, Tax) :-
    status(T, Y, Status),
    taxable_income(T, Y, TI),
    compute_tax(Status, TI, Tax).

/**********************************************************************
*  Case facts (already included above)                               *
**********************************************************************/

/* Already declared above in the rules section for clarity          */

/**********************************************************************
*  Query                                                             *
**********************************************************************/
:- tax(bob, 2015, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2015?
reference_prolog:
% ----------------------------
% Statutory reasoning (subset)
% ----------------------------

% ---- Basic financial facts interface ----
gross_income(Taxpayer, Year, Amount) :- fact_gross_income(Taxpayer, Year, Amount).
adjusted_gross_income(Taxpayer, Year, AGI) :- gross_income(Taxpayer, Year, AGI).

% ---- §152: Dependent = qualifying child or qualifying relative ----
dependent(Taxpayer, Dep, Year) :-
    qualifying_relative(Taxpayer, Dep, Year).
dependent(Taxpayer, Dep, Year) :-
    qualifying_child(Taxpayer, Dep, Year).

% (No qualifying child facts in this case; rule included for completeness.)
qualifying_child(_Taxpayer, _Child, _Year) :- fail.

% §152(d)(1): qualifying relative requirements used here:
% (A) relationship per §152(d)(2)
% (B) no income for calendar year
% (D) not a qualifying child of any taxpayer
qualifying_relative(Taxpayer, Dep, Year) :-
    related_for_qr(Dep, Taxpayer),
    income(Dep, Year, Inc), Inc =:= 0,
    not_qualifying_child_of_anyone(Dep, Year).

income(Person, Year, Amount) :- fact_income(Person, Year, Amount).

not_qualifying_child_of_anyone(Dep, Year) :-
    fact_not_qc_any_taxpayer(Dep, Year).

% §152(d)(2) Relationship (only what we need for this problem):
related_for_qr(Dep, Taxpayer) :-
    father(Dep, Taxpayer).
related_for_qr(Dep, Taxpayer) :-
    brother_of_parent(Dep, Taxpayer).

father(Father, Child) :- fact_father(Father, Child).

% §152(d)(2)(F): brother or sister of the father or mother of the taxpayer
brother_of_parent(Uncle, Taxpayer) :-
    father(Parent, Taxpayer),
    brother(Uncle, Parent).

brother(Bro, Sib) :- fact_brother(Bro, Sib).

% ---- §151: personal exemptions (2015 uses $2,000 exemption amount) ----
exemption_amount(Year, 2000) :- Year < 2018.

% Taxpayer exemption (always 1 for an individual taxpayer)
taxpayer_exemptions_count(_Taxpayer, 1).

dependent_exemptions_count(Taxpayer, Year, Count) :-
    findall(Dep, dependent(Taxpayer, Dep, Year), Deps),
    sort(Deps, UniqueDeps),
    length(UniqueDeps, Count).

total_personal_exemptions_amount(Taxpayer, Year, TotalAmt) :-
    exemption_amount(Year, ExAmt),
    taxpayer_exemptions_count(Taxpayer, TaxpayerCount),
    dependent_exemptions_count(Taxpayer, Year, DepCount),
    TotalCount is TaxpayerCount + DepCount,
    TotalAmt is TotalCount * ExAmt.

% ---- §63: standard deduction (2015 amounts: HOH 4400, other 3000, joint/surviving 6000) ----
filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, Status) :- fact_filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, Status).

standard_deduction(Taxpayer, Year, SD) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, joint), !,
    SD is 6000.
standard_deduction(Taxpayer, Year, SD) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, surviving_spouse), !,
    SD is 6000.
standard_deduction(Taxpayer, Year, SD) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, head_of_household), !,
    SD is 4400.
standard_deduction(_Taxpayer, _Year, 3000).

taxable_income(Taxpayer, Year, TI) :-
    adjusted_gross_income(Taxpayer, Year, AGI),
    standard_deduction(Taxpayer, Year, SD),
    total_personal_exemptions_amount(Taxpayer, Year, ExTotal),
    TI0 is AGI - SD - ExTotal,
    (TI0 =< 0 -> TI = 0 ; TI = TI0).

% ---- §1: tax rate schedules (only the ones potentially needed here) ----
tax(Taxpayer, Year, Tax) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, Status),
    taxable_income(Taxpayer, Year, TI),
    tax_by_status(Status, TI, Tax).

% §1(c) Unmarried individuals (not surviving spouse, not head of household)
tax_by_status(unmarried, TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 22100 ->
        Tax is 0.15 * TI
    ; TI =< 53500 ->
        Tax is 3315 + 0.28 * (TI - 22100)
    ; TI =< 115000 ->
        Tax is 12107 + 0.31 * (TI - 53500)
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        Tax is 31172 + 0.36 * (TI - 115000)
    ;   Tax is 79772 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000)
    ).

% (Other statuses omitted; not needed for this case)
tax_by_status(head_of_household, _TI, _Tax) :- fail.
tax_by_status(joint, _TI, _Tax) :- fail.
tax_by_status(surviving_spouse, _TI, _Tax) :- fail.
tax_by_status(married_separate, _TI, _Tax) :- fail.

% ----------------------------
% Case facts
% ----------------------------

% Relationships
fact_father("Charlie", "Alice").
fact_brother("Bob", "Charlie").

% Income facts (2015)
fact_gross_income("Alice", 2015, 87319).
fact_income("Charlie", 2015, 0).
fact_income("Bob", 2015, 0).

% Not qualifying children to any taxpayer (given)
fact_not_qc_any_taxpayer("Charlie", 2015).
fact_not_qc_any_taxpayer("Bob", 2015).

% Filing status (not specified in facts; assume "unmarried" under §1(c))
fact_filing_status("Alice", 2015, unmarried).

% ----------------------------
% Query
% ----------------------------
:- tax("Alice", 2015, Tax),
   format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

text: Alice was paid $73200 in 2017 as an employee of the State of Maryland in Baltimore, Maryland, USA. Alice takes the standard deduction in 2017.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ---------- RULES (derived from the quoted statutes) ---------------- */

% filing_status(Person, Year, Status).
% Here the only statuses we need are: single, head_of_household, surviving_spouse, married_filing_jointly, married_filing_separately.
% For Alice we will know she is single (unmarried, no dependents).

% 1.  Standard deduction for 2017 (§63(c)(2) – 2017 values)
standard_deduction(2017, single,                 3000).
standard_deduction(2017, head_of_household,      4400).
standard_deduction(2017, surviving_spouse,       6000).   % 200 % of 3000
standard_deduction(2017, married_filing_jointly, 6000).   % 200 % of 3000
standard_deduction(2017, married_filing_separately, 3000).

% 2. Personal-exemption amount for 2017 (§151(d)(1))
personal_exemption_amount(2017, 2000).

% 3. 2017 phase-out thresholds for the personal exemption (none applies to Alice, but we encode anyway, §151(d)(3) + §68(b))
phaseout_start(2017, single,                 250000).
phaseout_start(2017, head_of_household,      275000).
phaseout_start(2017, surviving_spouse,       300000).
phaseout_start(2017, married_filing_jointly, 300000).
phaseout_start(2017, married_filing_separately, 150000).

% percentage reduction: 2 % for each $2 500 (or part) over the threshold.
personal_exemption(Year, Status, AGI, Exemption) :-
    personal_exemption_amount(Year, Amount),
    phaseout_start(Year, Status, Threshold),
    ( AGI =< Threshold ->
        Exemption = Amount
    ; Excess is AGI - Threshold,
      Steps is ceiling(Excess / 2500),
      Percent is Steps * 2,
      Percent >= 100 -> Exemption = 0
    ; Reduction is Amount * Percent / 100,
      Exemption is Amount - Reduction
    ).

% For Alice the above simplifies to the constant $2 000, but the general rule is kept.

% 4. Taxable income (TI) for a taxpayer who uses the standard deduction (§63(b))
taxable_income(Person, Year, TI) :-
    wages(Person, Year, Wages),
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    standard_deduction(Year, Status, SD),
    personal_exemption_amount(Year, PE),   % no phase-out needed for Alice
    TI is max(0, Wages - SD - PE).

% 5. 2017 §1(c) tax table for unmarried individuals (single)
income_tax_single(Income, Tax) :-
    ( Income =< 22100 ->
        Tax is Income * 0.15
    ; Income =< 53500 ->
        Tax is 3315 + (Income - 22100) * 0.28
    ; Income =< 115000 ->
        Tax is 12107 + (Income - 53500) * 0.31
    ; Income =< 250000 ->
        Tax is 31172 + (Income - 115000) * 0.36
    ;                                     % Income > 250 000
        Tax is 79772 + (Income - 250000) * 0.396
    ).

% 6. Top-level predicate: tax(Person, Year, Tax)
tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    filing_status(Person, Year, single),     % Only single implemented for this case
    taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
    income_tax_single(TI, Tax).

/* ---------- FACTS (Alice’s situation) -------------------------------- */

% Wages (§3306(b)/(c) definitions not needed for income tax – simple fact)
wages('Alice', 2017, 73200).

% Filing status: Alice is unmarried, with no dependents – single (§1(c))
filing_status('Alice', 2017, single).

/* ---------- QUERY ---------------------------------------------------- */

:- tax('Alice', 2017, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice and Bob got married on Feb 3rd, 1992. Alice and Bob have a child, Charlie, born October 9th, 2000. Alice died on July 9th, 2014. From 2004 to 2019, Bob furnished 40% of the costs of maintaining the home where he and Charlie lived during that time. In 2013, Alice and Bob filed jointly, and took the standard deduction. In 2013, Alice earned $65400 and Bob earned $56400.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2013?
reference_prolog:
% Facts from the case
person(alice).
person(bob).
person(charlie).

spouse(alice,bob).
spouse(bob,alice).

joint_return(alice,bob,2013).

income(alice,2013,65400).
income(bob,2013,56400).

birth_year(charlie,2000).
parent(alice,charlie).
parent(bob,charlie).

lived_with(alice,charlie,2013).
lived_with(bob,charlie,2013).

younger(charlie,alice).
younger(charlie,bob).

% Rules for qualifying child (Section 152)
relationship(T,C) :- parent(T,C).

same_abode(T,C,Y) :- lived_with(T,C,Y).

age(C,Y,Age) :- birth_year(C,BY), Age is Y - BY.

age_requirement(T,C,Y) :- age(C,Y,Age), Age < 25, younger(C,T).

qualifying_child(T,C,Y) :-
    relationship(T,C),
    same_abode(T,C,Y),
    age_requirement(T,C,Y),
    \+ joint_return(C,_,Y).

dependent(T,C,Y) :- qualifying_child(T,C,Y).

% Exemption amount (Section 151)
exemption_amount(Year,Amount) :-
    (Year >= 2018, Year < 2026 -> Amount = 0 ; Amount = 2000).

% Count dependents for joint return
count_dependents_joint(P1,P2,Y,Count) :-
    findall(D,(dependent(P1,D,Y); dependent(P2,D,Y)),List),
    sort(List,Unique),
    length(Unique,Count).

exemptions_joint(P1,P2,Y,ExAmount) :-
    exemption_amount(Y,ExAmt),
    count_dependents_joint(P1,P2,Y,Count),
    ExAmount is ExAmt * (2 + Count).

% Standard deduction (Section 63)
basic_standard_deduction(joint,Year,Amount) :-
    (Year >= 2018, Year < 2026 -> Amount is 2*12000 ; Amount is 2*3000).

additional_standard_deduction(_,_,0).

standard_deduction(joint,Year,SD) :-
    basic_standard_deduction(joint,Year,BSD),
    additional_standard_deduction(joint,Year,ASD),
    SD is BSD + ASD.

% AGI and taxable income
agi_joint(P1,P2,Y,AGI) :-
    income(P1,Y,I1),
    income(P2,Y,I2),
    AGI is I1 + I2.

taxable_income_joint(P1,P2,Y,TI) :-
    agi_joint(P1,P2,Y,AGI),
    standard_deduction(joint,Y,SD),
    exemptions_joint(P1,P2,Y,EX),
    Temp is AGI - SD - EX,
    (Temp < 0 -> TI = 0 ; TI = Temp).

% Tax rate for joint return (Section 1(a))
tax_rate_joint(TI,Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 36900 -> Tax is 0.15*TI
    ; TI =< 89150 -> Tax is 5535 + 0.28*(TI-36900)
    ; TI =< 140000 -> Tax is 20165 + 0.31*(TI-89150)
    ; TI =< 250000 -> Tax is 35928.5 + 0.36*(TI-140000)
    ; Tax is 75528.5 + 0.396*(TI-250000)
    ).

% Main tax computation
tax(Person,Y,Tax) :-
    joint_return(Person,Spouse,Y),
    taxable_income_joint(Person,Spouse,Y,TI),
    tax_rate_joint(TI,Tax).

tax(Person,Y,Tax) :-
    joint_return(Spouse,Person,Y),
    taxable_income_joint(Spouse,Person,Y,TI),
    tax_rate_joint(TI,Tax).

:- tax(alice, 2013, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

text: In 2017, Alice's gross income was $310192. Since 2014, Alice maintains a house where she and her daughter live. Alice was married since Jan 14th, 2010 until her husband died on Nov 23rd, 2016. In 2017, Alice is allowed itemized deductions of $17890. Alice has paid $45252 to Bob for work done in the year 2017. In 2017, Alice has also paid $9832 into a retirement fund for Bob, and $5322 into health insurance for Charlie, who is Alice's father and has retired in 2016. Charlie had no income in 2017.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/*  =========  STATUTORY RULES (selected provisions needed for the case)  ========= */

/* ----- basic filing-status thresholds for §§1, 2 & 68 ----- */
status_threshold(surviving_spouse, 300000).
status_threshold(head_of_household, 275000).
status_threshold(single, 250000).
status_threshold(married_separate, 150000).

/* §1 – tax-rate tables (only the table for surviving spouses is needed here)          */
/* bracket(LowerBound , UpperBound , BaseTax , MarginalRate ).  UpperBound ‘inf’ = no upper limit */
rate_table(surviving_spouse,
    [ bracket(     0   ,  36900 ,          0      , 0.15)
    , bracket( 36900   ,  89150 ,       5535      , 0.28)
    , bracket( 89150   , 140000 ,      20165      , 0.31)
    , bracket(140000   , 250000 ,   35928.50      , 0.36)
    , bracket(250000   ,    inf ,   75528.50      , 0.396)
    ]).

/* §63(c)(2) – basic standard deduction (only figures that might be relevant) */
basic_standard_deduction(surviving_spouse, 6000).   /* not used because Alice itemises */

/* §151 – personal-exemption base amount (pre-2018 rules) */
personal_exemption_base(2000).

/* §3301 – FUTA rate */
futa_rate(0.06).
futa_wage_base(7000).

/*  =========  FACTS FOR THE INSTANT CASE  ========= */

taxpayer(alice).

/* gross income (§63) */
gross_income(alice, 2017, 310192).

/* itemised deductions before §68 limitation */
itemised_deductions(alice, 2017, 17890).

/* dependants (§§151 & 152) */
dependent(alice, daughter, qualifying_child).
dependent(alice, charlie , qualifying_relative).

/* filing status (§2) – we assert that the statutory conditions are met */
filing_status(alice, 2017, surviving_spouse).

/* employee wages etc. for FUTA (§3306) */
wages_paid(alice, 2017, bob,            45252).   % cash wages
wages_paid(alice, 2017, bob_retirement,  9832).   % paid into fund – still “wages”

/*  =========  DERIVED QUANTITIES  ========= */

/* adjusted-gross income – no above-the-line deductions listed */
agi(T, Y, AGI) :- gross_income(T, Y, AGI).

/* §68 – limitation on itemised deductions */
itemised_after_pease(T, Y, Allowed) :-
    itemised_deductions(T, Y, ID0),
    filing_status(T, Y, Status),
    status_threshold(Status, Threshold),
    agi(T, Y, AGI),
    Excess is max(0, AGI - Threshold),
    Lim1  is 0.03 * Excess,
    Lim2  is 0.80 * ID0,
    Lim   is min(Lim1, Lim2),
    Allowed is ID0 - Lim.

/* §151 – phase-out of personal exemptions */
effective_personal_exemption(T, Y, EffAmount) :-
    personal_exemption_base(Base),
    filing_status(T, Y, Status),
    status_threshold(Status, Threshold),
    agi(T, Y, AGI),
    Over is max(0, AGI - Threshold),
    Steps is ceiling(Over / 2500),
    ApplicablePerc is min(100, Steps * 2),             % 2 % per step, cap 100 %
    EffAmount is Base * (1 - ApplicablePerc / 100).

/* total personal-exemption deduction */
personal_exemption_deduction(T, Y, Deduction) :-
    findall(D, dependent(T, D, _), Dependents),
    length(Dependents, DepCnt),
    EffBase is DepCnt + 1,      % +1 for the taxpayer herself
    effective_personal_exemption(T, Y, Each),
    Deduction is Each * EffBase.

/* taxable income (§63) */
taxable_income(T, Y, Taxable) :-
    gross_income(T, Y, GI),
    itemised_after_pease(T, Y, IDs),
    personal_exemption_deduction(T, Y, PED),
    Taxable is GI - IDs - PED.

/* §1 – regular income-tax liability (uses correct rate table) */
income_tax(T, Y, Tax) :-
    filing_status(T, Y, Status),
    taxable_income(T, Y, TI),
    rate_table(Status, Brackets),
    income_tax_from_brackets(TI, Brackets, Tax).

income_tax_from_brackets(TI, [bracket(L,U,B,R)|_], Tax) :-
    (   (U == inf, TI >= L)
    ;   TI >= L, TI =< U),
    Tax is B + R*(TI - L), !.
income_tax_from_brackets(TI, [_|Rest], Tax) :-
    income_tax_from_brackets(TI, Rest, Tax).

/* FUTA tax (§3301) */
futa_tax(T, Y, FUTATax) :-
    findall(W, wages_paid(T, Y, _, W), WageList),
    sum_list(WageList, TotalWages),                    % not actually needed per-employee here
    futa_wage_base(Base),
    futa_rate(Rate),
    TaxableWages is min(Base, TotalWages),
    FUTATax is Rate * TaxableWages.

/* total tax = income tax + FUTA */
tax(T, Y, TotalTax) :-
    income_tax(T, Y, IncomeTax),
    futa_tax(T, Y, FUTATax),
    TotalTax is round( (IncomeTax + FUTATax) * 100 ) / 100.   % round to cents

/*  =========  QUERY  ========= */
:- tax(alice, 2017, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice and Bob got married on Feb 3rd, 1992. Alice and Bob have a child, Charlie, born October 9th, 2000. From 2004 to 2019, Bob furnished the costs of maintaining the home where Alice, Bob and Charlie lived during that time. Charlie married Dan on Feb 14th, 2018 and they file separate tax returns in 2018. Dan's principal place of abode for 2018 is different from Charlie's. Alice's gross income in 2018 was $54232, and Bob's gross income was $43245. Alice and Bob file a joint return in 2018 and take the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2018?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ----------  Statutory rules (only those needed here) ---------- */

/* spouses */
married(alice,bob,1992).

spouse(X,Y) :- married(X,Y,_).
spouse(X,Y) :- married(Y,X,_).

/* filing status (joint return for 2018) */
file_status(alice,2018,joint).
file_status(bob,2018,joint).

filing_status(Person,Year,joint) :- file_status(Person,Year,joint).

/* gross income facts */
income(alice,2018,54232).
income(bob,2018,43245).

/* combined adjusted gross income for a joint return */
adjusted_gross_income(Person,Year,AGI) :-
    filing_status(Person,Year,joint),
    spouse(Person,Spouse),
    income(Person,Year,I1),
    income(Spouse,Year,I2),
    AGI is I1 + I2.

/* standard deduction (basic, no extra aged/blind amounts)          */
/*  For 2018-2025: joint = 2 * 12,000  = 24,000 (§63(c)(2)(A) & (7)) */
standard_deduction(joint,2018,24000).

/* personal exemptions are zero for 2018-2025 (§151(d)(5)) */
exemption_amount(2018,0).

personal_exemption(_Person,Year,0) :- exemption_amount(Year,0).

/* taxable income = AGI – standard deduction – personal exemptions  */
taxable_income(Person,Year,TI) :-
    adjusted_gross_income(Person,Year,AGI),
    filing_status(Person,Year,Status),
    standard_deduction(Status,Year,SD),
    personal_exemption(Person,Year,PE),
    TI is AGI - SD - PE.

/* §1(a) tax table for married filing jointly / surviving spouse    */
tax_bracket(joint,  0,   36900, 0.15,     0        ).
tax_bracket(joint, 36900, 89150, 0.28,  5535       ).
tax_bracket(joint, 89150,140000, 0.31, 20165       ).
tax_bracket(joint,140000,250000, 0.36, 35928.50    ).
tax_bracket(joint,250000,1.0e99,0.396, 75528.50    ).

tax_amount(Status,TI,Tax) :-
    tax_bracket(Status,Lower,Upper,Rate,Base),
    TI > Lower, TI =< Upper,
    Tax is Base + Rate*(TI-Lower).

/* top-level predicate: tax owed by Person for Year                 */
tax(Person,Year,Tax) :-
    taxable_income(Person,Year,TI),
    filing_status(Person,Year,Status),
    tax_amount(Status,TI,Tax).

/* ---------------------------------------------------------------- */
/* Query required by problem statement                               */
/* ---------------------------------------------------------------- */
:- tax(alice, 2018, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

person(bob).

% -------- general rules drawn from the cited statutes ------------

% age of a person in a given year
age(Person, Year, Age) :-
    dob(Person, date(BirthYear,_,_)),
    Age is Year - BirthYear.

% qualifying child  (§152(c))
qualifying_child(Child, Taxpayer, Year) :-
    child_of(Child,Taxpayer),
    age(Child,Year,Age), Age < 25,
    lived_with(Child,Taxpayer,Year,full_year).

% surviving spouse  (§2(a))  – nobody here qualifies
surviving_spouse(_,_) :- fail.

% filing status (§2(b))
filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, head_of_household) :-
    married(Taxpayer,Year,false),

% personal exemptions (§151)

    adjusted_gross_income(P,Y,AGI),
    standard_deduction(P,Y,SD),
    personal_exemptions(P,Y,PE),
    Tmp is AGI - SD - PE,
    (Tmp > 0 -> TI = Tmp ; TI = 0).

% income-tax computation for head of household   (§1(b))
tax(Person,Year,Tax) :-
    filing_status(Person,Year,head_of_household),
    taxable_income(Person,Year,TI),
    ( TI =< 29600 ->
          Tax is TI*0.15

    ; TI =< 127500 ->

    ; TI =< 250000 ->

    ;   Tax is 77485 + 0.396*(TI-250000)
    ).

text: Alice and Bob got married on Feb 3rd, 1992. Alice died on July 9th, 2014. In 2014, Alice's gross income was $55431 and Bob's gross income was $64314. Bob files a joint return for himself and Alice for 2014. Bob takes the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Bob have to pay in 2014?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ----------  Statutory rules (only those needed for this case) ---------- */

% exemption_amount/2  (Year, Amount)
exemption_amount(Year, 2000) :- Year =< 2017.          % §151(d)(1)

% number_of_personal_exemptions/3  (Filer, Year, Number)
% joint return by the two spouses – 2 personal exemptions
number_of_personal_exemptions(bob, 2014, 2).

% standard_deduction/3 (FilingStatus, Year, Amount)
% §63(c)(2)(A) – joint return: 200 % × $3,000  (pre-2018 rule)
standard_deduction(married_filing_jointly, Year, 6000) :- Year =< 2017.

% income of each person
income(bob  , 2014, 64314).
income(alice, 2014, 55431).

% joint_adjusted_gross_income/2  (Year, AGI)
joint_adjusted_gross_income(Year, AGI) :-
    findall(Inc, income(_, Year, Inc), L),
    sumlist(L, AGI).

/* --- taxable_income/3  (Filer, Year, TaxableIncome) -------------------- */
taxable_income(Filer, Year, TI) :-
    joint_adjusted_gross_income(Year, AGI),
    standard_deduction(married_filing_jointly, Year, SD),
    number_of_personal_exemptions(Filer, Year, NumEx),
    exemption_amount(Year, ExAmt),
    PE is NumEx * ExAmt,
    TI is AGI - SD - PE.

/* --- Married–filing-jointly tax table (§1(a)) -------------------------- */
% tax_bracket(LowerExclusive, UpperInclusive, BaseTax, MarginalRate)
tax_bracket(    -1,   36900,      0.00, 0.15).
tax_bracket( 36900,   89150,   5535.00, 0.28).
tax_bracket( 89150,  140000,  20165.00, 0.31).
tax_bracket(140000,  250000, 35928.50, 0.36).
tax_bracket(250000, 1.0Inf, 75528.50, 0.396).

/* --- tax/3  (Filer, Year, Tax) ---------------------------------------- */
tax(Filer, Year, Tax) :-
    taxable_income(Filer, Year, TI),
    tax_bracket(L,U,Base,Rate),
    TI =< U, TI > L,                       % pick the right bracket
    Tax is Base + Rate * (TI - L).

/* ----------  auxiliary ------------------------------------------------- */
sumlist([],0).
sumlist([H|T],S) :- sumlist(T,S1), S is H + S1.

/* ----------  query ----------------------------------------------------- */
:- tax(bob, 2014, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice and Bob got married on November 23rd, 1994. Their son Charlie was born on July 5th, 2000. Bob died on March 15th, 2015. In 2017, Alice and Charlie lived in a house maintained by Alice. Alice's gross income for the year 2017 is $95129. Alice takes the standard deduction.

```prolog
/* ------------  Statutory rules (simplified to what is needed here)  ------------ */

/*  Relationship facts  */
spouse_of(alice, bob).      spouse_of(bob, alice).

/*  Marriage & death  */
married_on(alice, bob, date(1994,11,23)).
died_on(bob,  date(2015,3,15)).

/*  Household & dependents  */
maintains_household(alice, 2017).
qualifying_child(alice, charlie, 2017).   /* we are told Charlie satisfies §152(c) */

/*  Income  */
gross_income(alice, 2017, 95129).

/*  Alice did not remarry */
remarried(alice, _Year) :- fail.



/* ---- helper predicates ---- */

year_of(date(Y,_,_), Y).

/*  Years-between for surviving-spouse test (§2(a)(1)(A))  */
within_two_years(Year, DeathYear) :-
    Diff is Year - DeathYear,
    Diff >= 1, Diff =< 2.

/*  Is surviving spouse for a given tax year? (§2(a)) */
surviving_spouse(Person, Year) :-
    spouse_of(Person, Spouse),
    died_on(Spouse, DateOfDeath),
    year_of(DateOfDeath, DeathYear),
    within_two_years(Year, DeathYear),
    \+ remarried(Person, Year),
    maintains_household(Person, Year),
    qualifying_child(Person, _Child, Year).

/*  Standard deduction (§63(c)(2)(A)) – only amount we need */
standard_deduction(Person, Year, 6000) :-
    surviving_spouse(Person, Year).

/*  Personal exemptions (§151) */
personal_exemption_amount(2000).

personal_exemptions(Person, Year, Amount) :-
    personal_exemption_amount(E),
    % one for taxpayer …
    Base is E,
    % … plus one per dependent for whom deduction is allowed
    findall(Child, qualifying_child(Person, Child, Year), L),
    length(L, NumDeps),
    Amount is Base + NumDeps * E.

/*  Taxable income (§63(b)) */
taxable_income(Person, Year, TaxableIncome) :-
    gross_income(Person, Year, GI),
    standard_deduction(Person, Year, SD),
    personal_exemptions(Person, Year, PE),
    TaxableIncome is GI - SD - PE.

/*  Tax computation – §1(a) married-joint / surviving-spouse brackets */
tax_bracket_surviving_spouse(TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 36900 ->
        Tax is 0.15 * TI
    ; TI =< 89150 ->
        Tax is 5535 + 0.28 * (TI - 36900)
    ; TI =< 140000 ->
        Tax is 20165 + 0.31 * (TI - 89150)
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        Tax is 35928.5 + 0.36 * (TI - 140000)
    ;               % over 250,000
        Tax is 75528.5 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000)
    ).

/*  Main predicate asked for in the problem  */
tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    surviving_spouse(Person, Year),
    taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
    tax_bracket_surviving_spouse(TI, Tax).



/* ------------  Query ------------ */
:- tax(alice, 2017, Tax),
   format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: Bob is Charlie and Dorothy's son, born on April 15th, 2015. Alice married Charlie on August 8th, 2018. Alice's and Charlie's gross incomes in 2018 were $324311 and $414231 respectively. Alice, Bob and Charlie have the same principal place of abode in 2018. Alice and Charlie file jointly in 2018, and take the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2018?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ----------  Statutory rules we need  ---------- */

/*  Standard deduction for years 2018-2025, married filing jointly  */
standard_deduction(joint, Year, 24000) :-
    Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025.

/*  2018 rate schedule – §1(a) Married filing joint / surviving spouse  */
bracket_joint(36900,    0,        0.15,   0).
bracket_joint(89150,    5535,     0.28,   36900).
bracket_joint(140000,   20165,    0.31,   89150).
bracket_joint(250000,   35928.5,  0.36,   140000).
bracket_joint(1.0e15,   75528.5,  0.396,  250000).   /* top bracket, “infinite” ceiling */

/*  Compute tax for joint return once taxable income is known            */
joint_tax(TaxableIncome, Tax) :-
    bracket_joint(Upper, BaseTax, Rate, Lower),
    TaxableIncome =< Upper,
    Tax is BaseTax + Rate * (TaxableIncome - Lower),
    !.   /* cut – we stop at the first (correct) bracket */

/* ----------  Facts for the case  ---------- */

income("Alice",   2018, 324311).
income("Charlie", 2018, 414231).

married("Alice",  "Charlie", 2018).
married("Charlie","Alice",   2018).

/*  They filed a single joint return  */
filing_joint(2018, "Alice",   "Charlie").
filing_joint(2018, "Charlie", "Alice").

/* ----------  Derived rules  ---------- */

/*  Total gross income on the joint return                                */
total_income(Person, Year, Total) :-
    married(Person, Spouse, Year),
    filing_joint(Year, Person, Spouse),
    income(Person,  Year, Inc1),
    income(Spouse, Year, Inc2),
    Total is Inc1 + Inc2.

/*  Main predicate: tax(Person, Year, Tax)                                */
tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    total_income(Person, Year, Gross),
    standard_deduction(joint, Year, SD),
    Taxable is Gross - SD,
    joint_tax(Taxable, Tax).

/* ----------  Query requested by the problem  ---------- */
:- tax("Alice", 2018, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice employed Bob from Jan 2nd, 2011 to Oct 10, 2019, paying him $1513 in 2019. On Oct 10, 2019 Bob was diagnosed as disabled and retired. Alice paid Bob $298 because she had to terminate their contract due to Bob's disability. In 2019, Alice's gross income was $567192. In 2019, Alice lived together with Charlie, her father, in a house that she maintains. Charlie had no income in 2019. Alice takes the standard deduction in 2019.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2019?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ----------  FACTS  ---------- */

/* income and household facts */
gross_income(alice, 2019, 567_192).
maintains_household(alice, 2019).
dependent(alice, charlie, 2019).
relationship(charlie, alice, father).
income(charlie, 2019, 0).

/* employment facts (wages: Type = normal | termination )            */
/* only “normal” wages are FUTA-taxable, termination/disability aren’t*/
wages_paid(alice, bob, 2019, normal,      1_513).
wages_paid(alice, bob, 2019, termination,   298).

/* ----------  RULES  ---------- */

/* 1.  Filing-status: head-of-household ----------------------------- */
head_of_household(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    \+ married(Taxpayer, Year),                      % not married
    maintains_household(Taxpayer, Year),
    dependent(Taxpayer, Dep, Year),
    relationship(Dep, Taxpayer, father).             % father as dependent

married(_,_) :- fail.   /* no marriage facts → everyone single here*/

/* 2.  Standard deduction (§63(c)(2),(7)) --------------------------- */
standard_deduction(Year, head_of_household, 18_000) :-
    Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025.

/* 3.  Taxable income (§63) ---------------------------------------- */
taxable_income(Taxpayer, Year, TaxableIncome) :-
    gross_income(Taxpayer, Year, Gross),
    head_of_household(Taxpayer, Year),
    standard_deduction(Year, head_of_household, SD),
    TaxableIncome is Gross - SD.

/* 4.  Income-tax for heads of household (§1(b)) -------------------- */
income_tax_hoh(Income, Tax) :-
    ( Income =< 29_600 ->
          Tax is 0.15 * Income
    ; Income =< 76_400 ->
          Tax is 4_440 + 0.28 * (Income - 29_600)
    ; Income =< 127_500 ->
          Tax is 17_544 + 0.31 * (Income - 76_400)
    ; Income =< 250_000 ->
          Tax is 33_385 + 0.36 * (Income - 127_500)
    ; % Income over 250,000
          Tax is 77_485 + 0.396 * (Income - 250_000)
    ).

income_tax(Taxpayer, Year, Tax) :-
    taxable_income(Taxpayer, Year, TI),
    head_of_household(Taxpayer, Year),
    income_tax_hoh(TI, Tax).

/* 5.  FUTA (employer) tax (§3301, §3306) --------------------------- */
futa_wages(Employer, Year, Sum) :-
    findall(Amount,
            (wages_paid(Employer, _, Year, normal, Amount)),
            List),
    sum_list(List, Sum).

futa_tax(Employer, Year, Tax) :-
    futa_wages(Employer, Year, W),
    Tax is W * 0.06.

/* 6.  Total tax ---------------------------------------------------- */
tax(Taxpayer, Year, TotalTax) :-
    income_tax(Taxpayer, Year, IncTax),
    futa_tax(Taxpayer, Year, Futa),
    TotalTax is IncTax + Futa.

/* ----------  QUERY  ---------- */
:- tax(alice, 2019, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: In 2017, Alice was paid $33200. Bob is Alice's father since April 15th, 1978. In 2017, Alice and Bob lived in a house that Alice maintained. In 2017, Alice takes the standard deduction. Bob had no income in 2017.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ----------  FACTS FROM THE CASE  ---------- */
person("Alice").
person("Bob").

year(2017).

/* Gross income (assume all is wages) */
gross_income("Alice", 2017, 33200).

/* Relationship */
father("Bob","Alice").         /* Bob is Alice's father */

/* Household situation */
maintains_household("Alice", 2017).
principal_abode_of("Bob","Alice",2017).   /* Bob lives with Alice the whole year */
furnishes_half_cost("Alice",2017).

/* Marital status (simplified: Alice is unmarried) */
unmarried("Alice",2017).

/* Bob’s income */
gross_income("Bob",2017,0).

/* ----------  STATUTORY PARAMETERS  ---------- */

/* Personal-exemption amount (§151(d)(1)) */
personal_exemption_amount(2000).

/* Standard deduction basic amounts for 2017 (§63(c)(2)) */
basic_standard_deduction(joint_return_or_surviving_spouse,   6000).  /* not used here */
basic_standard_deduction(head_of_household,                  4400).
basic_standard_deduction(single,                             3000).

/* ----------  DEPENDENCY RULES (very reduced) ---------- */
qualifying_relative(Child, Taxpayer, Year) :-
    father(Child, Taxpayer) ;
    father(Taxpayer, Child) ;      /* include parent-child either way */
    false.                         /* (only need parent here) */

dependent_of(Dep, Taxpayer, Year) :-
    qualifying_relative(Dep, Taxpayer, Year),
    gross_income(Dep,Year,GI), GI =:= 0.        /* §152(d)(1)(B) zero income */

/* ----------  FILING-STATUS RULES ---------- */

/* Head-of-Household (§2(b)(1)(B)) */
filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, head_of_household) :-
    unmarried(Taxpayer,Year),
    maintains_household(Taxpayer,Year),
    furnishes_half_cost(Taxpayer,Year),
    dependent_of(Dep,Taxpayer,Year),
    principal_abode_of(Dep,Taxpayer,Year).

/* If not HoH and unmarried, treat as single */
filing_status(Taxpayer,Year,single) :-
    unmarried(Taxpayer,Year),
    \+ filing_status(Taxpayer,Year,head_of_household).

/* ----------  STANDARD DEDUCTION ---------- */
standard_deduction(Taxpayer,Year,SD) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer,Year,Status),
    basic_standard_deduction(Status,SD).

/* ----------  PERSONAL EXEMPTIONS ---------- */
number_of_personal_exemptions(Taxpayer,Year,Num) :-
    findall(Dep, dependent_of(Dep,Taxpayer,Year), DList),
    length(DList,Deps),
    Num is Deps + 1.         /* +1 for the taxpayer */

personal_exemptions_amount(Taxpayer,Year,Amount) :-
    number_of_personal_exemptions(Taxpayer,Year,Num),
    personal_exemption_amount(EA),
    Amount is Num * EA.

/* ----------  TAXABLE INCOME (§63(b)) ---------- */
taxable_income(Taxpayer,Year,TI) :-
    gross_income(Taxpayer,Year,GI),       /* treat AGI = gross for simplicity */
    standard_deduction(Taxpayer,Year,SD),
    personal_exemptions_amount(Taxpayer,Year,PE),
    TI0 is GI - SD - PE,
    ( TI0 < 0 -> TI = 0 ; TI = TI0 ).

/* ----------  §1(b) HEAD-OF-HOUSEHOLD RATE TABLE ---------- */
tax_bracket(head_of_household,       0,     29600, 0,       0.15).
tax_bracket(head_of_household,   29600,     76400, 4440,    0.28).
tax_bracket(head_of_household,   76400,    127500, 17544,   0.31).
tax_bracket(head_of_household,  127500,    250000, 33385,   0.36).
tax_bracket(head_of_household,  250000, 1.0e99,    77485,   0.396).

tax_bracket(single,                 0,     22100, 0,       0.15).
tax_bracket(single,             22100,     53500, 3315,    0.28).
tax_bracket(single,             53500,    115000, 12107,   0.31).
tax_bracket(single,            115000,    250000, 31172,   0.36).
tax_bracket(single,            250000, 1.0e99,    79772,   0.396).

/* ----------  TAX COMPUTATION ---------- */
tax(Taxpayer,Year,Tax) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer,Year,Status),
    taxable_income(Taxpayer,Year,TI),
    tax_bracket(Status,Lower,Upper,Base,Rate),
    TI =< Upper, TI > Lower,                         /* find matching bracket */
    Tax is Base + Rate * (TI - Lower).

/* ----------  QUERY ---------- */
:- tax("Alice", 2017, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice has paid wages of $53200 to Bob for domestic service done from Feb 1st, 2017 to Sep 2nd, 2017. Alice's gross income in 2017 was $921324. Alice takes the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ----------  Statutory rules (simplified for 2017)  ---------------- */

% basic standard deduction amounts valid for 2017
basic_standard_deduction(single,                3000).
basic_standard_deduction(head_of_household,     4400).
basic_standard_deduction(joint_or_surviving,    6000).

% exemption amount for 2017
exemption_amount(2017, 2000).

/* ----------  Facts from the case  ---------------------------------- */

gross_income(alice, 2017, 921324).
filing_status(alice, 2017, single).          % nothing given about spouse, HoH, etc.
exemptions(alice, 2017, 1).                  % personal exemption for herself
paid_domestic_wages(alice, bob, 2017, 53200).

/* ----------  Helper predicates  ------------------------------------ */

% phase-out percentage for personal exemptions – §151(d)(3)
phaseout_percentage(AGI, Percent) :-
    Excess is AGI - 250000,
    ( Excess =< 0 ->
        Percent = 0
    ;
        Steps      is ceiling(Excess / 2500),
        Percent0   is Steps * 2,
        Percent    is min(Percent0, 100)
    ).

% amount of personal exemptions actually allowed after phase-out
personal_exemptions_amount(Person, Year, Amount) :-
    gross_income(Person, Year, AGI),
    exemptions(Person, Year, Count),
    exemption_amount(Year, Base),
    phaseout_percentage(AGI, Percent),
    Amount is Base * Count * (100 - Percent) / 100.

% standard deduction – no aged/blind additions needed here
standard_deduction_amount(Person, Year, Amount) :-
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    basic_standard_deduction(Status, Amount).

% taxable income – §63(b)
taxable_income(Person, Year, TI) :-
    gross_income(Person, Year, GI),
    standard_deduction_amount(Person, Year, SD),
    personal_exemptions_amount(Person, Year, PE),
    TI is GI - SD - PE.

% income-tax computation for single filers – §1(c)
income_tax_single(TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 22100 ->
        Tax is TI * 0.15
    ; TI =< 53500 ->
        Tax is 3315 + (TI - 22100) * 0.28
    ; TI =< 115000 ->
        Tax is 12107 + (TI - 53500) * 0.31
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        Tax is 31172 + (TI - 115000) * 0.36
    ;                           % over $250,000
        Tax is 79772 + (TI - 250000) * 0.396
    ).

income_tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    filing_status(Person, Year, single),
    taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
    income_tax_single(TI, Tax).

% FUTA wages subject to the $7,000 cap – §3306(b)(1)
subject_wage(W, SW) :- SW is min(W, 7000).

% sum of FUTA-taxable wages for the year
total_futa_wages(Person, Year, Total) :-
    findall(SW, (paid_domestic_wages(Person, _, Year, W),
                 subject_wage(W, SW)), L),
    sumlist(L, Total).

% FUTA tax – §3301  (6 % of FUTA-taxable wages)
futa_tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    total_futa_wages(Person, Year, Wages),
    Tax is Wages * 0.06.

% Total tax Alice must pay for the year (income tax + FUTA)
tax(Person, Year, TotalTax) :-
    income_tax(Person, Year, IncomeTax),
    futa_tax(Person, Year, FutaTax),
    TotalTax is IncomeTax + FutaTax.

/* ----------  Utility  ---------------------------------------------- */

sumlist([], 0).
sumlist([H|T], S) :- sumlist(T, S0), S is S0 + H.

/* ----------  Query -------------------------------------------------- */

:- tax(alice, 2017, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice has been married since April 4th, 2015. Alice files a joint return with her spouse for 2016. Alice's and her spouse's gross income for the year 2016 is $164612. Alice has paid $4525 to Bob for work done in the year 2016. In 2016, Alice has also paid $9832 into a retirement fund for Bob, and $5322 into health insurance for Charlie, who is Alice's father and has retired in 2016. Charlie had no income in 2016. Alice and her spouse take the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2016?
reference_prolog:
% =========================
% Statutory rules (relevant)
% =========================

% ---- Filing status: joint return (for §1(a) rate table) ----
filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, joint) :-
    joint_return(Taxpayer, Year).

% ---- §63 standard deduction (pre-2018 amounts) ----
% §63(c)(2)(A): joint return basic standard deduction = 200% of $3,000 = $6,000
standard_deduction_amount_dollars(Taxpayer, Year, 6000) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, joint),
    Year < 2018.

% (Not using aged/blind additional standard deduction in this case.)

% ---- §152 qualifying relative (only the parts needed here) ----
dependent(Taxpayer, Dep, Year) :-
    qualifying_relative(Taxpayer, Dep, Year).

qualifying_relative(Taxpayer, Dep, Year) :-
    father_of(Dep, Taxpayer),
    no_income(Dep, Year),
    \+ qualifying_child_of_anyone(Dep, Year).

% (Case gives no facts making Charlie a qualifying child of anyone.)
qualifying_child_of_anyone(_Dep, _Year) :- fail.

% ---- §151 personal exemptions (modeled to match typical joint-return treatment) ----
exemption_amount_dollars(Year, 2000) :- Year < 2018.

% For this problem: joint return -> 2 taxpayer/spouse exemptions, plus dependents.
personal_exemptions_amount_dollars(Taxpayer, Year, Amount) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, joint),
    exemption_amount_dollars(Year, Ex),
    findall(D, dependent(Taxpayer, D, Year), Deps),
    length(Deps, NDeps),
    Amount is (2 + NDeps) * Ex.

% ---- §63(b) taxable income when standard deduction taken ----
taxable_income_dollars(Taxpayer, Year, Taxable) :-
    adjusted_gross_income_dollars(Taxpayer, Year, AGI),
    standard_deduction_amount_dollars(Taxpayer, Year, SD),
    personal_exemptions_amount_dollars(Taxpayer, Year, PE),
    Taxable0 is AGI - SD - PE,
    (Taxable0 >= 0 -> Taxable = Taxable0 ; Taxable = 0).

% ---- §1(a) income tax for joint returns / surviving spouse (2016 bracket amounts as given) ----
% Computation done in cents to avoid floating point issues.

income_tax_cents(Taxpayer, Year, TaxCents) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, joint),
    taxable_income_dollars(Taxpayer, Year, TI),
    income_tax_joint_from_table_cents(TI, TaxCents).

income_tax_joint_from_table_cents(TI, TaxCents) :-
    ( TI =< 36900 ->
        % 15%
        TaxCents is TI * 15 * 100 // 100
    ; TI =< 89150 ->
        % $5,535 + 28% of excess over $36,900
        Excess is TI - 36900,
        TaxCents is 553500 + (Excess * 28 * 100 // 100)
    ; TI =< 140000 ->
        % $20,165 + 31% of excess over $89,150
        Excess is TI - 89150,
        TaxCents is 2016500 + (Excess * 31 * 100 // 100)
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        % $35,928.50 + 36% of excess over $140,000
        Excess is TI - 140000,
        TaxCents is 3592850 + (Excess * 36 * 100 // 100)
    ;  % over 250,000
        Excess is TI - 250000,
        % $75,528.50 + 39.6% of excess over $250,000
        % 39.6% = 396/1000
        TaxCents is 7552850 + (Excess * 396 * 100 // 1000)
    ).

% ---- FUTA / unemployment excise tax: §3301 imposes 6% of §3306(b) wages paid w.r.t. §3306(c) employment ----
futa_tax_cents(Employer, Year, TaxCents) :-
    employer_for_year(Employer, Year),
    total_futa_wages_dollars(Employer, Year, Wages),
    % §3301 rate = 6%
    TaxCents is Wages * 6 * 100 // 100.

% Employer status for domestic service: §3306(a)(3) cash wages >= $1,000
employer_for_year(Employer, Year) :-
    cash_wages_paid_for_domestic_service_dollars(Employer, _Employee, Year, Cash),
    Cash >= 1000.

% FUTA wages include remuneration for employment, but cap at $7,000 per employee per year (§3306(b)(1)).
% Here, we only need the employee Bob, and only cash wages count because noncash for service not in course
% of employer's trade or business is excluded (§3306(b)(7)).
total_futa_wages_dollars(Employer, Year, Total) :-
    findall(W, futa_wages_for_employee_capped_dollars(Employer, Year, W), Ws),
    sum_list(Ws, Total).

futa_wages_for_employee_capped_dollars(Employer, Year, Capped) :-
    employee_of(Employer, Emp, Year),
    futa_wages_employee_dollars(Employer, Emp, Year, Wages),
    (Wages =< 7000 -> Capped = Wages ; Capped = 7000).

% Only cash domestic-service wages provided in the case (noncash retirement contribution excluded by §3306(b)(7))
futa_wages_employee_dollars(Employer, Emp, Year, Wages) :-
    cash_wages_paid_for_domestic_service_dollars(Employer, Emp, Year, Cash),
    Wages is Cash.

% ---- Total tax asked (income tax + FUTA tax) ----
total_tax_cents(Taxpayer, Year, TotalCents) :-
    income_tax_cents(Taxpayer, Year, IT),
    ( futa_tax_cents(Taxpayer, Year, FT) -> true ; FT = 0 ),
    TotalCents is IT + FT.

total_tax_dollars(Taxpayer, Year, TotalDollars) :-
    total_tax_cents(Taxpayer, Year, C),
    TotalDollars is C / 100.


% =========================
% Case facts
% =========================

% Alice files a joint return with her spouse for 2016.
joint_return(alice, 2016).

% Gross income / AGI (case gives gross income; we treat as AGI for this simplified model)
adjusted_gross_income_dollars(alice, 2016, 164612).

% Domestic worker Bob: Alice paid $4525 for work in 2016.
employee_of(alice, bob, 2016).
cash_wages_paid_for_domestic_service_dollars(alice, bob, 2016, 4525).

% Noncash benefits to Bob (retirement fund) - excluded from FUTA wages here by §3306(b)(7) modeling
paid_into_retirement_fund_dollars(alice, bob, 2016, 9832).

% Charlie is Alice's father, retired, no income in 2016 (dependent qualifying relative)
father_of(charlie, alice).
no_income(charlie, 2016).

% (Health insurance payment is not used to alter AGI/taxable income under the provided statutes.)
paid_health_insurance_dollars(alice, charlie, 2016, 5322).


% =========================
% Query
% =========================

:- total_tax_dollars(alice, 2016, Tax),
   format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
