Bob has no gross income in 2017. Alice and Bob take the standard deduction. Alice and Bob has the same principal place of abode from 2017 to 2020.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* Facts from the case */
gross_income(alice, 2017, 33200).
gross_income(bob,   2017,     0).

blind(alice, 2017).

married(alice, bob, 2017).

joint_return(2017).

/* Basic amounts taken straight from the statutes              */
/* §63(c)(2)(A): 200% × $3,000 = $6,000 for MFJ basic standard */
base_standard_deduction_joint(6000).

/* §63(f): $600 per blind (or age-65) spouse on a joint return */
additional_standard_deduction(Year, Add) :-
    findall(600, (blind(Person, Year), married(Person, _, Year)), L),
    sum_list(L, Add).

/* Full standard deduction for this joint return               */
standard_deduction(Year, SD) :-
    joint_return(Year),
    base_standard_deduction_joint(Base),
    additional_standard_deduction(Year, Add),
    SD is Base + Add.

/* §151(d)(1): personal exemption amount                        */
personal_exemption_amount(2000).

/* Two spouses ⇒ two personal exemptions on a joint return      */
personal_exemptions(Year, Exempt) :-
    joint_return(Year),
    personal_exemption_amount(A),
    Exempt is A * 2.

/* Combine spouses’ gross income                                */
combined_gross_income(Year, GI) :-
    findall(I, gross_income(_, Year, I), L),
    sum_list(L, GI).

/* §63(b): taxable income = AGI – std-deduction – exemptions     */
taxable_income(Year, TI) :-
    combined_gross_income(Year, GI),
    standard_deduction(Year, SD),
    personal_exemptions(Year, PE),
    TI0 is GI - SD - PE,
    ( TI0 > 0 -> TI = TI0 ; TI = 0 ).

/* §1(a) MFJ rate schedule – only first bracket needed here      */
tax_bracket_joint(TI, Tax) :-
    TI =< 36900,
    Tax is TI * 0.15.

/* Total tax for the joint return                               */
tax_due(Year, Tax) :-
    taxable_income(Year, TI),
    tax_bracket_joint(TI, Tax).

/* Alias so the query can use the exact name asked for          */
tax("Alice", Year, Tax) :-
    tax(alice, Year, Tax).

tax(alice, Year, Tax) :-
    tax_due(Year, Tax).

/* Helper: sum elements of a list                               */
sum_list([], 0).
sum_list([H|T], Sum) :- sum_list(T, R), Sum is H + R.

/* Query required by the problem statement                      */
:- tax("Alice", 2017, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: In 2017, Alice was paid $39212, and Bob had no income. Alice and Bob have been married since Feb 3rd, 2017. Alice and Bob file separately in 2017.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ----------  FACTS  ---------- */
income("Alice", 2017, 39212).
income("Bob",   2017,     0).

spouse("Alice", "Bob").
married("Alice", 2017).

filing_separately("Alice", 2017).

/* No one is the dependent of anyone else in this case */
dependent_of_another(_,_) :- fail.

/* ----------  RULES  ---------- */

/* filing status */
filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, married_filing_separate) :-
    married(Taxpayer, Year),
    filing_separately(Taxpayer, Year).

/* standard deduction (pre-2018 rules only) */
standard_deduction(married_filing_separate, _Year, 3000).

/* exemption amount (zero for 2018-2025, $2,000 otherwise) */
exemption_amount(Year, 0)    :- Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025, !.
exemption_amount(_Year, 2000).

/* does the taxpayer get an additional exemption for the spouse? */
qualify_spouse_exemption(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, married_filing_separate),
    spouse(Taxpayer, Spouse),
    income(Spouse, Year, Income), Income =:= 0,
    \+ dependent_of_another(Spouse, Year).

/* number of exemptions */
exemption_count(Taxpayer, Year, 2) :-
    qualify_spouse_exemption(Taxpayer, Year), !.
exemption_count(_Taxpayer, _Year, 1).

/* total personal exemption deduction */
personal_exemption(Taxpayer, Year, Amount) :-
    exemption_count(Taxpayer, Year, Count),
    exemption_amount(Year, EA),
    Amount is Count * EA.

/* ----------  TAX BRACKETS FOR 2017 – Married Filing Separately ---------- */
compute_tax(married_filing_separate, TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 18450 ->
        Tax is TI * 0.15
    ; TI =< 44575 ->
        Tax is 2767.50 + 0.28 * (TI - 18450)
    ; TI =< 70000 ->
        Tax is 10082.50 + 0.31 * (TI - 44575)
    ; TI =< 125000 ->
        Tax is 17964.25 + 0.36 * (TI - 70000)
    ;   Tax is 37764.25 + 0.396 * (TI - 125000)
    ).

/* ----------  MAIN TAX CALCULATION  ---------- */
tax(Taxpayer, Year, Tax) :-
    income(Taxpayer, Year, GI),
    filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, Status),
    standard_deduction(Status, Year, SD),
    personal_exemption(Taxpayer, Year, PE),
    TI0 is GI - SD - PE,
    ( TI0 < 0 -> TI = 0 ; TI = TI0 ),
    compute_tax(Status, TI, Tax).

/* ----------  QUERY  ---------- */
:- tax("Alice", 2017, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2019?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* =========  Statutory rules  ========= */

/*  §151(d)(5) – exemption amount is zero for 2018-2025  */
zero_exemption_year(Year) :- Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025.

/*  §63(c)(2) & (c)(7) – basic standard deduction amounts  */
basic_standard_deduction(unmarried, Year, 12000) :-
    Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025.

/*  §1 – income-tax rate schedule for unmarried individuals  */
income_tax_unmarried(TI,Tax) :-
    ( TI =<  22100 ->                     Tax is          TI * 0.15
    ; TI =<  53500 ->                     Tax is  3315 + (TI- 22100) * 0.28
    ; TI =< 115000 ->                     Tax is 12107 + (TI- 53500) * 0.31
    ; TI =< 250000 ->                     Tax is 31172 + (TI-115000) * 0.36
    ;                                      Tax is 79772 + (TI-250000) * 0.396 ).

/*  §3306(b)(1) – FUTA wage cap  */
futa_taxable_per_employee(Wages, Taxable) :-
    ( Wages > 7000 -> Taxable = 7000 ; Taxable = Wages ).

/* =========  Case facts  ========= */

marital_status("Alice", 2019, unmarried).

gross_income("Alice", 2019, 5723215).

%%  Payments to employee Bob
wages_paid("Alice","Bob",2019,3255).
retirement_bonus("Alice","Bob",2019,12980).   % treated as wages

/* =========  Derived facts & computations  ========= */

%%  Standard deduction (§63)
standard_deduction(Person,Year,SD) :-
    marital_status(Person,Year,Status),
    basic_standard_deduction(Status,Year,SD).

%%  Personal exemption (§151)
personal_exemption(_Person,Year,0) :-
    zero_exemption_year(Year).

%%  Taxable income (§63(b))
taxable_income(Person,Year,TI) :-
    gross_income(Person,Year,GI),
    standard_deduction(Person,Year,SD),
    personal_exemption(Person,Year,PE),
    TI is GI - SD - PE.

%%  Income tax (§1)
income_tax(Person,Year,Tax) :-
    marital_status(Person,Year,unmarried),
    taxable_income(Person,Year,TI),
    income_tax_unmarried(TI,Tax).

%%  Total FUTA-wages paid to each employee
futa_wages_to_employee(Person,Emp,Year,Wages) :-
    wages_paid(Person,Emp,Year,W1),
    retirement_bonus(Person,Emp,Year,W2),
    Wages is W1 + W2.

%%  FUTA taxable wages for all employees (cap $7,000 each)
futa_taxable_wages(Person,Year,Total) :-
    findall(Taxable,
        ( futa_wages_to_employee(Person,Emp,Year,W),
          futa_taxable_per_employee(W,Taxable) ),
        List),
    sum_list(List,Total).

%%  Employer excise tax (§3301 – 6% of FUTA wages)
employer_tax(Person,Year,Tax) :-
    futa_taxable_wages(Person,Year,Wages),
    Tax is Wages * 0.06.

%%  Total tax asked for in the question
tax(Person,Year,TotalTax) :-
    income_tax(Person,Year,IncomeTax),
    employer_tax(Person,Year,EmployerTax),
    TotalTax is IncomeTax + EmployerTax.

/* =========  Query & output  ========= */

:- tax("Alice", 2019, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

/* ----------  Statutory rules  ---------- */

/* FUTA tax (§3301) : 6 percent of “wages” (§3306(b))
   “Wages” are all remuneration up to $7,000 per employee (§3306(b)(1))           */

futa_tax_rate(0.06).
futa_wage_cap(7000).

/* Tax for one employer in one year:                                          */
futa_tax(Employer, Year, Tax) :-
    total_futa_wages(Employer, Year, Wages),
    futa_tax_rate(Rate),
    Tax is Wages * Rate.

/* ----------  Case facts  ---------- */

employees("Alice", 2015, 87).
wage_paid_per_employee("Alice", 2015, 5207).

/* ----------  Derived facts  ---------- */

/* total FUTA-subject wages for an employer in a year                         */
total_futa_wages(Employer, Year, Total) :-
    employees(Employer, Year, Count),
    wage_paid_per_employee(Employer, Year, WagePerEmployee),
    futa_wage_cap(Cap),
    ( WagePerEmployee =< Cap -> WageSubject is WagePerEmployee
    ; WageSubject is Cap ),
    Total is Count * WageSubject.

/* public predicate required by the autograder                               */
tax(Employer, Year, Tax) :- futa_tax(Employer, Year, Tax).

/* ----------  Query  ---------- */

personal_exemption_amount(Year, 0) :-    % suspension 2018-2025
    Year >= 2018,

personal_exemption_amount(_Year, 2000).  % $2,000 otherwise

    Year >= 2018,

    Year >= 2018,

standard_deduction_amount(_Year, head_of_household, 4400).

standard_deduction_amount(Year, joint_or_surviving, 24000) :- % 2018-2025

    !.
standard_deduction_amount(_Year, joint_or_surviving, 6000).

income_tax_single(TI, Tax) :-

% -------------

    personal_exemption_amount(Year, PE),
    TI0 is Gross - SD - PE,

    ; Status = head_of_household -> income_tax_head_of_household(TI, Tax)   % not needed here
    ; Status = joint_or_surviving -> income_tax_joint_surviving(TI, Tax)    % not needed here
    ).

text: Alice's gross income for the year 2017 is $22895. In 2017, Alice takes the standard deduction. Alice has a son, Bob, who has the same principal place of abode as her in 2017 and is not married.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
% Define facts from the case
gross_income(alice, 2017, 22895).
not_married(alice, 2017).
dependent(alice, bob, 2017).
lives_with(alice, bob, 2017).
not_married(bob, 2017).

% Define rules for marital status
married(_, _) :- fail.
surviving_spouse(_, _) :- fail.

% Define adjusted gross income (no adjustments)
adjusted_gross_income(Person, Year, AGI) :-
    gross_income(Person, Year, AGI).

% Define filing status: unmarried if not married, not head of household, not surviving spouse
filing_status(Person, Year, unmarried) :-
    not_married(Person, Year),
    \+ head_of_household(Person, Year),
    \+ surviving_spouse(Person, Year).

% Define head of household (not satisfied in this case)
head_of_household(Person, Year) :-
    not_married(Person, Year),
    \+ surviving_spouse(Person, Year),
    maintains_household(Person, Year),
    dependent(Person, Dependent, Year),
    lives_with(Person, Dependent, Year),
    \+ married(Dependent, Year).

% Since the case does not state that Alice pays over half the cost,
% we define maintains_household to fail.
maintains_household(_, _) :- fail.

% Define standard deduction amounts for 2017
standard_deduction(2017, unmarried, 3000).
standard_deduction(2017, head_of_household, 4400).

% Define base exemption amount for 2017
base_exemption_amount(2017, 2000).

% Define applicable amount for phaseout (§68(b))
applicable_amount(2017, unmarried, 250000).
applicable_amount(2017, head_of_household, 275000).

% Define exemption count: taxpayer + dependents
exemption_count(Person, Year, Count) :-
    ((not_married(Person, Year) ; married(Person, Year)) -> Count1 = 1 ; Count1 = 0),
    findall(Dep, dependent(Person, Dep, Year), Deps),
    length(Deps, DepCount),
    Count is Count1 + DepCount.

% Define exemption amount (including phaseout)
exemption_amount(Person, Year, ExemptionAmount) :-
    base_exemption_amount(Year, Base),
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    applicable_amount(Year, Status, ApplicableAmount),
    exemption_count(Person, Year, Count),
    adjusted_gross_income(Person, Year, AGI),
    (   AGI =< ApplicableAmount
    ->  ExemptionAmount is Base * Count
    ;   Excess is AGI - ApplicableAmount,
        Increments is ceil(Excess / 2500),
        ApplicablePercentage is min(100, 2 * Increments),
        TotalBase is Base * Count,
        ExemptionAmount is TotalBase * (1 - ApplicablePercentage / 100)
    ).

% Define taxable income
taxable_income(Person, Year, TaxableIncome) :-
    adjusted_gross_income(Person, Year, AGI),
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    standard_deduction(Year, Status, StdDed),
    exemption_amount(Person, Year, ExemptionAmount),
    TaxableIncome is AGI - StdDed - ExemptionAmount.

% Define tax brackets for unmarried individuals (§1(c))
tax_bracket(unmarried, 2017, 0, 22100, 0.15, 0).
tax_bracket(unmarried, 2017, 22100, 53500, 0.28, 3315).
tax_bracket(unmarried, 2017, 53500, 115000, 0.31, 12107).
tax_bracket(unmarried, 2017, 115000, 250000, 0.36, 31172).
tax_bracket(unmarried, 2017, 250000, 999999999, 0.396, 79772).

% Define compute tax
compute_tax(Status, Year, TI, Tax) :-
    tax_bracket(Status, Year, Lower, Upper, Rate, Base),
    TI > Lower,
    TI =< Upper,
    !,
    Tax is Base + Rate * (TI - Lower).

% Define tax predicate
tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    compute_tax(Status, Year, TI, Tax).

% Query to compute and print tax for Alice in 2017
:- tax(alice, 2017, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

text: Alice and Bob got married on Feb 3rd, 1992. Alice and Bob have a child, Charlie, born October 9th, 2000. From 2004 to 2019, Bob furnished the costs of maintaining the home where he and Charlie lived during that time. From 2015 to 2018, Alice had a different principal place of abode. In 2017, Alice was paid $33200, and Bob's income was $32311. Alice was born March 2nd, 1950 and Bob was born March 3rd, 1955. In 2017, Alice and Bob file jointly and take the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
% ---------- Statutory rules (simplified to what is needed here) ----------
% basic birth data
birth("Alice", 1950, 3, 2).
birth("Bob",   1955, 3, 3).
birth("Charlie",2000,10, 9).

% 2017 incomes
income("Alice",2017,33200).
income("Bob",  2017,32311).

% filing status (married filing jointly)
file_jointly(2017,true).

% Charlie is a dependent in 2017
dependent("Charlie",2017).

% ---------- Helper predicates ----------
age(Person, Year, Age) :-
    birth(Person, BornYear, _Month, _Day),
    Age is Year - BornYear.

aged(Person, Year) :-
    age(Person, Year, Age),
    Age >= 65.

% ---- Standard deduction for joint return (2017 values) ----
% Base = 2 * $3,000 = $6,000
% Additional $600 for each spouse age 65 or older
standard_deduction_joint(Year, SD) :-
    Base is 2*3000,
    (aged("Alice",Year) -> A1 = 600 ; A1 = 0),
    (aged("Bob",  Year) -> A2 = 600 ; A2 = 0),
    SD is Base + A1 + A2.

% ---- Personal exemptions (2017 values) ----
exemption_amount(2000).

personal_exemptions(Year, Num) :-
    % taxpayer + spouse + Charlie
    Num is 3.

% ---- Adjusted gross income ----
agi(Year, AGI) :-
    income("Alice",Year, IA),
    income("Bob",  Year, IB),
    AGI is IA + IB.

% ---- Taxable income ----
taxable_income(Year, TI) :-
    agi(Year, AGI),
    standard_deduction_joint(Year, SD),
    personal_exemptions(Year, NumEx),
    exemption_amount(EA),
    TI is AGI - SD - NumEx*EA.

% ---- 2017 joint-return tax brackets (§1(a)) ----
tax_bracket_joint(TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 36900 ->
        Tax is 0.15 * TI
    ; TI =< 89150 ->
        Tax is 5535 + 0.28 * (TI - 36900)
    ; TI =< 140000 ->
        Tax is 20165 + 0.31 * (TI - 89150)
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        Tax is 35928.50 + 0.36 * (TI - 140000)
    ;                   % over $250,000
        Tax is 75528.50 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000)
    ).

% ---- Main tax predicate for Alice ----
tax("Alice", Year, Tax) :-
    file_jointly(Year,true),          % they filed jointly
    taxable_income(Year, TI),
    tax_bracket_joint(TI, Tax).

% ------------------ Query ------------------
:- tax("Alice", 2017, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice was born January 10th, 1992. Bob was born January 31st, 2014. Alice adopted Bob on March 4th, 2018 and Bob has lived with Alice since, in a house that Alice maintains. Alice's gross income in 2018 was $141177. Alice takes the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2018?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
% Statutory rules (simplified to what is needed for this problem)
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
%  SECTION 152 – DEPENDENT %
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
% qualifying_child(Child, Taxpayer, Year)
qualifying_child(Child, Taxpayer, Year) :-
    child_of(Taxpayer, Child),                       % relationship (adopted child counts)
    principal_abode_same_household(Child, Taxpayer, Year, Days),
    Days > 182,                                      % more than one-half of the year
    age(Child, Year, AgeChild),
    age(Taxpayer, Year, AgeTP),
    AgeChild < 25,
    AgeChild < AgeTP,
    not_married(Child, Year).                        % child not married (true for Bob)

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
%  SECTION 2 – FILING STATUS   %
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
head_of_household(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    unmarried(Taxpayer, Year),
    qualifying_child(_, Taxpayer, Year),
    maintains_household(Taxpayer, Year).

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
%  SECTION 63 – STANDARD DEDUCTION 2018   %
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
standard_deduction(head_of_household, 2018, 18000).
standard_deduction(single,            2018, 12000).   % provided for completeness

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
%  SECTION 1 – TAX RATE SCHEDULES  %
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
% bracket(Status , Lower , Upper , Base , Rate )
% Upper == inf means no upper limit

bracket(head_of_household,     0,      29600,   0,      0.15).
bracket(head_of_household,  29600,     76400,   4440,   0.28).
bracket(head_of_household,  76400,    127500,  17544,   0.31).
bracket(head_of_household, 127500,    250000,  33385,   0.36).
bracket(head_of_household, 250000,       inf,  77485,   0.396).

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
%  Generic tax computation once status & taxable income  %
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
tax_due(Status, TaxableIncome, Tax) :-
    bracket(Status, Lower, Upper, Base, Rate),
    TaxableIncome >  Lower,
    (Upper == inf ; TaxableIncome =< Upper),
    Excess is TaxableIncome - Lower,
    Tax is Base + Rate * Excess.

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
%   Helper date-math rules    %
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
% day_number(Month, Day, OrdinalDay) for a non-leap year
day_number(M, D, N) :-
    month_cum_days(M, Cum), N is Cum + D.

month_cum_days(1,  0).
month_cum_days(2, 31).
month_cum_days(3, 59).
month_cum_days(4, 90).
month_cum_days(5,120).
month_cum_days(6,151).
month_cum_days(7,181).
month_cum_days(8,212).
month_cum_days(9,243).
month_cum_days(10,273).
month_cum_days(11,304).
month_cum_days(12,334).

% days_inclusive(Date1, Date2, Days)  (same calendar year, non-leap)
days_inclusive(date(Y,M1,D1), date(Y,M2,D2), Days) :-
    day_number(M1,D1,N1),
    day_number(M2,D2,N2),
    Days is N2 - N1 + 1.

% principal_abode_same_household(Child, Taxpayer, Year, Days)
principal_abode_same_household(Child, Taxpayer, Year, Days) :-
    lives_with(Child, Taxpayer, date(Year,SM,SD), date(Year,EM,ED)),
    days_inclusive(date(Year,SM,SD), date(Year,EM,ED), Days).

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
%   AGE CALCULATION %
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
age(Person, Year, Age) :-
    birth(Person, date(BY,_,_)),
    Age is Year - BY.     % good enough for determining < 25 here

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
%  Facts that are independent of the specific taxpayer %
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
%  Children normally are unmarried for our purposes
not_married(_, _).   % Bob is four years old – certainly unmarried

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
%  Alice–Bob specific facts
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
birth(alice, date(1992,1,10)).
birth(bob,   date(2014,1,31)).

% Alice adopted Bob – adopted children count as children
child_of(alice, bob).

% Bob has lived with Alice from 4 March 2018 to 31 Dec 2018
lives_with(bob, alice, date(2018,3,4), date(2018,12,31)).

% Household maintenance – Alice pays the bills
maintains_household(alice, 2018).

% Alice’s income
gross_income(alice, 2018, 141177).

% Alice is unmarried in 2018
unmarried(alice, 2018).

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
%   Computation of taxable income etc. %
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
taxable_income(Person, Year, TaxableIncome) :-
    gross_income(Person, Year, Gross),
    ( head_of_household(Person, Year) -> Status = head_of_household ; Status = single ),
    standard_deduction(Status, Year, SD),
    TaxableIncome is Gross - SD.

tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    ( head_of_household(Person, Year) -> Status = head_of_household ; Status = single ),
    taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
    tax_due(Status, TI, Tax).

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
%            Final query              %
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
:- tax(alice, 2018, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice got married on December 30th, 2017. Alice files a joint return with her spouse for 2017. Alice's and her spouse's gross income for the year 2017 is $684642. Alice has itemized deductions of $23029 for donating cash to a charity.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
% --------- BASIC FACTS FOR THE CASE ----------------------------------
gross_income("Alice", 2017, 684642).
filing_status("Alice", 2017, married_filing_jointly).
itemized_before_limit("Alice", 2017, 23029).
exemption_count("Alice", 2017, 2).          % Alice and spouse, no dependents

% --------- STATUTORY CONSTANTS --------------------------------------
% Standard deduction 2017
basic_standard_deduction(married_filing_jointly, 6000).

% Applicable amount for §68 and §151(d)(3) phase-outs
applicable_amount(married_filing_jointly, 300000).

% Exemption amount (pre-phase-out)
exemption_amount(2000).

% --------- HELPER PREDICATES ----------------------------------------
max(A, B, A) :- A >= B, !.
max(_, B, B).

min(A, B, A) :- A =< B, !.
min(_, B, B).

% ceiling division helper for positive numbers
ceil_div(PosNum, Divisor, Result) :-
    Temp is PosNum / Divisor,
    Result is ceiling(Temp).

% --------- ITEMIZED DEDUCTION LIMIT (§68) ----------------------------
itemized_after_limit(Status, AGI, ItemizedBefore, ItemizedAfter) :-
    applicable_amount(Status, AppAmt),
    Excess is max(AGI - AppAmt, 0),
    Lim1  is 0.03 * Excess,
    Lim2  is 0.80 * ItemizedBefore,
    min(Lim1, Lim2, Reduction),
    ItemizedAfter is ItemizedBefore - Reduction.

% --------- PERSONAL EXEMPTIONS WITH PHASE-OUT (§151) ----------------
personal_exemptions(Status, AGI, Count, FinalExemption) :-
    exemption_amount(Each),
    Base is Each * Count,
    applicable_amount(Status, AppAmt),
    Excess is max(AGI - AppAmt, 0),
    ( Excess =:= 0 ->
        FinalExemption = Base
    ;  ceil_div(Excess, 2500, Increments),
       Percentage is Increments * 0.02,
       ( Percentage >= 1 ->
            FinalExemption = 0
       ;  ReductionFactor is 1 - Percentage,
          FinalExemption is Base * ReductionFactor )
    ).

% --------- CHOOSE GREATER OF STANDARD OR ITEMIZED -------------------
deduction(Status, AGI, ItemizedBefore, DeductionChosen) :-
    basic_standard_deduction(Status, Std),
    itemized_after_limit(Status, AGI, ItemizedBefore, ItemAfterLimit),
    max(Std, ItemAfterLimit, DeductionChosen).

% --------- INCOME TAX (§1(a) Married Filing Jointly) -----------------
income_tax_married_joint(TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 36900 ->
          Tax is 0.15 * TI
    ; TI =< 89150 ->
          Tax is 5535 + 0.28 * (TI - 36900)
    ; TI =< 140000 ->
          Tax is 20165 + 0.31 * (TI - 89150)
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
          Tax is 35928.5 + 0.36 * (TI - 140000)
    ; Tax is 75528.5 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000)
    ).

% --------- MAIN TAX COMPUTATION -------------------------------------
tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    gross_income(Person, Year, GI),
    filing_status(Person, Year, married_filing_jointly),   % only case we need here
    itemized_before_limit(Person, Year, ItemBefore),
    deduction(married_filing_jointly, GI, ItemBefore, DeductionChosen),
    exemption_count(Person, Year, Cnt),
    personal_exemptions(married_filing_jointly, GI, Cnt, Exemptions),
    TaxableIncome is GI - DeductionChosen - Exemptions,
    income_tax_married_joint(TaxableIncome, Tax).

% --------- QUERY -----------------------------------------------------
:- tax("Alice", 2017, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice, born Jun 7, 1964, has a son, Bob, born Feb 7, 1988. Bob has a son, Charlie, born Jun 29, 2014. Alice, Bob and Charlie have the same principal place of abode in 2015. Bob's gross income in 2015 is $43591. Bob takes the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Bob have to pay in 2015?
reference_prolog:
/* ─────────────────────────  STATUTORY RULES  ───────────────────────── */

/* ---- helpers ---- */

sumlist([], Acc, Acc).
sumlist([H|T], Acc0, Sum) :-
    Acc1 is Acc0 + H,
    sumlist(T, Acc1, Sum).

/* ---- default negatives (closed world for unspecified statuses/facts) ---- */

married(_P,_Y) :- fail.
surviving_spouse(_P,_Y) :- fail.
nonresident_alien(_P,_Y) :- fail.

filed_joint_return(_P,_Y) :- fail.          % for §152(c)(1)(E)
maintains_household(_P,_Y) :- fail.         % for §2(a)/(b) household maintenance
over_half_cost(_P,_Y) :- fail.              % for §2(a)/(b) household cost test

/* ---- age computation (sufficient for this problem) ---- */

birth_year(P, Y) :- dob(P, _M, _D, Y).

age(P, TaxYear, Age) :-
    birth_year(P, BY),
    Age is TaxYear - BY.

/* ---- §152 relationships: child / descendant-of-child ---- */

descendant_of(Ancestor, Person) :-
    child(Ancestor, Person).
descendant_of(Ancestor, Person) :-
    child(Ancestor, X),
    descendant_of(X, Person).

relationship_152c2(Taxpayer, Person) :-
    child(Taxpayer, Person).
relationship_152c2(Taxpayer, Person) :-
    descendant_of(Taxpayer, Person).

/* ---- §152(c) qualifying child ---- */

same_principal_place_of_abode_more_than_half_year(Taxpayer, Person, Y) :-
    lived_with(Taxpayer, Person, Y).

qualifying_child(Taxpayer, Person, Y) :-
    relationship_152c2(Taxpayer, Person),
    same_principal_place_of_abode_more_than_half_year(Taxpayer, Person, Y),
    age(Person, Y, ChildAge),
    age(Taxpayer, Y, TaxpayerAge),
    ChildAge < 25,
    ChildAge < TaxpayerAge,
    \+ filed_joint_return(Person, Y).

/* ---- §152(a) dependent (only qualifying child needed here) ---- */
dependent(Taxpayer, Person, Y) :-
    qualifying_child(Taxpayer, Person, Y).

/* ---- filing status: §2(a) surviving spouse, §2(b) head of household, else single ---- */

surviving_spouse_status(P,Y) :-
    % would require spouse died in prior 2 years + household for dependent child etc.
    % no such facts in this case
    fail.

head_of_household(P,Y) :-
    \+ married(P,Y),
    \+ surviving_spouse_status(P,Y),
    maintains_household(P,Y),
    over_half_cost(P,Y),
    dependent(P, Child, Y),
    same_principal_place_of_abode_more_than_half_year(P, Child, Y).

status(P,Y,head_of_household) :- head_of_household(P,Y).
status(P,Y,surviving_spouse)  :- surviving_spouse_status(P,Y).
status(P,Y,married_joint)     :- married(P,Y), joint_return(P,Y).
status(P,Y,married_separate)  :- married(P,Y), \+ joint_return(P,Y).
status(P,Y,single) :-
    \+ married(P,Y),
    \+ surviving_spouse_status(P,Y),
    \+ head_of_household(P,Y).

joint_return(_P,_Y) :- fail.

/* ---- §63(c) standard deduction (2015 amounts; special 2018-2025 not applicable) ---- */

standard_deduction_amount(single,            _Y, 3000).
standard_deduction_amount(head_of_household, _Y, 4400).
standard_deduction_amount(surviving_spouse,  _Y, 6000).
standard_deduction_amount(married_joint,     _Y, 6000).
standard_deduction_amount(married_separate,  _Y, 3000).

standard_deduction_for(P,Y,SD) :-
    status(P,Y,S),
    standard_deduction_amount(S,Y,SD).

/* ---- §151 personal exemptions (2015: $2,000 each) ---- */

exemption_amount(2015, 2000).
exemption_amount(Y, 2000) :- Y \= 2015.  % fallback given provided statute (pre-2018 rule)

personal_exemptions(P,Y,TotalExempt) :-
    exemption_amount(Y,Amt),
    findall(Amt, dependent(P, _Dep, Y), DepList),
    length(DepList, NDeps),
    TotalExempt is Amt * (1 + NDeps).   % taxpayer + dependents (no spouse here)

/* ---- income and taxable income: §63(b) ---- */

adjusted_gross_income(P,Y,AGI) :- gross_income(P,Y,AGI).

taxable_income(P,Y,TI) :-
    adjusted_gross_income(P,Y,AGI),
    standard_deduction_for(P,Y,SD),
    personal_exemptions(P,Y,PE),
    TI0 is AGI - SD - PE,
    ( TI0 > 0 -> TI = TI0 ; TI = 0 ).

/* ---- §1 rate schedules ---- */

tax_single(TI,Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 22100 ->
        Tax is 0.15 * TI
    ; TI =< 53500 ->
        Tax is 3315 + 0.28 * (TI - 22100)
    ; TI =< 115000 ->
        Tax is 12107 + 0.31 * (TI - 53500)
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        Tax is 31172 + 0.36 * (TI - 115000)
    ;   Tax is 79772 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000)
    ).

tax_head_of_household(TI,Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 29600 ->
        Tax is 0.15 * TI
    ; TI =< 76400 ->
        Tax is 4440 + 0.28 * (TI - 29600)
    ; TI =< 127500 ->
        Tax is 17544 + 0.31 * (TI - 76400)
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        Tax is 33385 + 0.36 * (TI - 127500)
    ;   Tax is 77485 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000)
    ).

tax_married_joint_or_surviving(TI,Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 36900 ->
        Tax is 0.15 * TI
    ; TI =< 89150 ->
        Tax is 5535 + 0.28 * (TI - 36900)
    ; TI =< 140000 ->
        Tax is 20165 + 0.31 * (TI - 89150)
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        Tax is 35928.50 + 0.36 * (TI - 140000)
    ;   Tax is 75528.50 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000)
    ).

tax_married_separate(TI,Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 18450 ->
        Tax is 0.15 * TI
    ; TI =< 44575 ->
        Tax is 2767.50 + 0.28 * (TI - 18450)
    ; TI =< 70000 ->
        Tax is 10082.50 + 0.31 * (TI - 44575)
    ; TI =< 125000 ->
        Tax is 17964.25 + 0.36 * (TI - 70000)
    ;   Tax is 37764.25 + 0.396 * (TI - 125000)
    ).

tax(P,Y,Tax) :-
    taxable_income(P,Y,TI),
    status(P,Y,Status),
    ( Status = single ->
        tax_single(TI,Tax)
    ; Status = head_of_household ->
        tax_head_of_household(TI,Tax)
    ; (Status = married_joint ; Status = surviving_spouse) ->
        tax_married_joint_or_surviving(TI,Tax)
    ; Status = married_separate ->
        tax_married_separate(TI,Tax)
    ).

/* name alias required by prompt */
tax("Bob", Y, Tax) :- tax(bob, Y, Tax).

/* ─────────────────────────────  CASE FACTS  ───────────────────────────── */

dob(alice,   6,  7, 1964).
dob(bob,     2,  7, 1988).
dob(charlie, 6, 29, 2014).

child(alice, bob).
child(bob, charlie).

% same principal place of abode in 2015
lived_with(alice, bob, 2015).
lived_with(alice, charlie, 2015).
lived_with(bob, charlie, 2015).

gross_income(bob, 2015, 43591).

/* ───────────────────────────────  QUERY  ─────────────────────────────── */

:- tax("Bob", 2015, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

text: Alice has paid $3200 to Bob for domestic service done from Feb 1st, 2012 to Sep 2nd, 2012, in Baltimore, Maryland, USA. Bob has paid $4500 to Alice for work done from Apr 1st, 2012 to Dec 31st, 2012. Alice takes the standard deduction in 2012.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2012?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ---------- Statutory rules relevant to the problem ---------- */

/* ----------  §63  –  taxable income (standard-deduction filer) ---------- */
standard_deduction(unmarried, 3000).              % §63(c)(2)(C)
personal_exemption_amount(2000).                  % §151(d)(1)

adjusted_gross_income(Person, Year, AGI) :-
    wages_received(Person, Year, AGI).            % only wages in this case

taxable_income(Person, Year, TI) :-
    filing_status(Person, Year, unmarried),       % status worked out below
    adjusted_gross_income(Person, Year, AGI),
    standard_deduction(unmarried, SD),
    personal_exemption_amount(PE),
    TI0 is AGI - SD - PE,
    ( TI0 > 0 -> TI = TI0 ; TI = 0 ).             % cannot be negative

/* ----------  §1(c)  –  income-tax table for unmarried individuals ---------- */
income_tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
    ( TI =< 22100 -> Tax is 0.15 * TI            % §1(c)(i) – only bracket needed here
    ; TI =< 53500 -> Tax is 3315  + 0.28*(TI-22100)   % §1(c)(ii)
    ; TI =<115000 -> Tax is 12107 + 0.31*(TI-53500)   % §1(c)(iii)
    ; TI =<250000 -> Tax is 31172 + 0.36*(TI-115000)  % §1(c)(iv)
    ;                  Tax is 79772 + 0.396*(TI-250000)  % §1(c)(v)
    ).

/* ----------  FUTA  –  §§3301, 3306 ---------- */
/* employer if ≥$1,000 cash paid for domestic service (§3306(a)(3)) */
is_employer_domestic(Person, Year) :-
    wages_paid_domestic(Person, Year, Cash),
    Cash >= 1000.

employment_domestic(Person, Year, Cash) :-
    is_employer_domestic(Person, Year),
    wages_paid_domestic(Person, Year, Cash).      % domestic service is “employment” §3306(c)(2)

futa_tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    employment_domestic(Person, Year, Cash),
    Tax is 0.06 * Cash.                           % §3301 (6 %)

/* ----------  Overall tax owed by an individual ---------- */
tax(Person, Year, TotalTax) :-
    income_tax(Person, Year, IncomeTax),
    ( futa_tax(Person, Year, FUTA) -> true ; FUTA = 0 ),
    TotalTax is IncomeTax + FUTA.

/* ----------  Filing-status determination (simplified) ---------- */
filing_status(Person, Year, unmarried) :-          % no facts suggesting spouse, etc.
    \+ married(Person, Year).

/* ----------  Case-specific facts ---------- */
wages_received(alice, 2012, 4500).                 % Alice earned from Bob
wages_paid_domestic(alice, 2012, 3200).            % Alice paid Bob for housework
married(alice, 2012) :- fail.                      % unknown/false – keep Prolog silent

/* ----------  Query ---------- */
:- tax(alice, 2012, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice has paid $3200 to her father Bob for work done from Feb 1st, 2017 to Sep 2nd, 2017, in Baltimore, Maryland, USA. Alice and Charlie got married on April 5th, 2012. Alice and Charlie were legally separated under a decree of divorce on September 16th, 2017. Alice's gross income in 2017 was $756420. Alice takes the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/*********************************************************************
  Statutory rules (abridged to the provisions that matter here)
*********************************************************************/

% ---------- filing-status ------------------------------------------
filing_status(Person, Year, single) :-
    legally_separated(Person, Date),
    year(Date, Year).     % §7703(a)(2) – legally-separated ≡ not married

% ---------- standard deduction (§63(c)(2)) --------------------------
standard_deduction(single,   2017, 3000).
standard_deduction(joint,    2017, 6000).   % 200 % of $3 000
standard_deduction(head,     2017, 4400).
standard_deduction(widower,  2017, 6000).

% ---------- personal-exemption (§151) -------------------------------
personal_exemption_amount(2017, 2000).

% §151(d)(3) phase-out
%  – 2 % for every $2 500 (or fraction) AGI over threshold (§68(b))
%  – capped at 100 %
exemption_phaseout_fraction(AGI, Status, Frac) :-
    phaseout_threshold(Status, Threshold),
    Excess  is max(0, AGI - Threshold),
    Steps   is ceiling(Excess / 2500),
    Frac0   is Steps * 0.02,
    Frac    is min(1.00, Frac0).

phaseout_threshold(single, 250000).
phaseout_threshold(head,   275000).
phaseout_threshold(joint,  300000).
phaseout_threshold(sep,    150000).
phaseout_threshold(widower,300000).

% ---------- taxable income (§63) ------------------------------------
taxable_income(Person, Year, Taxable) :-
    gross_income(Person, Year, Gross),
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    standard_deduction(Status, Year, StdDed),
    personal_exemption(Person, Year, ExemptDed),
    Taxable is Gross - StdDed - ExemptDed.

personal_exemption(Person, Year, Deduction) :-
    personal_exemption_amount(Year, EA),
    gross_income(Person, Year, AGI),
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    exemption_phaseout_fraction(AGI, Status, Frac),
    Deduction is EA * (1 - Frac).

% ---------- income-tax computation (§1) -----------------------------
% helper: bracket( Status, UpperLimit, BaseTax, RateOver )
% UpperLimit =  inf  for the last bracket
bracket(single, 22100,     0,      0.15).
bracket(single, 53500,  3315,      0.28).
bracket(single,115000, 12107,      0.31).
bracket(single,250000, 31172,      0.36).
bracket(single,    inf,79772,      0.396).

income_tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    bracket(Status, Limit, Base, Rate),
    ( Limit == inf ->     Excess is TI - 250000  % only reached in last bracket
    ; TI =< Limit ->      Excess is TI - Previous, !
    ;                     fail ),
    tax(Status, Limit, Previous),       % find lower-bracket edge
    Tax is Base + Rate * Excess.

% find start of a bracket
tax(Status, Limit, Start) :-
    bracket(Status, Limit, _, _),
    bracket_start(Status, Limit, Start).

bracket_start(_, 22100,    0).
bracket_start(_, 53500,22100).
bracket_start(_,115000,53500).
bracket_start(_,250000,115000).
bracket_start(_,    inf,250000).

% ---------- total tax Alice owes in 2017 ----------------------------
tax(Person, Year, Tax) :- income_tax(Person, Year, Tax).

/*********************************************************************
  Case-specific facts
*********************************************************************/
gross_income(alice, 2017, 756420).

% Alice & Charlie legally separated (divorce decree) on 16-Sep-2017
legally_separated(alice, date(2017,9,16)).

year(date(Y,_,_), Y).

/*********************************************************************
  Query
*********************************************************************/
:- tax(alice, 2017, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

/* ---------- Statutory rules (only what is needed for this case) ---------- */

% Exemption amount for 2013 (§151(d)(1))
exemption_amount(2000).

% Standard deduction – zero for non-resident aliens (§63(c)(6)(B))

    nonresident_alien(Person, Year).

% Personal exemptions – Alice can claim only her own (§151(b))
personal_exemptions(_, _, PE) :-

taxable_income(Person, Year, TaxableIncome) :-

% Main tax predicate

text: Alice and Bob got married on Feb 3rd, 1992. Alice was a nonresident alien. Alice died on July 9th, 2014. Bob married Charlie on September 14th, 2015. Bob's gross income for the year 2015 is $28864, Charlie's gross income is $27953. Bob and Charlie file jointly in 2015 and take the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Bob have to pay in 2015?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
%  Statutory rules (only the provisions needed for 2015)  %
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

% personal-exemption amount (sec. 151(d)(1))
exemption_amount(2000).

% basic standard deduction base amount in 2015 (sec. 63(c)(2)(C))
base_standard_deduction_amount(3000).

% standard deduction for each filing status (sec. 63(c)(2))
standard_deduction(joint,Amount) :-
    base_standard_deduction_amount(Base),
    Amount is 2*Base.                 % 200 % for joint return

standard_deduction(single,Amount) :-
    base_standard_deduction_amount(Amount).

standard_deduction(head_of_household,4400).  % unused here

% number of personal exemptions allowed (sec. 151(b))
personal_exemption_count(joint,2).
personal_exemption_count(single,1).

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
%          Sec. 1(a) 2015 rate schedule – joint           %
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

tax_bracket_joint(Taxable,Tax) :-
    (   Taxable =< 36900 ->
        Tax is 0.15*Taxable
    ;   Taxable =< 89150 ->
        Excess is Taxable - 36900,
        Tax is 5535 + 0.28*Excess
    ;   Taxable =< 140000 ->
        Excess is Taxable - 89150,
        Tax is 20165 + 0.31*Excess
    ;   Taxable =< 250000 ->
        Excess is Taxable - 140000,
        Tax is 35928.50 + 0.36*Excess
    ;   Excess is Taxable - 250000,
        Tax is 75528.50 + 0.396*Excess
    ).

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
%                     Case-specific facts                 %
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

% Gross income (2015)
gross_income(bob,2015,28864).
gross_income(charlie,2015,27953).

% Marriage status in 2015
spouse(bob,charlie,2015).
spouse(charlie,bob,2015).

% They choose to file a single joint return (fact given)
filed_joint(bob,2015).
filed_joint(charlie,2015).

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
%                 Derived financial quantities             %
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

% Total gross income on the joint return
total_gross_income(Person,Year,Total) :-
    filed_joint(Person,Year),
    spouse(Person,Spouse,Year),
    gross_income(Person,Year,Inc1),
    gross_income(Spouse,Year,Inc2),
    Total is Inc1 + Inc2.

% Standard deduction applicable
standard_deduction_amount(Person,Year,Amount) :-
    filed_joint(Person,Year),
    standard_deduction(joint,Amount).

% Personal-exemption deduction
personal_exemptions_total(Person,Year,Total) :-
    filed_joint(Person,Year),
    personal_exemption_count(joint,Count),
    exemption_amount(Each),
    Total is Each*Count.

% Taxable income (sec. 63(b))
taxable_income(Person,Year,Taxable) :-
    total_gross_income(Person,Year,GI),
    standard_deduction_amount(Person,Year,SD),
    personal_exemptions_total(Person,Year,PE),
    Temp is GI - SD - PE,
    (Temp < 0 -> Taxable = 0 ; Taxable = Temp).

% Actual tax due
tax(Person,Year,Tax) :-
    filed_joint(Person,Year),
    taxable_income(Person,Year,Taxable),
    tax_bracket_joint(Taxable,Tax).

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
%                         Query                            %
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
:- tax(bob, 2015, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: In 2017, Alice was paid $117192. Alice and Bob got married on Feb 3rd, 2017. Alice was a nonresident alien from August 23rd, 2016 to September 15th, 2018. Bob earned $37820 in 2017. Alice takes the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
% Facts
person(alice).
person(bob).

income(alice, 2017, 117192).
income(bob, 2017, 37820).

spouse(alice, bob).
spouse(bob, alice).
marriage_date(alice, bob, date(2017,2,3)).
marriage_date(bob, alice, date(2017,2,3)).

nonresident_alien_period(alice, date(2016,8,23), date(2018,9,15)).

% Helper predicates
year_of_date(date(Y,_,_), Y).

nonresident_alien_year(Person, Year) :-
    nonresident_alien_period(Person, date(Y1,_,_), date(Y2,_,_)),
    Y1 =< Year, Year =< Y2.

married(Person, Year) :-
    spouse(Person, Spouse),
    marriage_date(Person, Spouse, date(Y,_,_)),
    Y =< Year.

% Joint return not allowed if either spouse is nonresident alien
joint_return_allowed(Person, Year) :-
    married(Person, Year),
    spouse(Person, Spouse),
    \+ nonresident_alien_year(Person, Year),
    \+ nonresident_alien_year(Spouse, Year).

% Filing status
filing_status(Person, Year, married_separate) :-
    married(Person, Year),
    \+ joint_return_allowed(Person, Year).

% AGI (no adjustments)
agi(Person, Year, AGI) :-
    income(Person, Year, AGI).

% Standard deduction
standard_deduction(Person, Year, 0) :-
    nonresident_alien_year(Person, Year), !.
standard_deduction(Person, Year, SD) :-
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    base_standard_deduction(Status, Year, SD).

base_standard_deduction(married_joint, _Year, 6000).
base_standard_deduction(surviving_spouse, _Year, 6000).
base_standard_deduction(head_of_household, _Year, 4400).
base_standard_deduction(married_separate, _Year, 3000).
base_standard_deduction(single, _Year, 3000).

% Exemption amount
exemption_amount(Year, 0) :-
    Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025, !.
exemption_amount(_Year, 2000).

% Applicable amount for phaseout
applicable_amount(married_joint, 300000).
applicable_amount(surviving_spouse, 300000).
applicable_amount(head_of_household, 275000).
applicable_amount(single, 250000).
applicable_amount(married_separate, 150000).

% Personal exemptions with phaseout
personal_exemptions(Person, Year, Total) :-
    exemption_amount(Year, ExAmt),
    Ex1 is ExAmt,
    ( filing_status(Person, Year, married_separate),
      spouse(Person, Spouse),
      income(Spouse, Year, IncomeS),
      IncomeS =:= 0
    -> Ex2 is ExAmt
    ; Ex2 is 0),
    BaseTotal is Ex1 + Ex2,
    apply_phaseout(Person, Year, BaseTotal, Total).

apply_phaseout(Person, Year, Amount, Reduced) :-
    agi(Person, Year, AGI),
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    applicable_amount(Status, Applicable),
    ( AGI =< Applicable ->
        Reduced = Amount
    ;
        ( Status = married_separate -> Step = 1250 ; Step = 2500 ),
        Excess is AGI - Applicable,
        Incr is ceiling(Excess / Step),
        Percentage is Incr * 0.02,
        ( Percentage > 1 -> Pct = 1 ; Pct = Percentage ),
        Reduced is Amount * (1 - Pct)
    ).

% Taxable income
taxable_income(Person, Year, TI) :-
    agi(Person, Year, AGI),
    standard_deduction(Person, Year, SD),
    personal_exemptions(Person, Year, PE),
    TI0 is AGI - SD - PE,
    (TI0 < 0 -> TI = 0 ; TI = TI0).

% Tax rates
tax_rate(married_separate, Income, Tax) :-
    ( Income =< 18450 -> Tax is 0.15 * Income
    ; Income =< 44575 -> Tax is 2767.50 + 0.28 * (Income - 18450)
    ; Income =< 70000 -> Tax is 10082.50 + 0.31 * (Income - 44575)
    ; Income =< 125000 -> Tax is 17964.25 + 0.36 * (Income - 70000)
    ; Tax is 37764.25 + 0.396 * (Income - 125000)
    ).

% Overall tax
tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    tax_rate(Status, TI, Tax).

:- tax(alice, 2017, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

text: Alice was paid $200 in March 2017 for services performed at Johns Hopkins Hospital. Alice was a patient at Johns Hopkins Hospital from March 15th, 2017 to April 2nd, 2017. Bob is Alice's son since April 15th, 2014. Bob and Alice have the same principal place of abode, a house maintained by Alice. Alice takes the standard deduction in 2017.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ---------- FACTS DESCRIBING 2017 CASE ---------- */

income("Alice", 2017, 200).                 % §61 (gross income) – Alice earned $200
dependent("Bob", "Alice", 2017).            % Bob is Alice’s dependent (§151, §152)
filing_status("Alice", 2017, hoh).          % Head-of-Household (§2(b))

/* ---------- STATUTORY PARAMETERS FOR 2017 ---------- */

% Personal-exemption amount (§151(d)(1))
exemption_amount(2017, 2000).

% Standard deduction for 2017 (§63(c)(2))
standard_deduction(hoh, 2017, 4400).

/* ---------- HELPER RULES ---------- */

% Count dependents for whom the taxpayer is entitled to §151 deduction
number_of_dependents(Taxpayer, Year, Count) :-
    findall(D, dependent(D, Taxpayer, Year), L),
    length(L, Count).

% Total personal-exemption units = taxpayer + dependents  (§151(b) & (c))
total_personal_exemptions(Taxpayer, Year, Total) :-
    number_of_dependents(Taxpayer, Year, DCount),
    Total is DCount + 1.                    % add the taxpayer

/* ---------- COMPUTE TAXABLE INCOME (§63) ---------- */

taxable_income(Taxpayer, Year, TaxableIncome) :-
    income(Taxpayer, Year, Gross),
    filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, Status),
    standard_deduction(Status, Year, SD),
    total_personal_exemptions(Taxpayer, Year, ExCount),
    exemption_amount(Year, ExAmt),
    Deduct is SD + ExCount * ExAmt,
    Temp is Gross - Deduct,
    ( Temp > 0 -> TaxableIncome = Temp ; TaxableIncome = 0).

/* ---------- HEAD-OF-HOUSEHOLD RATE SCHEDULE 2017 (§1(b)) ---------- */

tax_liability(hoh, TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 29600  -> Tax is 0.15  * TI
    ; TI =< 76400  -> Tax is 4440  + 0.28 * (TI - 29600)
    ; TI =< 127500 -> Tax is 17544 + 0.31 * (TI - 76400)
    ; TI =< 250000 -> Tax is 33385 + 0.36 * (TI - 127500)
    ;                 Tax is 77485 + 0.396* (TI - 250000)
    ).

/* ---------- OVERALL TAX COMPUTATION ---------- */
tax(Taxpayer, Year, Tax) :-
    taxable_income(Taxpayer, Year, TI),
    ( TI =:= 0
    -> Tax = 0
    ; filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, Status),
      tax_liability(Status, TI, Tax)
    ).

/* ---------- QUERY ---------- */
:- tax("Alice", 2017, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice has paid $3200 to Bob for agricultural labor done from Feb 1st, 2020 to Sep 2nd, 2020. Alice paid Bob with eggs, grapes and hay. Alice has been married since 1999. Alice files a joint return with her spouse for 2020. Alice's and her spouse's gross income for the year 2020 is $103272. Alice takes the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2020?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
% ---------- facts ----------
taxpayer(alice).
filing_status(alice,2020,married_filing_jointly).
gross_income(alice,2020,103272).

% In-kind payment for agricultural labor – not cash wages
payment(alice,bob,agricultural_labor,2020,3200,in_kind).

% ---------- FUTA (employer unemployment) tax ----------
% remuneration in kind for agricultural labor is excluded from “wages”
futa_wages(Person,Year,0) :-
    payment(Person,_,agricultural_labor,Year,_,in_kind), !.
futa_wages(_,_,0).

futa_tax(Person,Year,Tax) :-
    futa_wages(Person,Year,Wages),
    Tax is Wages*0.06.

% ---------- standard deduction ----------
basic_standard_deduction(Year,married_filing_jointly,24000) :-
    Year>=2018, Year=<2025, !.
basic_standard_deduction(_,married_filing_jointly,6000).

standard_deduction(Person,Year,SD) :-
    filing_status(Person,Year,Status),
    basic_standard_deduction(Year,Status,SD).

% ---------- personal exemptions (amount = 0 for 2018-2025) ----------
personal_exemption_amount(Year,0) :-
    Year>=2018, Year=<2025, !.
personal_exemption_amount(_,2000).

personal_exemptions(Person,Year,0) :-
    personal_exemption_amount(Year,0), !.
personal_exemptions(_,_,0).  % no dependents given

% ---------- taxable income ----------
taxable_income(Person,Year,TI) :-
    gross_income(Person,Year,GI),
    standard_deduction(Person,Year,SD),
    personal_exemptions(Person,Year,PE),
    TI is GI - SD - PE.

% ---------- income-tax brackets (married filing jointly) ----------
income_tax_mfj(TI,Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 36900 ->
        Tax is TI*0.15
    ; TI =< 89150 ->
        Tax is 5535 + 0.28*(TI-36900)
    ; TI =< 140000 ->
        Tax is 20165 + 0.31*(TI-89150)
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        Tax is 35928.50 + 0.36*(TI-140000)
    ;   Tax is 75528.50 + 0.396*(TI-250000)
    ).

income_tax(Person,Year,Tax) :-
    filing_status(Person,Year,married_filing_jointly),
    taxable_income(Person,Year,TI),
    income_tax_mfj(TI,Tax).

% ---------- total tax ----------
tax(Person,Year,TotalTax) :-
    income_tax(Person,Year,IncomeTax),
    futa_tax(Person,Year,FUTATax),
    TotalTax is IncomeTax + FUTATax.

% ---------- query ----------
:- tax(alice, 2020, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: In 2017, Alice earned $133200. Bob's income in 2017 was $44311. Alice and Bob have been married since Feb 3rd, 2017. Alice has been blind since Feb 28, 2014. Alice has paid $4525 to Charlie for work done in the year 2017. In 2017, Alice has also paid $983 into a retirement fund for Charlie, and $5322 into health insurance for Charlie, both under a plan. Alice and Bob file jointly and take the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
% Facts from the case
income(alice,2017,133200).
income(bob,2017,44311).

married_fact(alice,bob,2017).
joint_return_fact(alice,bob,2017).

blind(alice,2017).

employment(charlie,alice,2017).
cash_payment(alice,charlie,2017,4525).
retirement_payment(alice,charlie,2017,983).
health_payment(alice,charlie,2017,5322).

% Spouse and marriage status
spouse(A,B,Y) :- married_fact(A,B,Y).
spouse(A,B,Y) :- married_fact(B,A,Y).

married_person(P,Y) :- spouse(P,_,Y).

% Joint return status (symmetric)
joint_return(P,S,Y) :- joint_return_fact(P,S,Y).
joint_return(P,S,Y) :- joint_return_fact(S,P,Y).

% Filing status
filing_status(P,Y,married_joint) :- joint_return(P,_,Y).
filing_status(P,Y,married_separate) :- married_person(P,Y), \+ joint_return(P,_,Y).
filing_status(P,Y,single) :- \+ married_person(P,Y).

% Adjusted gross income (AGI)
agi(P,Y,AGI) :-
    joint_return(P,S,Y), !,
    income(P,Y,I1),
    income(S,Y,I2),
    AGI is I1 + I2.
agi(P,Y,AGI) :-
    income(P,Y,AGI).

% Basic standard deduction
base_single_amount(Y,12000) :- Y >= 2018, Y =< 2025.
base_single_amount(Y,3000) :- Y < 2018; Y > 2025.

basic_standard_deduction(married_joint,Y,Amount) :-
    base_single_amount(Y,B),
    Amount is 2*B.
basic_standard_deduction(head_of_household,Y,18000) :- Y >= 2018, Y =< 2025.
basic_standard_deduction(head_of_household,Y,4400) :- Y < 2018; Y > 2025.
basic_standard_deduction(single,Y,Amount) :- base_single_amount(Y,Amount).
basic_standard_deduction(married_separate,Y,Amount) :- base_single_amount(Y,Amount).

% Additional standard deduction for aged or blind
unmarried(P,Y) :- \+ married_person(P,Y).

additional_amount(P,Y,Amount) :-
    (blind(P,Y)),
    (unmarried(P,Y) -> Amount = 750 ; Amount = 600).
additional_amount(_,_,0).

% Persons on the return
persons_on_return(P,Y,[P,S]) :- joint_return(P,S,Y), !.
persons_on_return(P,Y,[P]).

sum_list([],0).
sum_list([H|T],Sum) :- sum_list(T,Rest), Sum is H + Rest.

sum_additional([],_,0).
sum_additional([P|T],Y,Total) :-
    additional_amount(P,Y,A),
    sum_additional(T,Y,Rest),
    Total is A + Rest.

% Standard deduction total
standard_deduction(P,Y,Total) :-
    filing_status(P,Y,Status),
    basic_standard_deduction(Status,Y,Basic),
    persons_on_return(P,Y,Persons),
    sum_additional(Persons,Y,Add),
    Total is Basic + Add.

% Exemption amount
exemption_amount(Y,0) :- Y >= 2018, Y =< 2025.
exemption_amount(Y,2000) :- Y < 2018; Y > 2025.

% Dependents (none in this case)
num_dependents(_,_,0).

num_exemptions(P,Y,N) :-
    joint_return(P,_,Y), !,
    num_dependents(P,Y,D),
    N is 2 + D.
num_exemptions(P,Y,N) :-
    num_dependents(P,Y,D),
    N is 1 + D.

% Applicable amount for phaseout (section 68(b))
applicable_amount(married_joint,300000).
applicable_amount(head_of_household,275000).
applicable_amount(single,250000).
applicable_amount(married_separate,150000).

ceil_div(Excess,Div,Ceil) :- Ceil is (Excess + Div - 1) // Div.

exemption_phaseout(P,Y,Percent) :-
    agi(P,Y,AGI),
    filing_status(P,Y,Status),
    applicable_amount(Status,Applicable),
    (AGI =< Applicable ->
        Percent = 0
    ;
        Excess is AGI - Applicable,
        (Status = married_separate -> Div = 1250 ; Div = 2500),
        ceil_div(Excess,Div,Incr),
        P0 is Incr * 2,
        (P0 > 100 -> Percent = 100 ; Percent = P0)
    ).

personal_exemption_deduction(P,Y,Amount) :-
    exemption_amount(Y,EA),
    num_exemptions(P,Y,N),
    Base is N * EA,
    exemption_phaseout(P,Y,Percent),
    Amount is Base * (100-Percent) / 100.

% Taxable income
taxable_income(P,Y,TI) :-
    agi(P,Y,AGI),
    standard_deduction(P,Y,SD),
    personal_exemption_deduction(P,Y,PE),
    TI0 is AGI - SD - PE,
    (TI0 < 0 -> TI = 0 ; TI = TI0).

% Income tax brackets
tax_joint(TI,Tax) :- TI =< 36900, Tax is 0.15*TI.
tax_joint(TI,Tax) :- TI > 36900, TI =< 89150, Tax is 5535 + 0.28*(TI-36900).
tax_joint(TI,Tax) :- TI > 89150, TI =< 140000, Tax is 20165 + 0.31*(TI-89150).
tax_joint(TI,Tax) :- TI > 140000, TI =< 250000, Tax is 35928.50 + 0.36*(TI-140000).
tax_joint(TI,Tax) :- TI > 250000, Tax is 75528.50 + 0.396*(TI-250000).

tax_head(TI,Tax) :- TI =< 29600, Tax is 0.15*TI.
tax_head(TI,Tax) :- TI > 29600, TI =< 76400, Tax is 4440 + 0.28*(TI-29600).
tax_head(TI,Tax) :- TI > 76400, TI =< 127500, Tax is 17544 + 0.31*(TI-76400).
tax_head(TI,Tax) :- TI > 127500, TI =< 250000, Tax is 33385 + 0.36*(TI-127500).
tax_head(TI,Tax) :- TI > 250000, Tax is 77485 + 0.396*(TI-250000).

tax_single(TI,Tax) :- TI =< 22100, Tax is 0.15*TI.
tax_single(TI,Tax) :- TI > 22100, TI =< 53500, Tax is 3315 + 0.28*(TI-22100).
tax_single(TI,Tax) :- TI > 53500, TI =< 115000, Tax is 12107 + 0.31*(TI-53500).
tax_single(TI,Tax) :- TI > 115000, TI =< 250000, Tax is 31172 + 0.36*(TI-115000).
tax_single(TI,Tax) :- TI > 250000, Tax is 79772 + 0.396*(TI-250000).

tax_separate(TI,Tax) :- TI =< 18450, Tax is 0.15*TI.
tax_separate(TI,Tax) :- TI > 18450, TI =< 44575, Tax is 2767.50 + 0.28*(TI-18450).
tax_separate(TI,Tax) :- TI > 44575, TI =< 70000, Tax is 10082.50 + 0.31*(TI-44575).
tax_separate(TI,Tax) :- TI > 70000, TI =< 125000, Tax is 17964.25 + 0.36*(TI-70000).
tax_separate(TI,Tax) :- TI > 125000, Tax is 37764.25 + 0.396*(TI-125000).

income_tax(P,Y,Tax) :-
    filing_status(P,Y,Status),
    taxable_income(P,Y,TI),
    (Status = married_joint -> tax_joint(TI,Tax)
    ;Status = head_of_household -> tax_head(TI,Tax)
    ;Status = single -> tax_single(TI,Tax)
    ;Status = married_separate -> tax_separate(TI,Tax)).

% Wages and employer tax
remuneration(Emp,Emply,Y,A) :- cash_payment(Emp,Emply,Y,A).
remuneration(Emp,Emply,Y,A) :- retirement_payment(Emp,Emply,Y,A).
remuneration(Emp,Emply,Y,A) :- health_payment(Emp,Emply,Y,A).

excluded_remuneration(Emp,Emply,Y,A) :- health_payment(Emp,Emply,Y,A).

total_remuneration(Emp,Emply,Y,Total) :-
    findall(A, remuneration(Emp,Emply,Y,A), L),
    sum_list(L,Total).

total_excluded(Emp,Emply,Y,Total) :-
    findall(A, excluded_remuneration(Emp,Emply,Y,A), L),
    sum_list(L,Total).

wages_employee(Emp,Emply,Y,W) :-
    employment(Emply,Emp,Y),
    total_remuneration(Emp,Emply,Y,Total),
    total_excluded(Emp,Emply,Y,Excl),
    W0 is Total - Excl,
    (W0 < 0 -> W1 = 0 ; W1 = W0),
    (W1 > 7000 -> W = 7000 ; W = W1).

total_wages_employer(Emp,Y,Total) :-
    findall(W, wages_employee(Emp,Emply,Y,W), L),
    sum_list(L,Total).

employer_tax(Emp,Y,Tax) :-
    total_wages_employer(Emp,Y,Total),
    (Total >= 1500 -> Tax is 0.06 * Total ; Tax = 0).

% Total tax
tax(P,Y,TaxRounded) :-
    income_tax(P,Y,IncomeTax),
    employer_tax(P,Y,EmployerTax),
    Total is IncomeTax + EmployerTax,
    TaxRounded is round(Total*100)/100.

:- tax(alice, 2017, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

text: Alice and Bob got married on Feb 3rd, 1992. Alice and Bob have a child, Charlie, born October 9th, 2000. Alice died on July 9th, 2014. From 2004 to 2018, Bob furnished the costs of maintaining the home where he and Charlie lived during that time. Charlie married Dan on Feb 14th, 2018, and they file a joint return that same year. Bob's gross income in 2018 was $132268. Bob takes the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Bob have to pay in 2018?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* =========  Basic facts from the case  ========= */

person(bob).
person(alice).
person(charlie).
person(dan).

spouse(bob,alice).           % Bob and Alice were married
spouse(alice,bob).

marriage_date(bob,alice,date(1992,2,3)).
death(alice,date(2014,7,9)). % Alice died in 2014

child_of(charlie,bob).
child_of(charlie,alice).

marriage_date(charlie,dan,date(2018,2,14)).
joint_return(charlie,dan,2018).

gross_income(bob,2018,132268).

% Bob has maintained and paid >50% of the cost of the household he and
% Charlie lived in from 2004-2018.
maintains_household(bob,home1,Year) :- between(2004,2018,Year).
principal_abode(charlie,home1,Year) :- between(2004,2018,Year).

/* =========  Auxiliary predicates  ========= */

/*  Was P married at the close of Year?  */
married_at_close(P,Year) :-
    spouse(P,S),
    \+ (death(S,date(DeathYear,_,_)), DeathYear =< Year).   % spouse still alive.

/*  Did P remarry by close of Year (other than original spouse)?  */
remarried(_,_) :- fail.   % no remarriage mentioned in the facts

/*  Is P a surviving spouse for Year?  §2(a) */
surviving_spouse(P,Year) :-
    spouse(P,S),
    death(S,date(DY,_,_)),
    YearsSince is Year - DY,
    YearsSince =< 2,                 % “either of the two years immediately preceding”
    \+ remarried(P,Year).

/*  Can P claim anyone as a dependent under §152 for Year?  
    (Here we only encode the facts we have – Charlie cannot be a dependent
    in 2018 because he filed a joint return with Dan.)          */
dependent_of(_,_,false).   % nobody qualifies in this case

/*  Head-of-household test for P in Year.                     */
head_of_household(P,Year) :-
    \+ married_at_close(P,Year),
    \+ surviving_spouse(P,Year),
    maintains_household(P,_,Year),
    dependent_of(P,Year,true).

/*  Filing status for P in Year.                              */
filing_status(P,Year,surviving_spouse) :- surviving_spouse(P,Year), !.
filing_status(P,Year,head_of_household) :- head_of_household(P,Year), !.
filing_status(_,_,single).   % default

/* =========  Deductions  ========= */

/*  Standard deduction amounts, §63(c)(2) & (7)  */
basic_standard_deduction(Year,surviving_spouse,24000) :- Year>=2018,Year=<2025.
basic_standard_deduction(Year,head_of_household,18000) :- Year>=2018,Year=<2025.
basic_standard_deduction(Year,single,12000)            :- Year>=2018,Year=<2025.

/*  Additional standard deduction for aged/blind – none stated here. */
additional_standard_deduction(_,_,0).

standard_deduction(P,Year,SD) :-
    filing_status(P,Year,Status),
    basic_standard_deduction(Year,Status,Basic),
    additional_standard_deduction(P,Year,Add),
    SD is Basic + Add.

/*  Personal exemption – zero for 2018-2025, §151(d)(5) */
personal_exemption(_,Year,0) :- Year>=2018,Year=<2025.

/* =========  Taxable income  ========= */

taxable_income(P,Year,TI) :-
    gross_income(P,Year,GI),
    standard_deduction(P,Year,SD),
    personal_exemption(P,Year,PE),
    TI is GI - SD - PE.

/* =========  Tax computation (single rates)  =========
   §1(c) brackets:
     (i)  up to 22,100      – 15%
     (ii) next to 53,500    – 28%
     (iii) next to 115,000  – 31%
     (iv) next to 250,000   – 36%
     (v) over 250,000       – 39.6%
*/
single_tax(TI,Tax) :-              % TI = taxable income
    ( TI =< 22100 ->
        Tax is 0.15*TI
    ; TI =< 53500 ->
        Tax is 3315 + 0.28*(TI-22100)
    ; TI =< 115000 ->
        Tax is 12107 + 0.31*(TI-53500)
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        Tax is 31172 + 0.36*(TI-115000)
    ;                       % over 250,000
        Tax is 79772 + 0.396*(TI-250000)
    ).

/*  Master predicate – tax owed by P in Year  */
tax(P,Year,Tax) :-
    filing_status(P,Year,single),        % only case that applies here
    taxable_income(P,Year,TI),
    single_tax(TI,Tax).

/* =========  Query  ========= */
:- tax(bob,2018,Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n',[Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: Bob is Alice and Charlie's father. Bob had no income in 2015. Alice's gross income in 2015 is $264215. Alice takes the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2015?
reference_prolog:
% =========================
% Statutory logic (relevant parts)
% =========================

% ---- Helpers ----
to_cents(Dollars, Cents) :- Cents is Dollars * 100.

max0(X, 0) :- X =< 0, !.
max0(X, X).

min(X,Y,X) :- X =< Y, !.
min(_,Y,Y).

ceiling_div(PosInt, Div, Q) :-
    % assumes Div > 0
    Q is (PosInt + Div - 1) // Div.

% ---- §7703 Marital status (simplified to facts in this problem) ----
not_married(Person, Year) :- marital_status(Person, Year, not_married).

% ---- §152 qualifying relative (only elements provided in prompt) ----
relationship_152d(Ind, Taxpayer) :-
    parent(Ind, Taxpayer).   % father or mother relationship fits §152(d)(2)(C)

qualifying_relative(Ind, Taxpayer, Year) :-
    relationship_152d(Ind, Taxpayer),
    income(Ind, Year, 0),
    \+ qualifying_child(Ind, _AnyTaxpayer, Year).

% If no facts assert qualifying_child/3, assume false by closed world.
qualifying_child(_,_,_) :- fail.

dependent(Taxpayer, Ind, Year) :-
    qualifying_relative(Ind, Taxpayer, Year).

% ---- §151 exemption amount ----
exemption_amount(Year, 0) :- Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025, !.  % §151(d)(5)
exemption_amount(_Year, 2000).                                 % §151(d)(1)

% Count personal exemptions: taxpayer + each dependent (§151(a),(c))
personal_exemption_count(Taxpayer, Year, Count) :-
    findall(D, dependent(Taxpayer, D, Year), Ds),
    length(Ds, DepCount),
    Count is 1 + DepCount.

% ---- §68(b) applicable amount (only the unmarried case needed here) ----
applicable_amount_68(unmarried, 250000). % §68(b)(1)(C)

% ---- §151(d)(3) phaseout of exemptions ----
exemption_phaseout_percent(Taxpayer, Year, Percent) :-
    adjusted_gross_income(Taxpayer, Year, AGI),
    filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, Status),
    applicable_amount_68(Status, Applicable),
    Excess is AGI - Applicable,
    max0(Excess, ExcessPos),
    ( ExcessPos =:= 0 ->
        Percent = 0
    ;
        ceiling_div(ExcessPos, 2500, Increments),  % §151(d)(3)(B)
        P0 is 2 * Increments,
        min(P0, 100, Percent)
    ).

personal_exemptions_allowed_cents(Taxpayer, Year, AllowedCents) :-
    personal_exemption_count(Taxpayer, Year, Count),
    exemption_amount(Year, ExAmtDollars),
    TotalDollars is Count * ExAmtDollars,
    to_cents(TotalDollars, TotalCents),
    exemption_phaseout_percent(Taxpayer, Year, Percent),
    AllowedCents is (TotalCents * (100 - Percent)) // 100.

% ---- §63 standard deduction (basic only; no aged/blind facts given) ----
basic_standard_deduction(unmarried, Year, 12000) :- Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025, !. % §63(c)(7)(ii)
basic_standard_deduction(unmarried, _Year, 3000).                                  % §63(c)(2)(C)

standard_deduction_cents(Taxpayer, Year, SDCents) :-
    takes_standard_deduction(Taxpayer, Year),
    filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, unmarried),
    basic_standard_deduction(unmarried, Year, SDDollars),
    to_cents(SDDollars, SDCents).

% ---- Filing status (only what is needed for this case) ----
filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, unmarried) :-
    not_married(Taxpayer, Year),
    \+ surviving_spouse(Taxpayer, Year),
    \+ head_of_household(Taxpayer, Year).

surviving_spouse(_,_) :- fail.
head_of_household(_,_) :- fail.

% ---- Income definitions ----
adjusted_gross_income(Person, Year, AGI) :-
    gross_income(Person, Year, AGI).  % no above-the-line deductions given

taxable_income_cents(Taxpayer, Year, TICents) :-
    adjusted_gross_income(Taxpayer, Year, AGI),
    to_cents(AGI, AGICents),
    standard_deduction_cents(Taxpayer, Year, SDCents),
    personal_exemptions_allowed_cents(Taxpayer, Year, ExCents),
    TICents is AGICents - SDCents - ExCents.

% ---- §1(c) tax for unmarried individuals ----
% tax_bracket(Status, LowerDollars, UpperDollarsOrInf, BaseTaxDollars, RateNum, RateDen).
tax_bracket(unmarried,       0,   22100,     0,   15, 100).
tax_bracket(unmarried,   22100,   53500,  3315,   28, 100).
tax_bracket(unmarried,   53500,  115000, 12107,   31, 100).
tax_bracket(unmarried,  115000,  250000, 31172,   36, 100).
tax_bracket(unmarried,  250000,     inf, 79772,  396,1000). % 39.6%

in_bracket(TI, LowerC, inf) :- TI >= LowerC, !.
in_bracket(TI, LowerC, UpperC) :- TI >= LowerC, TI =< UpperC.

tax_cents(Taxpayer, Year, TaxCents) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, Status),
    taxable_income_cents(Taxpayer, Year, TI),
    tax_bracket(Status, LowerD, UpperD, BaseD, RateNum, RateDen),
    to_cents(LowerD, LowerC),
    ( UpperD == inf -> UpperC = inf ; to_cents(UpperD, UpperC) ),
    in_bracket(TI, LowerC, UpperC),
    to_cents(BaseD, BaseC),
    ExcessCents is TI - LowerC,
    AddCents is (ExcessCents * RateNum) // RateDen,
    TaxCents is BaseC + AddCents,
    !.

% =========================
% Case facts
% =========================
parent("Bob","Alice").
parent("Bob","Charlie").

income("Bob", 2015, 0).
gross_income("Alice", 2015, 264215).

marital_status("Alice", 2015, not_married).
takes_standard_deduction("Alice", 2015).

% =========================
% Query (compute and print tax)
% =========================
:- tax_cents("Alice", 2015, TaxCents),
   Dollars is TaxCents // 100,
   Cents is TaxCents mod 100,
   format('Tax result: ~w.~|~`0t~w~2+~n', [Dollars, Cents]).
:- halt.

text: In 2017, Alice's gross income was $326332. Alice and Bob have been married since Feb 3rd, 2017, and have had the same principal place of abode since 2015. Alice was born March 2nd, 1950 and Bob was born March 3rd, 1955. Alice and Bob file separately in 2017. Bob has no gross income that year. Alice takes the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ---------- Facts describing the case ---------- */
dob('Alice',date(1950,3,2)).
dob('Bob',  date(1955,3,3)).

spouse_of('Alice','Bob').
spouse_of('Bob','Alice').

gross_income('Alice',2017,326332).
gross_income('Bob',  2017,0).

filing_status('Alice',2017,married_filing_separately).
filing_status('Bob',  2017,married_filing_separately).

/* neither spouse itemizes deductions */
itemizes('Alice',2017,false).
itemizes('Bob',  2017,false).

/* ---------- Generic auxiliary predicates ---------- */
age(Person,Year,Age):-
    dob(Person,date(BY,_,_)),
    Age is Year-BY.

/* ---------- Standard deduction (§63) ---------- */
basic_standard_deduction(married_filing_separately,3000).
basic_standard_deduction(head_of_household,4400).
basic_standard_deduction(joint,6000).
basic_standard_deduction(single,3000).

additional_standard_deduction(Person,Year,600):-
    age(Person,Year,Age),
    Age>=65,!.                        /* aged (not blind case) */
additional_standard_deduction(_,_,0).

standard_deduction(Person,Year,0):-              /* §63(c)(6)(A) */
    filing_status(Person,Year,married_filing_separately),
    spouse_of(Person,Sp),
    itemizes(Sp,Year,true),!.
standard_deduction(Person,Year,SD):-
    filing_status(Person,Year,Status),
    basic_standard_deduction(Status,Base),
    additional_standard_deduction(Person,Year,Add),
    SD is Base+Add.

/* ---------- Personal exemptions (§151) ---------- */
exemption_amount(2000).

additional_spouse_exemption(Person,Year,1):-
    spouse_of(Person,Sp),
    gross_income(Sp,Year,G), G=:=0,            /* spouse has no gross income */
    !.
additional_spouse_exemption(_,_,0).

num_exemptions(Person,Year,N):-
    additional_spouse_exemption(Person,Year,SpN),
    N is 1+SpN.                                /* taxpayer + (possible) spouse */

applicable_amount(Year,married_filing_separately,150000).  /* §68(b)(1)(D) */
phaseout_increment(married_filing_separately,1250).        /* §151(d)(3)(B) */

phaseout_percent(AGI,App,Inc,Percent):-
    AGI=<App, Percent=0,!;
    Excess is AGI-App,
    Steps  is ceil(Excess/Inc),
    P0     is Steps*2,
    (P0>100 -> Percent=100 ; Percent=P0).

exemption_allowed(Person,Year,Allowed):-
    gross_income(Person,Year,AGI),
    filing_status(Person,Year,FS),
    applicable_amount(Year,FS,App),
    phaseout_increment(FS,Inc),
    phaseout_percent(AGI,App,Inc,Percent),
    num_exemptions(Person,Year,N),
    exemption_amount(EA),
    Total is N*EA,
    Allowed is Total*(100-Percent)/100.

/* ---------- Taxable income (§63) ---------- */
adjusted_gross_income(Person,Year,AGI):- gross_income(Person,Year,AGI).

taxable_income(Person,Year,TI):-
    adjusted_gross_income(Person,Year,AGI),
    standard_deduction(Person,Year,SD),
    exemption_allowed(Person,Year,EX),
    TI is AGI-SD-EX.

/* ---------- Tax computation for married filing separately (§1(d)) ---------- */
tax_mfs(I,T):- I=<18450, T is 0.15*I, !.
tax_mfs(I,T):- I=<44575, T is 2767.5 + 0.28*(I-18450), !.
tax_mfs(I,T):- I=<70000, T is 10082.5 + 0.31*(I-44575), !.
tax_mfs(I,T):- I=<125000, T is 17964.25 + 0.36*(I-70000), !.
tax_mfs(I,T):-                T is 37764.25 + 0.396*(I-125000).

tax(Person,Year,Tax):-
    filing_status(Person,Year,married_filing_separately),
    taxable_income(Person,Year,TI),
    tax_mfs(TI,TaxRaw),
    Tax is round(TaxRaw*100)/100.     /* round to cents */

/* ---------- Query ---------- */
:- tax('Alice',2017,Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n',[Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice got married on October 9th, 2016. Alice files a joint return with her spouse for 2017. Alice's and her spouse's gross income for the year 2017 is $42876. They take the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:

/* ----------  Statutory rules we need for this problem ---------- */

/* personal exemption amount for 2017 (§151(d)(1)) */
personal_exemption_amount(2017, 2000).

/* standard deduction basic amounts for 2017 (§63(c)(2))           */
standard_deduction_amount(2017, married_joint, 6000).   % 200% of $3,000
standard_deduction_amount(2017, head_of_household, 4400).
standard_deduction_amount(2017, single, 3000).

/* tax-rate schedule for married filing joint & surviving spouse (§1(a)) */
tax_bracket_joint(TI, Tax) :-                 TI =< 36900,  Tax is 0.15 * TI.
tax_bracket_joint(TI, Tax) :- TI >  36900, TI =<  89150,  Tax is  5535  + 0.28*(TI-36900).
tax_bracket_joint(TI, Tax) :- TI >  89150, TI =< 140000,  Tax is 20165  + 0.31*(TI-89150).
tax_bracket_joint(TI, Tax) :- TI > 140000, TI =< 250000,  Tax is 35928.5+ 0.36*(TI-140000).
tax_bracket_joint(TI, Tax) :- TI > 250000,                    Tax is 75528.5+ 0.396*(TI-250000).

status(alice, 2017, married_joint).               % filed a joint return
number_of_personal_exemptions(alice, 2017, 2).    % Alice + spouse
dependency_count(alice, 2017, 0).                 % no other dependents

/* ----------  Derived calculations ----------------------- */

adjusted_gross_income(P, Y, AGI) :-
    gross_income(P, Y, AGI).                      % no above-the-line adjustments

standard_deduction(P, Y, SD) :-
    status(P, Y, S),
    standard_deduction_amount(Y, S, SD).

personal_exemptions(P, Y, PE) :-
    number_of_personal_exemptions(P, Y, N),
    personal_exemption_amount(Y, EA),
    PE is N * EA.

taxable_income(P, Y, TI) :-
    adjusted_gross_income(P, Y, AGI),
    standard_deduction(P, Y, SD),
    personal_exemptions(P, Y, PE),
    TI is AGI - SD - PE.

income_tax(P, Y, Tax) :-
    status(P, Y, married_joint),
    taxable_income(P, Y, TI),
    tax_bracket_joint(TI, Tax).

/* public predicate */
tax(Person, Year, Tax) :- income_tax(Person, Year, Tax).

/* ----------  Query -------------------------------------- */
:- tax(alice, 2017, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

In 2017, Alice maintains as her principal place of abode a house where her mother Dorothy lives. Alice's gross income for the year 2017 is $197407. Alice takes the standard deduction.
