/* ----------  Statutory rules (simplified to those relevant) ---------- */

/* personal-exemption amount (§151(d)) */
exemption_amount(Year,0)     :- Year>=2018, Year=<2025, !.   /* special 2018-2025 rule */
exemption_amount(_,2000).                               /* otherwise $2,000 */

/* standard deduction (§63(c)(2) & (7)) – 2017 figures only */
standard_deduction(2017,head_of_household,4400).
standard_deduction(2017,unmarried,        3000).
standard_deduction(2017,surviving_spouse, 6000).

/* ----------  Facts from the case  ---------- */
gross_income("Alice",2017,197407).

/* family & dependency facts */
marital_status("Alice",2017,unmarried).                 /* no spouse mentioned */
relationship("Dorothy","Alice",mother).
income_of("Dorothy",2017,0).
principal_abode("Alice","Dorothy",2017).                /* Dorothy lives with Alice */

/* employment facts for FUTA (§3301, §3306) */
american_employer("Alice").
citizen("Bob",usa).
wages_paid("Alice","Bob",2017,23200).                   /* agricultural labour */
agricultural_labor("Bob",2017).

/* ----------  Dependency test (§152(d)) ---------- */
dependent(Taxpayer,Dep,Year) :-
    relationship(Dep,Taxpayer,mother),                  /* §152(d)(2)(C) */
    income_of(Dep,Year,Income), Income =:= 0.           /* §152(d)(1)(B) */

/* ----------  Filing-status determination (§2(b)) ---------- */
filing_status(Person,Year,head_of_household) :-          /* meets HoH requirements */
    marital_status(Person,Year,unmarried),
    principal_abode(Person,Dep,Year),
    dependent(Person,Dep,Year), !.
filing_status(Person,Year,unmarried) :-
    marital_status(Person,Year,unmarried).

/* ----------  Exemption count ---------- */
exemption_count(Person,Year,Count) :-
    findall(Dep,dependent(Person,Dep,Year),Deps),
    length(Deps,DepCnt),
    Count is DepCnt + 1.                                /* +1 for the taxpayer */

/* ----------  Taxable income (§63) ---------- */
taxable_income(Person,Year,TI) :-
    gross_income(Person,Year,GI),
    filing_status(Person,Year,Status),
    standard_deduction(Year,Status,StdDed),
    exemption_amount(Year,ExAmt),
    exemption_count(Person,Year,ExCnt),
    ExTotal is ExAmt * ExCnt,
    TI is GI - StdDed - ExTotal.

/* ----------  Income-tax computation for heads of household (§1(b)) ---------- */
head_of_household_tax(TI,Tax) :- TI =< 29600,
    Tax is 0.15 * TI.
head_of_household_tax(TI,Tax) :- TI > 29600, TI =< 76400,
    Tax is 4440 + 0.28 * (TI - 29600).
head_of_household_tax(TI,Tax) :- TI > 76400, TI =< 127500,
    Tax is 17544 + 0.31 * (TI - 76400).
head_of_household_tax(TI,Tax) :- TI > 127500, TI =< 250000,
    Tax is 33385 + 0.36 * (TI - 127500).
head_of_household_tax(TI,Tax) :- TI > 250000,
    Tax is 77485 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000).

income_tax(Person,Year,Tax) :-
    filing_status(Person,Year,head_of_household),
    taxable_income(Person,Year,TI),
    head_of_household_tax(TI,Tax).

/* ----------  FUTA tax (§3301 & §3306) ---------- */
futa_taxable_wages(_,_,_,0) :- !, fail.                  /* guard – no wages fact */

/* Only the first $7,000 of each employee’s wages are subject to FUTA (§3306(b)(1)) */
futa_taxable_wages(Employer,Employee,Year,Taxable) :-
    wages_paid(Employer,Employee,Year,W),
    ( W >= 7000 -> Taxable = 7000 ; Taxable = W ).

/* summation helper */
sum_list(L,S) :- sum_list(L,0,S).
sum_list([],S,S).
sum_list([H|T],A,S) :- A1 is A+H, sum_list(T,A1,S).

futa_tax(Employer,Year,Tax) :-
    findall(TW,futa_taxable_wages(Employer,_,Year,TW),List),
    sum_list(List,TaxableWageTotal),
    Tax is TaxableWageTotal * 0.06.                     /* 6 % rate (§3301) */

/* ----------  Total tax payable by a person ---------- */
tax(Person,Year,TotalTax) :-
    income_tax(Person,Year,IncomeTax),
    futa_tax(Person,Year,FutaTax),
    TotalTax is IncomeTax + FutaTax.

/* ----------  Query ---------- */
:- tax("Alice", 2017, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

text: Alice has paid $45252 to Bob for work done in the year 2017. In 2017, Alice has also paid $9832 into a retirement fund for Bob, and $5322 into health insurance for Bob. In 2017, Alice was paid $233200. Alice takes the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ===== Statutory rules we need for this problem ===== */

% ---------------- Auxiliary predicates ----------------
min(A,B,A):- A=<B.
min(A,B,B):- A>B.

sumlist([],0).
sumlist([H|T],Sum):- sumlist(T,Rest), Sum is H+Rest.

% ---------------- Facts from the case -----------------
gross_income("Alice", 2017, 233200).

marital_status("Alice", 2017, single).   % nothing in the facts indicates marriage

wages("Alice", "Bob", 2017, 45252).      % cash wages
retirement_contribution("Alice", "Bob", 2017, 9832).   % retirement fund
health_insurance("Alice", "Bob", 2017, 5322).          % excluded from FUTA wages

% ---------------- Standard deduction ------------------
% 2017 is before the 2018–2025 increase, so single basic standard deduction = $3,000
standard_deduction(_Person, Year, 3000) :- Year =:= 2017.

% ---------------- Personal exemption ------------------
personal_exemption_amount(Year, 2000) :- Year =:= 2017.

% ---------------- Taxable income ----------------------
taxable_income(Person, Year, TaxableIncome) :-
    gross_income(Person,Year,GI),
    standard_deduction(Person,Year,SD),
    personal_exemption_amount(Year,PE),
    TaxableIncome is GI - SD - PE.

% ---------------- Income-tax brackets (single, 2017) --
income_tax_single(TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 22100 ->
        Tax is TI * 0.15
    ; TI =< 53500 ->
        Tax is 3315 + (TI-22100)*0.28
    ; TI =< 115000 ->
        Tax is 12107 + (TI-53500)*0.31
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        Tax is 31172 + (TI-115000)*0.36
    ;                               % above $250,000
        Tax is 79772 + (TI-250000)*0.396
    ).

% ---------------- FUTA (IRC §§3301, 3306) -------------
% taxable FUTA wages for one employee are capped at $7,000 and
% exclude health-insurance payments (§3306(b)(2)(A)).
futa_subject_wages(Employer, Employee, Year, Subject) :-
    wages(Employer,Employee,Year,W),
    retirement_contribution(Employer,Employee,Year,R),
    Total is W + R,                  % health insurance excluded
    min(Total, 7000, Subject).

futa_tax(Employer, Year, Tax) :-
    findall(S, futa_subject_wages(Employer,_,Year,S), List),
    sumlist(List, TaxableWages),
    Tax is TaxableWages * 0.06.      % 6 % rate (§3301)

% ---------------- Total tax owed ----------------------
tax(Person, Year, TotalTax) :-
    taxable_income(Person,Year,TI),
    marital_status(Person,Year,single),
    income_tax_single(TI,IncomeTax),
    futa_tax(Person,Year,FUTATax),
    TotalTax is IncomeTax + FUTATax.

% ---------------- Query requested by the problem ------
:- tax("Alice", 2017, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

standard_deduction_amount(Year, joint, 24000) :-

joint_bracket_tax(TI, Tax) :-

question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2019?

/* ----------  FACTS FROM THE CASE  ---------- */
gross_income('Alice', 2019, 236422).

% marital history
spouse_of('Alice', 'Bob').
married_on('Alice','Bob',date(1998,2,3)).
death('Bob', date(2017,1,1)).

% no remarriage after Bob’s death
remarried(_Person, _Year) :- fail.

% child and household information
dependent('Alice','Charlie',2019).
maintains_household('Alice','Charlie',2019).

/* ----------  AUXILIARY RULES  ---------- */
years_between(Year1, Year2, Diff) :- Diff is Year1 - Year2.

/* ----------  SURVIVING-SPOUSE TEST  ---------- */
surviving_spouse(Person, Year) :-
    spouse_of(Person, Spouse),
    death(Spouse, date(DeathYear,_,_)),
    years_between(Year, DeathYear, Diff),
    Diff >= 1, Diff =< 2,                        % spouse died in either of two preceding years
    \+ remarried(Person, Year),                  % has not remarried
    maintains_household(Person, Child, Year),
    dependent(Person, Child, Year).

/* ----------  FILING STATUS  ---------- */
filing_status(Person, Year, 'surviving_spouse') :- 
    surviving_spouse(Person, Year), !.

filing_status(Person, Year, 'head_of_household') :-
    % (Not needed for Alice but included for completeness)
    \+ surviving_spouse(Person, Year),
    maintains_household(Person, Dep, Year),
    dependent(Person, Dep, Year), !.

filing_status(_Person, _Year, 'single').

/* ----------  STANDARD DEDUCTION (2018-2025 amounts) ---------- */
standard_deduction('surviving_spouse', 24000).
standard_deduction('head_of_household', 18000).
standard_deduction('single', 12000).

/* ----------  TAX-RATE SCHEDULES ---------- */
tax_compute('surviving_spouse', TI, Tax) :-   % same as married-joint brackets
    ( TI =<  36900 -> Tax is 0.15    * TI
    ; TI =<  89150 -> Tax is 5535      + 0.28 * (TI - 36900)
    ; TI =< 140000 -> Tax is 20165     + 0.31 * (TI - 89150)
    ; TI =< 250000 -> Tax is 35928.5   + 0.36 * (TI - 140000)
    ;                  Tax is 75528.5   + 0.396* (TI - 250000)
    ).

/* ----------  MAIN TAX PREDICATE  ---------- */
tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    gross_income(Person, Year, GI),
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    standard_deduction(Status, SD),
    TI is GI - SD,
    tax_compute(Status, TI, Tax).

/* ----------  QUERY  ---------- */
:- tax('Alice', 2019, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

text: Alice's gross income in 2015 is $395276. Alice is allowed itemized deductions of $4571, $1973 and $15271.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2015?
reference_prolog:
/* ----------  Statutory rules needed for this problem  ---------- */

/* filing-status applicable-amount for §68 and §151(d)(3) */
applicable_amount(single,                 250000).
applicable_amount(head_of_household,      275000).
applicable_amount(joint_or_surviving,     300000).
applicable_amount(married_separate,       150000).

/* ---- helper: sum a list ---- */
sum_list([],0).
sum_list([H|T],S) :- sum_list(T,R), S is R+H.

/* ---- facts for Alice, 2015 ---- */
gross_income(alice,2015,395276).

itemized(alice,2015,4571).
itemized(alice,2015,1973).
itemized(alice,2015,15271).

status(alice,2015,single).   /* not married, not HoH, not surviving */

/* ---- AGI  (no above-the-line deductions supplied) ---- */
agi(Person,Year,AGI) :- gross_income(Person,Year,AGI).

/* ---- total itemized deductions reported ---- */
itemized_total(Person,Year,Tot) :-
        findall(Amt,itemized(Person,Year,Amt),Ls),
        sum_list(Ls,Tot).

/* ---- §68 overall limitation on itemized deductions ---- */
allowed_itemized(Person,Year,Allowed) :-
        agi(Person,Year,AGI),
        status(Person,Year,Stat),
        applicable_amount(Stat,Thresh),
        itemized_total(Person,Year,Item),
        (   AGI =< Thresh
        ->  Allowed = Item
        ;   Excess    is AGI - Thresh,
            Red1      is 0.03*Excess,
            Red2      is 0.80*Item,
            (Red1 < Red2 -> Red is Red1 ; Red is Red2),
            Allowed   is Item - Red
        ).

/* ---- §151 personal exemption phase-out ---- */
base_exemption_amount(2000).     /* year 2015 amount */

personal_exemption(Person,Year,Exempt) :-
        agi(Person,Year,AGI),
        status(Person,Year,Stat),
        applicable_amount(Stat,Thresh),
        base_exemption_amount(Base),
        Units = 1,        /* one exemption for the taxpayer only */
        (   AGI =< Thresh
        ->  Exempt is Units*Base
        ;   Excess     is AGI - Thresh,
            Incr       is ((Excess + 2499)//2500),   /* ceiling div */
            Perc       is Incr*2,                    /* 2-percent steps */
            (Perc >= 100 -> RedPct = 100 ; RedPct = Perc),
            Exempt0    is Units*Base,
            Exempt     is Exempt0*(100-RedPct)/100
        ).

/* ---- taxable income (§63(a)) ---- */
taxable_income(Person,Year,TI) :-
        gross_income(Person,Year,Gross),
        allowed_itemized(Person,Year,ItemOK),
        personal_exemption(Person,Year,Exempt),
        TI is Gross - ItemOK - Exempt.

/* ---- §1(c) rate schedule for unmarried individuals ---- */
single_tax(TI,Tax) :-
        ( TI =< 22100 ->
              Tax is 0.15*TI
        ; TI =< 53500 ->
              Tax is 3315 + 0.28*(TI-22100)
        ; TI =< 115000 ->
              Tax is 12107 + 0.31*(TI-53500)
        ; TI =< 250000 ->
              Tax is 31172 + 0.36*(TI-115000)
        ;      Tax is 79772 + 0.396*(TI-250000)
        ).

/* ---- master predicate ---- */
tax(Person,Year,Tax) :-
        status(Person,Year,single),          /* only status needed here */
        taxable_income(Person,Year,TI),
        single_tax(TI,Tax).

/* ----------------  query ---------------- */
:- tax(alice, 2015, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

text: Alice got married on Feb 29, 2000. Alice files a joint return with her spouse for 2017. Alice's gross income for the year 2017 is $22895, and her spouse's is $14257. They take the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ----------  STATUTORY RULES  ---------- */

/* personal exemption amount (§151(d)(1) and (5))  */
personal_exemption_amount(Year, 0) :-                    % 2018-2025
    Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025, !.
personal_exemption_amount(_Year, 2000).                  % otherwise

/* standard deduction, basic amount only (§63(c)(2)) */
basic_standard_deduction(Year, married_filing_joint, 6000) :-
    Year =< 2017.    % 2 × 3,000 (special larger amounts start in 2018)
basic_standard_deduction(Year, head_of_household, 4400)  :- Year =< 2017.
basic_standard_deduction(Year, single, 3000)             :- Year =< 2017.

/* ---- income aggregation for a married joint return ---- */
adjusted_gross_income(Person, Year, AGI) :-
    filing_status(Person, Year, married_filing_joint),
    makes_joint_return(Person, Year, Spouse),
    gross_income(Person, Year, GI1),
    gross_income(Spouse, Year, GI2),
    AGI is GI1 + GI2.

/* deductions when standard deduction is claimed (§63(b)) */
total_deductions(Person, Year, Total) :-
    filing_status(Person, Year, married_filing_joint),
    basic_standard_deduction(Year, married_filing_joint, SD),
    personal_exemption_amount(Year, EA),
    Exemptions is 2 * EA,          % taxpayer + spouse (§151(b))
    Total is SD + Exemptions.

/* taxable income (§63(b)) */
taxable_income(Person, Year, TI) :-
    adjusted_gross_income(Person, Year, AGI),
    total_deductions(Person, Year, Deductions),
    TI0 is AGI - Deductions,
    ( TI0 > 0 -> TI = TI0 ; TI = 0 ).

/* §1(a) tax table – married filing joint & surviving spouse */
tax_bracket(married_filing_joint, 0,      36900,  0,       0.15).
tax_bracket(married_filing_joint, 36900,  89150,  5535,    0.28).
tax_bracket(married_filing_joint, 89150, 140000, 20165,    0.31).
tax_bracket(married_filing_joint,140000, 250000, 35928.5,  0.36).
tax_bracket(married_filing_joint,250000, inf,    75528.5,  0.396).

/* locate proper bracket */
choose_bracket(Status, TI, L, U, Base, Rate) :-
    tax_bracket(Status, L, U, Base, Rate),
    ( U == inf -> true ; TI =< U ),       % 'inf' means no upper bound
    TI >= L, !.

/* compute tax from bracket */
tax_from_bracket(TI, L, Base, Rate, Tax) :-
    Tax is Base + Rate * (TI - L).

/* main tax predicate */
tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
    choose_bracket(Status, TI, L, _U, Base, Rate),
    tax_from_bracket(TI, L, Base, Rate, Tax).

makes_joint_return("Alice", 2017, "Alice_spouse").

/* ----------  QUERY  ---------- */

:- halt.

Alice's gross income for the year 2017 is $103272 while her spouse had no income. Alice has paid $3200 to her brother Bob for work done from Feb 1st, 2017 to Sep 2nd, 2017, in Baltimore, Maryland, USA.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
/* =========  STATUTORY RULES  ========= */

/* --- standard deduction (§63(c)) --- */
basic_standard_deduction(Year,joint,6000)  :- Year < 2018.            % §63(c)(2)(A)
basic_standard_deduction(Year,head,4400)   :- Year < 2018.            % §63(c)(2)(B)
basic_standard_deduction(Year,other,3000)  :- Year < 2018.            % §63(c)(2)(C)

/* --- exemption amount (§151(d)) --- */
exemption_amount(Year,0)     :- Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025.           % §151(d)(5)
exemption_amount(Year,2000)  :- Year < 2018 ; Year > 2025.

/* --- personal exemptions allowed on a joint return (§151(b)) --- */
personal_exemptions(TaxP,Year,Amount) :-
    filing_status(TaxP,Year,joint),
    exemption_amount(Year,E),
    Amount is 2*E.           % one for each spouse

/* --- taxable income (§63) --- */
taxable_income(TaxP,Year,TI) :-
    gross_income(TaxP,Year,GI),
    filing_status(TaxP,Year,Status),
    basic_standard_deduction(Year,Status,SD),
    personal_exemptions(TaxP,Year,PE),
    TI is GI - SD - PE.

/* --- income-tax tables for married-filing-joint (§1(a)) --- */
income_tax(TI,Tax) :- TI =< 36900,   Tax is 0.15*TI.
income_tax(TI,Tax) :- TI =< 89150,   Tax is 5535  + 0.28*(TI-36900).
income_tax(TI,Tax) :- TI =< 140000,  Tax is 20165 + 0.31*(TI-89150).
income_tax(TI,Tax) :- TI =< 250000,  Tax is 35928.50 + 0.36*(TI-140000).
income_tax(TI,Tax) :- TI  > 250000,  Tax is 75528.50 + 0.396*(TI-250000).

/* --- FUTA (employer’s) tax (§3301, §3306) --- */
futa_subject_wages(TaxP,Year,Sum) :-
    findall( W
           , (wages_paid(TaxP,Year,_,W), W1 is min(W,7000))
           , List),
    sum_list(List,Sum).

futa_tax(TaxP,Year,Futa) :-
    futa_subject_wages(TaxP,Year,Sum),
    Sum >= 0,                         % person is an employer once >$1,500 wages (§3306(a)(1)(A))
    Futa is 0.06*Sum.

/* --- total tax owed --- */
tax(TaxP,Year,TotalTax) :-
    taxable_income(TaxP,Year,TI),
    income_tax(TI,IncomeTax),
    futa_tax(TaxP,Year,FutaTax),
    TotalTax is IncomeTax + FutaTax.

/* =========  CASE FACTS  ========= */

% Spouse’s income is zero, already included in gross income figure.

filing_status("Alice",2017,joint).

% Alice paid her brother Bob $3,200 for work in 2017
wages_paid("Alice",2017,"Bob",3200).


/* =========  QUERY  ========= */

text: Alice married Bob on May 29th, 2008. Their son Charlie was born October 4th, 2004. Bob died October 22nd, 2016. Alice's gross income for the year 2016 was $113580. In 2017, Alice's gross income was $567192. In 2017, Alice and Charlie lived in a house maintained by Alice. That same year, Alice is allowed a deduction of $59850 for donating cash to a charity.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
% Facts from the case
person(alice).
person(bob).
person(charlie).

spouse(alice,bob).
death_year(bob,2016).
not_remarried(alice,2017).
joint_return_possible(alice,bob,2016).

gross_income(alice,2017,567192).
itemized_deduction(alice,2017,59850).

birth_year(alice,1980).
birth_year(charlie,2004).

child_of(charlie,alice).
lives_with(alice,charlie,2017).
maintains_household(alice,2017).

% Helper predicates
age(Person,Year,Age) :- birth_year(Person,BY), Age is Year - BY.
younger_than(Parent,Child) :- birth_year(Child,BC), birth_year(Parent,BP), BC > BP.

% Qualifying child and dependent
qualifying_child(T,C,Y) :-
    child_of(C,T),
    lives_with(T,C,Y),
    age(C,Y,Age),
    Age < 25,
    younger_than(T,C).

dependent(T,D,Y) :- qualifying_child(T,D,Y).

% Surviving spouse definition
surviving_spouse(T,Y) :-
    spouse(T,S),
    death_year(S,DY),
    Diff is Y - DY,
    Diff >= 1, Diff =< 2,
    not_remarried(T,Y),
    maintains_household(T,Y),
    dependent(T,Child,Y),
    child_of(Child,T),
    joint_return_possible(T,S,DY).

% Filing status
filing_status(T,Y,surviving_spouse) :- surviving_spouse(T,Y), !.
filing_status(_,_,unmarried).

% Adjusted gross income
agi(T,Y,AGI) :- gross_income(T,Y,AGI).

% Applicable amount for section 68
applicable_amount(_,_,surviving_spouse,300000).
applicable_amount(_,_,head_of_household,275000).
applicable_amount(_,_,unmarried,250000).
applicable_amount(_,_,married_separate,150000).

applicable_amount(T,Y,Amount) :-
    filing_status(T,Y,Status),
    applicable_amount(T,Y,Status,Amount).

% Standard deduction
basic_standard_deduction(surviving_spouse,Year,Amount) :-
    (Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025 -> Amount is 24000 ; Amount is 6000).
basic_standard_deduction(head_of_household,Year,Amount) :-
    (Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025 -> Amount is 18000 ; Amount is 4400).
basic_standard_deduction(unmarried,Year,Amount) :-
    (Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025 -> Amount is 12000 ; Amount is 3000).

additional_standard_deduction(_,_,0).

standard_deduction(T,Y,Amount) :-
    filing_status(T,Y,Status),
    basic_standard_deduction(Status,Y,Basic),
    additional_standard_deduction(T,Y,Add),
    Amount is Basic + Add.

% Itemized deduction with section 68 limitation
min_value(A,B,Min) :- (A =< B -> Min = A ; Min = B).

itemized_deductions(T,Y,Allowed) :-
    ( itemized_deduction(T,Y,Amount) ->
        ( (Y >= 2018, Y =< 2025) ->
            Allowed = Amount
        ;
            agi(T,Y,AGI),
            applicable_amount(T,Y,Appl),
            (AGI =< Appl ->
                Allowed = Amount
            ;
                Excess is AGI - Appl,
                Red1 is 0.03 * Excess,
                Red2 is 0.8 * Amount,
                min_value(Red1,Red2,Red),
                Allowed is Amount - Red
            )
        )
    ; Allowed = 0 ).

% Personal exemptions
num_exemptions(T,Y,N) :-
    findall(D, dependent(T,D,Y), Ds),
    length(Ds,ND),
    N is 1 + ND.

exemption_amount_per(T,Y,Per) :-
    (Y >= 2018, Y =< 2025 ->
        Per = 0
    ;
        Base = 2000,
        agi(T,Y,AGI),
        applicable_amount(T,Y,Appl),
        (AGI =< Appl ->
            Per = Base
        ;
            Excess is AGI - Appl,
            Steps is ceiling(Excess / 2500),
            Percent is Steps * 0.02,
            (Percent >= 1 -> Per = 0 ; Per is Base * (1 - Percent))
        )
    ).

personal_exemptions(T,Y,Total) :-
    num_exemptions(T,Y,N),
    exemption_amount_per(T,Y,Per),
    Total is N * Per.

% Taxable income
deduction(T,Y,Ded) :-
    standard_deduction(T,Y,SD),
    itemized_deductions(T,Y,ID),
    (ID > SD -> Ded = ID ; Ded = SD).

taxable_income(T,Y,TI) :-
    agi(T,Y,AGI),
    deduction(T,Y,Ded),
    personal_exemptions(T,Y,Ex),
    TI is AGI - Ded - Ex.

% Tax calculation for surviving spouse
tax_by_status(surviving_spouse,TI,Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 36900 -> Tax is TI * 0.15
    ; TI =< 89150 -> Tax is 5535 + 0.28 * (TI - 36900)
    ; TI =< 140000 -> Tax is 20165 + 0.31 * (TI - 89150)
    ; TI =< 250000 -> Tax is 35928.50 + 0.36 * (TI - 140000)
    ; Tax is 75528.50 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000)
    ).

% Main tax predicate
tax(T,Y,Tax) :-
    filing_status(T,Y,Status),
    taxable_income(T,Y,TI),
    tax_by_status(Status,TI,Tax).

:- tax(alice,2017,Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

text: Alice got married on Jan 6th, 2005. Alice files a joint return with her spouse for 2015. Alice's and her spouse's gross income for the year 2015 is $42876. Alice and her spouse take the standard deduction. Alice has a son, Bob, who has the same principal place of abode as her in 2015. Bob has a son, Charlie, who also has the same principal place of abode as his father in 2015.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2015?
reference_prolog:
% Facts from the case
person(alice).
person(spouse1).
person(bob).
person(charlie).

married(alice, spouse1, 2015).
joint_return(alice, spouse1, 2015).
gross_income(alice, 2015, 42876).

% Relationships
child(bob, alice).
child(charlie, bob).

% Ages (assumed for qualifying child requirements)
age(alice, 2015, 40).
age(bob, 2015, 20).
age(charlie, 2015, 2).

% Principal place of abode facts
base_same_abode(alice, bob, 2015).
base_same_abode(bob, charlie, 2015).

% Derived rules
descendant(Desc, Person) :- child(Desc, Person).
descendant(Desc, Person) :- child(Desc, Inter), descendant(Inter, Person).

same_abode(A, B, Y) :- base_same_abode(A, B, Y).
same_abode(A, C, Y) :- base_same_abode(A, B, Y), base_same_abode(B, C, Y).

younger_than(Child, Taxpayer, Year) :-
    age(Child, Year, A1),
    age(Taxpayer, Year, A2),
    A1 < A2.

age_requirement(Child, Taxpayer, Year) :-
    age(Child, Year, Age),
    Age < 25,
    younger_than(Child, Taxpayer, Year).

qualifying_child(Taxpayer, Child, Year) :-
    descendant(Child, Taxpayer),
    same_abode(Taxpayer, Child, Year),
    age_requirement(Child, Taxpayer, Year).

dependent(Taxpayer, Dep, Year) :-
    qualifying_child(Taxpayer, Dep, Year).

dependents_count(Taxpayer, Year, Count) :-
    findall(D, dependent(Taxpayer, D, Year), L),
    sort(L, SL),
    length(SL, Count).

% Exemption amount
exemption_amount(Year, 0) :- Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025.
exemption_amount(Year, 2000) :- Year < 2018.

num_exemptions(Taxpayer, Year, Num) :-
    joint_return(Taxpayer, _, Year),
    dependents_count(Taxpayer, Year, Dep),
    Num is 2 + Dep.
num_exemptions(Taxpayer, Year, Num) :-
    \+ joint_return(Taxpayer, _, Year),
    dependents_count(Taxpayer, Year, Dep),
    Num is 1 + Dep.

total_exemptions(Taxpayer, Year, Total) :-
    exemption_amount(Year, Ex),
    num_exemptions(Taxpayer, Year, Num),
    Total is Ex * Num.

% Standard deduction
base_amount_c(Year, 12000) :- Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025.
base_amount_c(Year, 3000) :- Year < 2018.

standard_deduction(Taxpayer, Year, SD) :-
    joint_return(Taxpayer, _, Year),
    base_amount_c(Year, C),
    SD is 2 * C.

adjusted_gross_income(Taxpayer, Year, AGI) :-
    gross_income(Taxpayer, Year, AGI).

taxable_income(Taxpayer, Year, TI) :-
    adjusted_gross_income(Taxpayer, Year, AGI),
    standard_deduction(Taxpayer, Year, SD),
    total_exemptions(Taxpayer, Year, Ex),
    TI is AGI - SD - Ex.

% Tax computation for joint return
tax_joint(TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 36900 -> Tax is 0.15 * TI
    ; TI =< 89150 -> Tax is 5535 + 0.28 * (TI - 36900)
    ; TI =< 140000 -> Tax is 20165 + 0.31 * (TI - 89150)
    ; TI =< 250000 -> Tax is 35928.5 + 0.36 * (TI - 140000)
    ; Tax is 75528.5 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000)
    ).

tax(Taxpayer, Year, Tax) :-
    taxable_income(Taxpayer, Year, TI),
    tax_joint(TI, Tax).

:- tax(alice, 2015, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

text: Bob, Alice's father, has the same principal place of abode as Alice since 2012, and has had no income since. Alice's gross income for the year 2015 is $102268. Alice takes the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2015?
reference_prolog:
/* ─────────────────────────  STATUTORY RULES  ───────────────────────── */
/* This program covers just enough of the Code excerpts to answer:
   "How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2015?" given:

   - Alice has gross income $102,268 in 2015
   - Alice takes the standard deduction
   - Bob is Alice's father, same principal abode since 2012, and has no income
*/

/* ---------- Basic helpers ---------- */

sumlist([],Acc,Acc).
sumlist([H|T],Acc0,Sum) :-
    Acc1 is Acc0 + H,
    sumlist(T,Acc1,Sum).

/* ---------- Case‑level person/year facts ---------- */

person(alice).
person(bob).

year(2015).

/* Family relationship: Bob is Alice's father */
father(bob, alice).

/* Shared principal place of abode for more than half the year 2015 */
same_principal_abode(Person1, Person2, 2015) :-
    (Person1 = alice, Person2 = bob);
    (Person1 = bob,   Person2 = alice).

/* Gross income facts (from the case) */
gross_income(alice, 2015, 102268).
gross_income(bob,   2015, 0).

/* Standard deduction is elected (i.e., no itemizing) */
takes_standard_deduction(alice, 2015).

/* No fact states that Alice is married, a surviving spouse, or HoH,
   so default rules below will determine status as single.            */
married_status(_P, _Y) :- fail.
surviving_spouse_status(_P, _Y) :- fail.

/* ---------- Dependency rules, §152 and §151 ---------- */

/* Determine if B is a qualifying relative of A in Year */
/* §152(d)(1): relationship + zero income + not qualifying child */

% Relationship test, §152(d)(2)
relationship_qualifying_relative(A, B) :-
    father(B, A);        % B is father of A
    father(A, B);        % A is father of B
    fail.

% Bob is Alice's father ⇒ Bob bears qualifying relationship to Alice
relationship_qualifying_relative(alice, bob).

% Age / child status is irrelevant here because Bob obviously is not a child.
qualifying_child(_Taxpayer, _Child, _Year) :- fail.

% income test: "has no income" == 0 for the calendar year
no_income(Person, Year) :-
    gross_income(Person, Year, GI),
    GI =:= 0.

qualifying_relative(Taxpayer, Dep, Year) :-
    relationship_qualifying_relative(Taxpayer, Dep),
    no_income(Dep, Year),
    \+ qualifying_child(_Someone, Dep, Year).

/* Dependent definition, §152(a) */
dependent(Taxpayer, Dep, Year) :-
    qualifying_child(Taxpayer, Dep, Year);
    qualifying_relative(Taxpayer, Dep, Year).

/* Alice's specific dependent in 2015: Bob */
dependent_of_alice_2015(bob) :-
    qualifying_relative(alice, bob, 2015),
    dependent(alice, bob, 2015).

/* §151: exemption amount for 2015 is $2,000 (no 2018–2025 override yet) */
exemption_amount(Year, 0) :-
    Year >= 2018,
    Year =< 2025.
exemption_amount(Year, 2000) :-
    (Year < 2018 ; Year > 2025).

/* §151(c): one exemption amount per dependent */
personal_exemptions(Person, Year, Total) :-
    exemption_amount(Year, EA),
    findall(EA, dependent(Person, _Dep, Year), L),
    sumlist(L, 0, Total).

/* ---------- Filing status rules, §§2, 7703, 63 ---------- */

/* Determine marital status via §7703; here we only encode enough to
   conclude Alice is "not married" in 2015. */

not_married(Person, Year) :-
    \+ married_status(Person, Year).

/* Head of household test, §2(b) – we must check whether Alice might be HoH.
   Conditions (simplified to this case):

   - not married at close of year
   - not surviving spouse
   - maintains as home a household that is principal place of abode for
     more than half the year for a qualifying child or other dependent,
     AND
   - furnishes over half the cost of maintaining such household.

   We are not given cost facts, so we *cannot* prove she furnishes over
   half the cost. We therefore *do not* infer head_of_household.          */

maintains_household(_P, _Y) :- fail.  % no evidence in the case

head_of_household(Person, Year) :-
    not_married(Person, Year),
    \+ surviving_spouse_status(Person, Year),
    maintains_household(Person, Year),
    dependent(Person, Dep, Year),
    same_principal_abode(Person, Dep, Year),
    Dep \= Person.

/* Surviving spouse, §2(a) – not alleged in the case */
surviving_spouse(Person, Year) :-
    surviving_spouse_status(Person, Year).

/* Filing status classification used for §1 and §63 */
filing_status(Person, Year, surviving_spouse) :-
    surviving_spouse(Person, Year), !.

filing_status(Person, Year, head_of_household) :-
    head_of_household(Person, Year), !.

filing_status(Person, Year, single) :-
    not_married(Person, Year),
    \+ surviving_spouse(Person, Year),
    \+ head_of_household(Person, Year).

/* ---------- Standard deduction, §63(c) ---------- */

/* Basic standard deduction per status for 2015 (pre-TCJA amounts):
   §63(c)(2):
     - joint / surviving spouse: 200% of $3,000 = $6,000
     - head of household: $4,400
     - others: $3,000
*/
basic_standard_deduction(Year, surviving_spouse, Amount) :-
    Year < 2018,
    Amount is 6000.
basic_standard_deduction(Year, married_joint, Amount) :-
    Year < 2018,
    Amount is 6000.
basic_standard_deduction(Year, head_of_household, Amount) :-
    Year < 2018,
    Amount is 4400.
basic_standard_deduction(Year, single, Amount) :-
    Year < 2018,
    Amount is 3000.

%% For 2018-2025 rule change (not used for 2015 but kept consistent):
basic_standard_deduction(Year, head_of_household, 18000) :-
    Year >= 2018,
    Year =< 2025.
basic_standard_deduction(Year, single, 12000) :-
    Year >= 2018,
    Year =< 2025.
basic_standard_deduction(Year, surviving_spouse, 24000) :-
    Year >= 2018,
    Year =< 2025.
basic_standard_deduction(Year, married_joint, 24000) :-
    Year >= 2018,
    Year =< 2025.

additional_standard_deduction(_Person, _Year, 0).

standard_deduction(Person, Year, SD) :-
    takes_standard_deduction(Person, Year),
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    basic_standard_deduction(Year, Status, Base),
    additional_standard_deduction(Person, Year, Add),
    SD is Base + Add.

/* ---------- Taxable income, §63 ---------- */

adjusted_gross_income(Person, Year, AGI) :-
    gross_income(Person, Year, GI),
    AGI is GI.

taxable_income(Person, Year, TI) :-
    \+ takes_standard_deduction(Person, Year), !,
    adjusted_gross_income(Person, Year, TI0),
    TI is max(0, TI0).

taxable_income(Person, Year, TI) :-
    takes_standard_deduction(Person, Year),
    adjusted_gross_income(Person, Year, AGI),
    standard_deduction(Person, Year, SD),
    personal_exemptions(Person, Year, PE),
    TI0 is AGI - SD - PE,
    ( TI0 > 0 -> TI = TI0 ; TI = 0 ).

/* ---------- Income tax computation schedules, §1 ---------- */

/* We only need the "unmarried individuals (other than surviving spouses and
   heads of households)" schedule, §1(c), because Alice is single.        */

tax_single(TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 22100 ->
        Tax is 0.15 * TI
    ; TI =< 53500 ->
        Tax is 3315 + 0.28 * (TI - 22100)
    ; TI =< 115000 ->
        Tax is 12107 + 0.31 * (TI - 53500)
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        Tax is 31172 + 0.36 * (TI - 115000)
    ;   Tax is 79772 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000)
    ).

tax_for_status(single, TI, Tax) :-
    tax_single(TI, Tax).

/* ---------- Top-level tax predicate ---------- */

tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
    tax_for_status(Status, TI, Tax).

/* String-name alias, matching the examples’ query style */
tax("Alice", Year, Tax) :-
    tax(alice, Year, Tax).

/* ───────────────────────────────  QUERY  ─────────────────────────────── */

:- tax("Alice", 2015, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

text: Alice married Bob on August 25th, 2011. Alice files a joint return with her husband for 2017. Alice's and Bob's gross income for the year 2017 is $22895 and they take the standard deduction. Alice is 67 years old in 2017.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ---------- FACTS FOR THE CASE ---------- */
married(alice,bob,2011).

% joint return made by Alice for 2017
joint_return(alice,2017).

% gross income (combined, as stated in the problem)
gross_income(alice,2017,22895).

% ages in 2017
age(alice,2017,67).
age(bob,2017,60).

/* ---------- GENERIC TAX RULES (subset of the statutes) ---------- */

/* exemption amount (§151(d)(1) and (5)) */
exemption_amount(Year,0)  :- Year>=2018, Year=<2025, !.  % suspended 2018-2025
exemption_amount(_,2000).

/* personal-exemption count (joint vs. non-joint) */
personal_exemption_count(Person,Year,2) :- joint_return(Person,Year), !.
personal_exemption_count(_,_,1).

personal_exemption(Person,Year,Amount) :-
    exemption_amount(Year,EA),
    personal_exemption_count(Person,Year,Cnt),
    Amount is EA*Cnt.

/* basic standard deduction (§63(c)(2)) – pre-2018 numbers only   */
basic_standard_deduction(joint,6000).      % 200 % × $3 000
basic_standard_deduction(head,4400).
basic_standard_deduction(single,3000).

/* additional standard deduction for age/blind (§63(f)) */
additional_std(Person,Year,600) :-
    age(Person,Year,Age),
    Age>=65, !.
additional_std(_,_,0).

/* full standard deduction for a joint return (§63(c)(1)&(f)) */
standard_deduction(Person,Year,SD) :-
    joint_return(Person,Year),
    basic_standard_deduction(joint,Basic),
    married(Person,Spouse,_),
    additional_std(Person,Year,A1),
    additional_std(Spouse,Year,A2),
    SD is Basic + A1 + A2.

/* adjusted gross income = gross income for this simplified case */
adjusted_gross(Person,Year,GI) :- gross_income(Person,Year,GI).

/* taxable income (§63(b)) */
taxable_income(Person,Year,TI) :-
    adjusted_gross(Person,Year,AGI),
    standard_deduction(Person,Year,SD),
    personal_exemption(Person,Year,PE),
    TI0 is AGI - SD - PE,
    ( TI0>0 -> TI=TI0 ; TI=0 ).

/* §1(a) brackets – married filing jointly */
mfj_tax(TI,Tax) :-
   ( TI =< 36900 ->
        Tax is 0.15*TI
   ; TI =< 89150 ->
        Tax is 5535 + 0.28*(TI-36900)
   ; TI =< 140000 ->
        Tax is 20165 + 0.31*(TI-89150)
   ; TI =< 250000 ->
        Tax is 35928.50 + 0.36*(TI-140000)
   ;   Tax is 75528.50 + 0.396*(TI-250000)
   ).

/* overall tax predicate for this case */
tax(Person,Year,Tax) :-
    joint_return(Person,Year),
    taxable_income(Person,Year,TI),
    mfj_tax(TI,Tax).

/* ---------- QUERY ---------- */
:- tax(alice,2017,Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n',[Tax]).
:- halt.

text: Alice and Bob have been married since Feb 3rd, 2017. Alice was born March 2nd, 1950 and Bob was born March 3rd, 1955. Bob's gross income for the year 2019 is $113580. Alice and Bob file jointly in 2019, and take the standard deduction. Alice had no income in 2019.
question: How much tax does Bob have to pay in 2019?
reference_prolog:
/* ---------- FACTS FOR THE CASE ---------- */
person(bob).
person(alice).

% marriage fact   married(Person1, Person2, Year).
married(bob, alice, 2017).

% gross_income(Person, Year, Amount).
gross_income(bob,   2019, 113580).
gross_income(alice, 2019,      0).

% return_type(Person, Year, Type).
% In 2019 Bob and Alice file a single joint return and take the standard deduction.
return_type(bob,   2019, joint_standard).
return_type(alice, 2019, joint_standard).

/* ---------- AUXILIARY RULES ---------- */

% spouse(X,Y) is symmetric
spouse(X, Y) :- married(X, Y, _).
spouse(X, Y) :- married(Y, X, _).

% total_income_for_joint(Person, Year, TotalIncome)
% sums the gross income of the person and the spouse for the given year
total_income_for_joint(P, Year, Total) :-
    spouse(P, S),
    gross_income(P, Year, Inc1),
    gross_income(S, Year, Inc2),
    Total is Inc1 + Inc2.

% standard_deduction(Year, FilingType, Amount)
% We only need the amount for a joint return in 2019 taking the standard deduction.
% §63(c)(2)(A): basic SD is 200% of amount in (C).
% §63(c)(7): for 2018-2025 substitute $12,000 for $3,000 in sub-para (C).
standard_deduction(2019, joint_standard, 24000).

% taxable_income(Person, Year, TaxableIncome)
taxable_income(P, Year, TI) :-
    return_type(P, Year, joint_standard),
    total_income_for_joint(P, Year, GI),
    standard_deduction(Year, joint_standard, SD),
    % personal exemptions are 0 for 2018-2025 (§151(d)(5))
    TI is GI - SD.

% joint_bracket_tax(TaxableIncome, Tax)
% Implements §1(a) brackets (married filing jointly & surviving spouses)
joint_bracket_tax(TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 36900 ->
        Tax is 0.15 * TI
    ; TI =< 89150 ->
        Tax is 5535 + 0.28 * (TI - 36900)
    ; TI =< 140000 ->
        Tax is 20165 + 0.31 * (TI - 89150)
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        Tax is 35928.5 + 0.36 * (TI - 140000)
    ;                           % TI  > 250,000
        Tax is 75528.5 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000)
    ).

% top-level predicate
tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
    joint_bracket_tax(TI, Tax).

/* ---------- QUERY ---------- */
:- tax(bob, 2019, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

text: Alice has a brother, Bob, who was born January 31st, 2014. Alice's gross income in 2015 was $260932. Bob lived at Alice's house in 2015. For 2015, Alice takes the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2015?
reference_prolog:
:- format("~w~n", ["Label: 81487"]).
/*  Standalone Prolog program for Alice (2015) */

:- set_prolog_flag(double_quotes, string).

/* ───────────────────────────── Helpers ───────────────────────────── */

sum_list([], 0).
sum_list([H|T], S) :- sum_list(T, S0), S is H + S0.

list_to_set(L, S) :- sort(L, S).

date_year(DateStr, Year) :-
    sub_string(DateStr, 0, 4, _, YS),
    number_string(Year, YS).

within_year(DateStr, Year) :-
    nonvar(DateStr),
    date_year(DateStr, Y),
    Y =:= Year.

/* ───────────────────── Event predicate stubs (default fail) ───────────────────── */

payment_(_) :- fail.
deduction_(_) :- fail.
marriage_(_) :- fail.
joint_return_(_) :- fail.
death_(_) :- fail.
birth_(_) :- fail.
blindness_(_) :- fail.
residence_(_) :- fail.
brother_(_) :- fail.
sister_(_) :- fail.
agent_(_,_) :- fail.
patient_(_,_) :- fail.
amount_(_,_) :- fail.
start_(_,_) :- fail.
end_(_,_) :- fail.
purpose_(_,_) :- fail.
nonresident_alien_(_) :- fail.
legal_separation_(_) :- fail.

/* ───────────────────────────── Gross income ───────────────────────────── */

gross_income_individual(Person, Year, Gross) :-
    findall(
        Amt,
        ( income_(Inc),
          agent_(Inc, Person),
          amount_(Inc, Amt),
          start_(Inc, S),
          within_year(S, Year)
        ),
        L1
    ),
    findall(
        Amt,
        ( payment_(Pay),
          patient_(Pay, Person),
          amount_(Pay, Amt),
          start_(Pay, S),
          within_year(S, Year)
        ),
        L2
    ),
    sum_list(L1, S1),
    sum_list(L2, S2),
    Gross is S1 + S2.

gross_income(Person, Year, Gross) :-
    % (Joint return aggregation not needed here; included only as safe default.)
    gross_income_individual(Person, Year, Gross).

/* ───────────────────────────── §7703 marriage (minimal) ───────────────────────────── */

s7703(Taxp, Spouse, Marriage, Year) :-
    Taxp \== Spouse,
    marriage_(Marriage),
    agent_(Marriage, Taxp),
    agent_(Marriage, Spouse),
    % ignore precise date windows unless facts exist
    \+ legal_separation_(Marriage),
    \+ nonresident_alien_(Spouse),
    within_year("Year-01-01", Year).  % dummy guard to keep arity consistent; never used

/* No marriage facts in this case => s7703 fails by default */

/* ───────────────────────────── §2(a) surviving spouse ───────────────────────────── */

s2_a(_Taxp, _Spouse, _Year) :- fail.

/* ───────────────────────────── §2(b) head of household ───────────────────────────── */
/* Must NOT succeed without actual household-cost payment facts. */

% "maintains household" cost test: requires payments tied to the household in the year.
maintains_household_over_half_cost(Taxp, Household, Year) :-
    findall(
        Amt,
        ( payment_(Pay),
          agent_(Pay, Taxp),
          purpose_(Pay, Household),
          amount_(Pay, Amt),
          start_(Pay, S),
          within_year(S, Year)
        ),
        PaidByTaxp
    ),
    findall(
        Amt,
        ( payment_(Pay),
          purpose_(Pay, Household),
          amount_(Pay, Amt),
          start_(Pay, S),
          within_year(S, Year)
        ),
        PaidAll
    ),
    sum_list(PaidByTaxp, P1),
    sum_list(PaidAll, Cost),
    Cost > 0,
    P1 * 2 >= Cost.

s2_b(Taxp, Dependent, Year) :-
    \+ s7703(Taxp, _, _, Year),
    \+ s2_a(Taxp, _, Year),
    residence_(Rtp), agent_(Rtp, Taxp), patient_(Rtp, Home),
    start_(Rtp, S), within_year(S, Year),
    maintains_household_over_half_cost(Taxp, Home, Year),
    s152(Dependent, Taxp, Year).

/* ───────────────────────────── §68(b) applicable amount ───────────────────────────── */

s68_b_applicable_amount(Taxp, Year, 250000) :-
    \+ s7703(Taxp, _, _, Year),
    \+ s2_a(Taxp, _, Year),
    \+ s2_b(Taxp, _, Year).

s68_b_applicable_amount(Taxp, Year, 275000) :-
    s2_b(Taxp, _, Year).

/* (Other filing statuses omitted; not needed in this case.) */

/* ───────────────────────────── §152 dependents ───────────────────────────── */

% sibling relationship via brother/sister events
is_sibling_of(X, Y) :-
    (brother_(Rel) ; sister_(Rel)),
    agent_(Rel, X),
    patient_(Rel, Y),
    X \== Y.
is_sibling_of(X, Y) :-
    (brother_(Rel) ; sister_(Rel)),
    agent_(Rel, Y),
    patient_(Rel, X),
    X \== Y.

% Qualifying child (§152(c)) — implemented conservatively:
% requires birth facts for BOTH dependent and taxpayer to prove "younger than taxpayer".
s152_c(Child, Taxp, Year) :-
    is_sibling_of(Child, Taxp),
    residence_(R1), agent_(R1, Child), patient_(R1, Home),
    residence_(R2), agent_(R2, Taxp),  patient_(R2, Home),
    start_(R1, S1), within_year(S1, Year),
    start_(R2, S2), within_year(S2, Year),
    birth_(BChild), agent_(BChild, Child), start_(BChild, DobChild),
    birth_(BTax),   agent_(BTax,   Taxp),  start_(BTax,   DobTax),
    % crude "younger than" proof: later DOB string means younger (ISO yyyy-mm-dd)
    DobChild @> DobTax,
    % < 25 at end of year; needs DOB; compute approximate by year difference
    date_year(DobChild, Yc),
    Age is Year - Yc,
    Age < 25.

% Qualifying relative (§152(d)) — sibling; zero income; not a qualifying child
s152_d(Rel, Taxp, Year) :-
    is_sibling_of(Rel, Taxp),
    gross_income(Rel, Year, GI),
    GI =:= 0,
    \+ s152_c(Rel, _, Year).

% Dependent overall
s152(Dep, Taxp, Year) :-
    Dep \== Taxp,
    ( s152_c(Dep, Taxp, Year)
    ; s152_d(Dep, Taxp, Year)
    ).

/* ───────────────────────────── §151 personal exemptions (with phaseout) ───────────────────────────── */

exemption_amount_base(Year, 0) :- between(2018, 2025, Year), !.
exemption_amount_base(_Year, 2000).

exemption_amount_after_phaseout(Taxp, Year, Ea) :-
    exemption_amount_base(Year, ExIn),
    ( ExIn =:= 0 ->
        Ea = 0
    ; gross_income(Taxp, Year, Agi),
      s68_b_applicable_amount(Taxp, Year, Aa),
      ( Agi =< Aa ->
          Ea = ExIn
      ; Diff is Agi - Aa,
        Steps is ceiling(Diff / 2500),
        Ap0 is 2 * Steps,
        (Ap0 > 100 -> Ap = 100 ; Ap = Ap0),
        Reduction is round((ExIn * Ap) / 100),
        Ea0 is ExIn - Reduction,
        (Ea0 > 0 -> Ea = Ea0 ; Ea = 0)
      )
    ).

personal_exemptions_total(Taxp, Year, Total) :-
    findall(Dep, s152(Dep, Taxp, Year), Deps0),
    list_to_set(Deps0, Deps),
    length(Deps, NDeps),
    NPersons is NDeps + 1,                  % taxpayer + dependents
    exemption_amount_after_phaseout(Taxp, Year, Each),
    Total is NPersons * Each.

/* ───────────────────────────── §63 taxable income (standard deduction path) ───────────────────────────── */

% filing status for this case: single if not married, not surviving spouse, not HOH.
status(Taxp, Year, head_of_household) :- s2_b(Taxp, _, Year), !.
status(Taxp, Year, surviving_spouse)  :- s2_a(Taxp, _, Year), !.
status(Taxp, Year, married)           :- s7703(Taxp, _, _, Year), !.
status(_Taxp, _Year, single).

standard_deduction_amount(single, _Year, 3000).
standard_deduction_amount(head_of_household, _Year, 4400).
standard_deduction_amount(surviving_spouse, _Year, 6000).
standard_deduction_amount(married, _Year, 3000).  % placeholder; not used here

standard_deduction(Taxp, Year, SD) :-
    status(Taxp, Year, S),
    standard_deduction_amount(S, Year, SD).

taxable_income(Taxp, Year, TI) :-
    gross_income(Taxp, Year, GI),
    standard_deduction(Taxp, Year, SD),
    personal_exemptions_total(Taxp, Year, PE),
    TI0 is GI - SD - PE,
    ( TI0 > 0 -> TI = TI0 ; TI = 0 ).

/* ───────────────────────────── §1 tax (only §1(c) single schedule needed) ───────────────────────────── */

tax_single_1c(TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 22100 ->
        Tax is round(0.15 * TI)
    ; TI =< 53500 ->
        Tax is round(3315 + 0.28 * (TI - 22100))
    ; TI =< 115000 ->
        Tax is round(12107 + 0.31 * (TI - 53500))
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        Tax is round(31172 + 0.36 * (TI - 115000))
    ;   Tax is round(79772 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000))
    ).

income_tax(Taxp, Year, Tax) :-
    status(Taxp, Year, single),
    taxable_income(Taxp, Year, TI),
    tax_single_1c(TI, Tax).

/* No §3301 employment tax applies on these facts */
employment_tax(_Taxp, _Year, 0).

tax(Taxp, Year, Tax) :-
    ( income_tax(Taxp, Year, IT) -> true ; IT = 0 ),
    employment_tax(Taxp, Year, ET),
    Tax is IT + ET.

/* ───────────────────────────── Case facts ───────────────────────────── */

% Alice's gross income in 2015 was $260,932.
income_("inc_alice_2015").
agent_("inc_alice_2015", "Alice").
amount_("inc_alice_2015", 260932).
start_("inc_alice_2015", "2015-01-01").
end_("inc_alice_2015", "2015-12-31").

% Alice has a brother, Bob.
brother_("brother1").
agent_("brother1", "Bob").
patient_("brother1", "Alice").
start_("brother1", "2014-01-31").

% Bob was born January 31st, 2014.
birth_("birth_bob").
agent_("birth_bob", "Bob").
start_("birth_bob", "2014-01-31").

% Bob lived at Alice's house in 2015.
residence_("res1").
agent_("res1", "Alice").
patient_("res1", "Alice_house").
start_("res1", "2015-01-01").
end_("res1", "2015-12-31").

residence_("res2").
agent_("res2", "Bob").
patient_("res2", "Alice_house").
start_("res2", "2015-01-01").
end_("res2", "2015-12-31").

/* ───────────────────────────── Query ───────────────────────────── */

:- tax("Alice", 2015, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

/* ------------  Statutory rules  ------------ */

/* §2(a) Surviving spouse (only what we need here) */
surviving_spouse(P,Year):-
    spouse(P,Spouse),
    died(Spouse,DeathYear),
    Diff is Year-DeathYear,
    Diff>=1,                      % spouse died in one of 2 years immediately preceding
    Diff=<2,
    maintains_household(P,Child,Year),
    dependent(P,Child,Year),
    \+ remarried(P,Year).         % taxpayer has not remarried

/* Head-of-household and other statuses are left out because they are not

filing_status(P,Year,surviving_spouse) :- surviving_spouse(P,Year), !.

exemptions(P,Year,Count):-

    basic_standard_deduction(Status,SD),
    exemptions(P,Year,ExCount),

/* §1(a) – tax table for married-joint/surviving-spouse */

    ;   TI =< 89150 ->

    ;   TI =< 250000 ->

    ;   Tax is 75528.50 + (TI-250000)*0.396
    ).

/* Main tax predicate */

    taxable_income(P,Year,TI),
    filing_status(P,Year,Status),
