text: Alice got married on Dec 10th, 2009. Alice's gross income for the year 2016 is $554313. Alice files separately and takes the standard deduction. Her husband's gross income in 2016 is $56298 and he takes itemized deductions of $4421.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2016?
reference_prolog:
/* ---------- FACTS DESCRIBING THE CASE ---------- */

% identity
person("Alice").

% year concerned
year(2016).

% incomes
gross_income("Alice", 2016, 554313).
gross_income("Alice_spouse", 2016, 56298).

% filing choices
filing_status("Alice", 2016, married_filing_separate).
uses_standard_deduction("Alice", 2016).          % Alice wants to take it
itemizes_deductions("Alice_spouse", 2016).       % spouse itemizes

% personal data
age("Alice", 2016, 30).      % not aged
blind("Alice", 2016, false). % not blind

/* ---------- STATUTORY RULES NEEDED FOR THE COMPUTATION ---------- */

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
%  1.  Standard deduction  (I.R.C. §63(c))
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

% basic amount for married-filing-separate (pre-2018 rules)
basic_standard_deduction_amount(married_filing_separate, 3000).

% §63(c)(6)(A) – standard deduction denied if either spouse itemizes
standard_deduction(Person, Year, 0) :-
    filing_status(Person, Year, married_filing_separate),
    itemizes_deductions(_, Year),                 % SOME spouse itemizes
    !.                                            % rule takes precedence

% Otherwise take the normal basic standard deduction (no aged/blind add-ons needed here)
standard_deduction(Person, Year, Amount) :-
    filing_status(Person, Year, married_filing_separate),
    basic_standard_deduction_amount(married_filing_separate, Amount).

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
%  2.  Personal exemption and phase-out (I.R.C. §151(d)(3))
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

% base exemption (pre-2018)
base_exemption_amount(2000).

% section 68(b) applicable amount for married-filing-separate
phaseout_threshold(married_filing_separate, 150000).

% compute one exemption after phase-out
one_exemption_after_phaseout(AGI, married_filing_separate, FinalExemption) :-
    base_exemption_amount(Base),
    phaseout_threshold(married_filing_separate, Threshold),
    Excess is max(0, AGI - Threshold),
    Units is ceiling(Excess / 1250),          % $1,250 increments
    Percent is min(100, Units * 2),           % 2 percentage points per unit
    FinalExemption is Base * (100 - Percent) / 100.

% total exemption deduction for the taxpayer (no spouse deduction allowed here)
total_personal_exemption(Person, Year, Total) :-
    gross_income(Person, Year, AGI),
    filing_status(Person, Year, married_filing_separate),
    one_exemption_after_phaseout(AGI, married_filing_separate, OneEx),
    % only the taxpayer herself qualifies for an exemption
    Total is OneEx.

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
%  3.  Taxable income  (I.R.C. §63)
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

taxable_income(Person, Year, TaxableIncome) :-
    gross_income(Person, Year, AGI),
    standard_deduction(Person, Year, StdDed),
    total_personal_exemption(Person, Year, PersEx),
    TaxableIncome is AGI - StdDed - PersEx.

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
%  4.  Tax computation – Married Filing Separate brackets
%      (I.R.C. §1(d))
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

mfs_tax(TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 18450 ->
        Tax is TI * 0.15
    ; TI =< 44575 ->
        Tax is 2767.5 + (TI - 18450) * 0.28
    ; TI =< 70000 ->
        Tax is 10082.5 + (TI - 44575) * 0.31
    ; TI =< 125000 ->
        Tax is 17964.25 + (TI - 70000) * 0.36
    ; % over 125,000
      Tax is 37764.25 + (TI - 125000) * 0.396
    ).

tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
    mfs_tax(TI, Tax).

/* ---------- QUERY REQUIRED BY THE TASK ---------- */

:- tax("Alice", 2016, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

text: Alice has paid $3200 to Bob for domestic service done from Feb 1st, 2017 to Sep 2nd, 2017. In 2018, Bob has paid $4500 to Alice for work done from Apr 1st, 2017 to Sep 2nd, 2018. Alice was otherwise paid $113209 in 2018.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2018?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* -------------  Statutory rules (simplified for this case) ------------- */

:- use_module(library(lists)).

/* filing-status ─ Alice is an unmarried individual (§1(c)) */
status(alice, 2018, single).

/* wages received by Alice during 2018 (gross income) */
wage(alice, 2018, 113209).   /* “otherwise paid” */
wage(alice, 2018,   4500).   /* amount Bob paid in 2018 */

/* ---------- income aggregation ---------- */
gross_income(Person, Year, Total) :-
    findall(Amt, wage(Person, Year, Amt), L),
    sum_list(L, Total).

/* ---------- standard deduction (§63(c)(7)) ---------- */
standard_deduction(Person, Year, Ded) :-
    status(Person, Year, single),
    Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025,   /* years where new amounts apply */
    Ded = 12000.

/* ---------- taxable income (§63(b)) ---------- */
taxable_income(P, Y, TI) :-
    gross_income(P, Y, GI),
    standard_deduction(P, Y, SD),
    TI is GI - SD.

/* ---------- tax tables for unmarried individuals (§1(c)) ---------- */
tax_unmarried(TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 22100 ->
          Tax is 0.15 * TI
    ; TI =< 53500 ->
          Tax is 3315 + 0.28 * (TI - 22100)
    ; TI =< 115000 ->
          Tax is 12107 + 0.31 * (TI - 53500)
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
          Tax is 31172 + 0.36 * (TI - 115000)
    ;                       /* over $250,000 */
          Tax is 79772 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000)
    ).

/* ---------- master predicate ---------- */
tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    status(Person, Year, single),
    taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
    tax_unmarried(TI, Tax).

/* ------------------------- query ------------------------- */
:- tax(alice, 2018, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice and Bob were married from Feb 3rd, 1997 to Oct 30th, 2001. Alice's gross income for the year 2014 is $718791 and she takes the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2014?
reference_prolog:
/* ----------  FACTS FROM THE CASE  ---------- */
gross_income("Alice",2014,718791).

/* Alice and Bob were married 2/3/1997 – 10/30/2001            */
/* We record the calendar years in which the marriage existed. */
married_year("Alice","Bob",Y) :- between(1997,2001,Y).

/* ----------  STATUS DETERMINATION  ---------- */
married_in_year(Person,Year) :-
    married_year(Person,_,Year).

filing_status(Person,Year,surviving_spouse) :-  % death must be within 2 yrs – none here
    fail.                                       
filing_status(Person,Year,head_of_household) :- % no dependents given – none here
    fail.
filing_status(Person,Year,unmarried) :-
    \+ married_in_year(Person,Year).

/* ----------  STANDARD DEDUCTION  ---------- */
standard_deduction(unmarried,Year,Amt) :-
    (Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025 -> Amt = 12000          % §63(c)(7)(C)
    ;                               Amt = 3000).        % §63(c)(2)(C)

/* ----------  PERSONAL EXEMPTION  ---------- */
base_exemption_amount(Year,0)  :- Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025.  % §151(d)(5)
base_exemption_amount(Year,2000) :- Year < 2018 ; Year > 2025.

applicable_amount(unmarried,250000).
applicable_amount(head_of_household,275000).
applicable_amount(joint,300000).
applicable_amount(surviving_spouse,300000).

personal_exemption(Status,Year,AGI,ExAmt) :-
    base_exemption_amount(Year,Base),
    (Base =:= 0 -> ExAmt = 0                                   % years 2018-25
    ; applicable_amount(Status,Lim),
      (AGI =< Lim -> ExAmt = Base                              % no phase-out
      ; Excess is AGI - Lim,
        Increments is ceiling(Excess / 2500),
        Percentage is min(100, Increments * 2),                % §151(d)(3)
        ExAmt is Base * (100 - Percentage) / 100
      )).

/* ----------  TAXABLE INCOME  ---------- */
taxable_income(Person,Year,TI) :-
    gross_income(Person,Year,GI),
    filing_status(Person,Year,Status),
    standard_deduction(Status,Year,SD),
    personal_exemption(Status,Year,GI,PE),
    TI is GI - SD - PE.

/* ----------  TAX COMPUTATION FOR UNMARRIED INDIVIDUALS  ---------- */
tax_bracket_unmarried(1,0,   22100,0.15,0).
tax_bracket_unmarried(2,22100,53500,0.28,3315).
tax_bracket_unmarried(3,53500,115000,0.31,12107).
tax_bracket_unmarried(4,115000,250000,0.36,31172).
tax_bracket_unmarried(5,250000,inf,0.396,79772).

tax_amount_unmarried(TI,Tax) :-
    tax_bracket_unmarried(_,Lower,Upper,Rate,Base),
    (Upper == inf -> TI > Lower
    ; TI =< Upper, TI > Lower),
    Tax is Base + Rate * (TI - Lower).

/* ----------  MASTER TAX PREDICATE  ---------- */
tax(Person,Year,Tax) :-
    filing_status(Person,Year,unmarried),          % this case only covers unmarried
    taxable_income(Person,Year,TI),
    tax_amount_unmarried(TI,Tax).

/* ----------  QUERY  ---------- */
:- tax("Alice", 2014, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

text: Alice and Bob got married on Feb 3rd, 2013. Alice died on July 9th, 2014. Alice was a nonresident alien. From 2004 to 2019, Bob furnished the costs of maintaining the home where he and his father Charlie lived during that time. Charlie had no income from 2015 to 2019. Bob's gross income in 2015 was $678323. Bob takes the standard deduction in 2015.
question: How much tax does Bob have to pay in 2015?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ---------- FACTS FOR THE CASE ---------- */
tax_year(2015).

% Income
gross_income(bob, 2015, 678323).

% Household and dependency facts
dependent_of(bob, charlie, 2015).
relationship(bob, charlie, father).           % Charlie is Bob's father
income(charlie, 2015, 0).                      % Charlie had no income

% Spouse facts
spouse_died(bob, 2014, alice).
nonresident_alien(alice).                      % Alice was a non-resident alien

/* ---------- STATUTORY PARAMETERS ---------- */

% Standard deduction – basic amounts for 2015
basic_standard_deduction(head_of_household, 4400).
basic_standard_deduction(unmarried,         3000).

% Personal-exemption amount for 2015
exemption_amount(2015, 2000).

% Applicable amounts for §68(b) (phase-out thresholds)
applicable_amount(head_of_household, 275000).

/* ---------- RULES IMPLEMENTING THE STATUTES ---------- */

% Determine filing status for the taxpayer in a given year
filing_status(TP, Year, head_of_household) :-
    \+ married_at_close(TP, Year),                 % not married (§2(b)(1))
    \+ surviving_spouse(TP, Year),                 % not a surviving spouse
    maintains_household_for_parent(TP, Year).      % meets §2(b)(1)(B).

% Helper: married at the close of the year?  (§7703)
married_at_close(TP, Year) :-                      % spouse alive & not alien
    spouse(TP, Sp),
    \+ spouse_died(TP, _, _),                      % spouse not dead
    \+ nonresident_alien(Sp),
    Year = 2015.                                   % (specific to this case)

% In this case Bob’s spouse is deceased, so the above fails:
spouse(bob, alice).

% Surviving-spouse test (§2(a))
surviving_spouse(_TP, _Year) :-                    % requires dependent child
    fail.                                          % Bob fails – no child

% Head-of-household household maintenance test (§2(b)(1)(B))
maintains_household_for_parent(TP, Year) :-
    dependent_of(TP, Parent, Year),
    relationship(TP, Parent, father),
    furnishes_over_half_cost(TP, Year).            % Bob furnishes >½ cost

% For this problem that fact is simply true:
furnishes_over_half_cost(bob, 2015).

/* ---------- DEPENDENT (QUALIFYING-RELATIVE) TEST – §152 ---------- */
dependent(TP, Dep, Year) :-
    dependent_of(TP, Dep, Year),
    income(Dep, Year, Inc), Inc =< 0,              % §152(d)(1)(B)
    relationship(TP, Dep, father).                 % father satisfies relationship

/* ---------- COMPUTE EXEMPTIONS WITH PHASE-OUT (§151(d)(3)) ---------- */
allowed_exemption_total(TP, Year, AllowedTotal) :-
    exemption_amount(Year, EA),
    findall(Dep, (dependent(TP, Dep, Year)), Deps),
    length(Deps, DepCount),
    BaseTotal is EA * (DepCount + 1),              % +1 for taxpayer
    gross_income(TP, Year, AGI),                   % assume AGI = gross income
    filing_status(TP, Year, FS),
    applicable_amount(FS, AppAmt),
    Excess is max(0, AGI - AppAmt),
    CeilDiv is (Excess + 2499) // 2500,            % ceil(Excess/2500)
    Percent is min(100, CeilDiv * 2),              % 2% per increment, capped 100%
    AllowedTotal is BaseTotal * (100 - Percent) / 100.

/* ---------- STANDARD DEDUCTION (§63(c)) ---------- */
standard_deduction(TP, Year, SD) :-
    filing_status(TP, Year, FS),
    basic_standard_deduction(FS, SD).

/* ---------- TAXABLE INCOME (§63(b)) ---------- */
taxable_income(TP, Year, TI) :-
    gross_income(TP, Year, AGI),
    standard_deduction(TP, Year, SD),
    allowed_exemption_total(TP, Year, ExEmp),
    TI is AGI - SD - ExEmp.

 /* ---------- TAX COMPUTATION FOR HEAD OF HOUSEHOLD – §1(b) ---------- */
tax_on_taxable_income(head_of_household, TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 29600 ->
        Tax is 0.15 * TI
    ; TI =< 76400 ->
        Tax is 4440 + 0.28 * (TI - 29600)
    ; TI =< 127500 ->
        Tax is 17544 + 0.31 * (TI - 76400)
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        Tax is 33385 + 0.36 * (TI - 127500)
    ; Tax is 77485 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000)
    ).

/* ---------- TOP-LEVEL TAX PREDICATE ---------- */
tax(TP, Year, Tax) :-
    filing_status(TP, Year, FS),
    taxable_income(TP, Year, TI),
    tax_on_taxable_income(FS, TI, Tax).

/* ---------- QUERY ---------- */
:- tax(bob, 2015, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice has a brother, Bob, who was born January 31st, 2014 and has always lived at his parents' place. In 2016, Alice's gross income was $567192. Alice got married on Jan 12, 2016. Her husband had no income in 2016. Alice does not file a joint return. Alice has itemized deductions of $100206.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2016?
reference_prolog:
/* ----------  Statutory rules (only the provisions needed for this case)  ---------- */

% filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, Status).
%   Status is one of: joint, ss (surviving-spouse), hoh, single, mfs
filing_status(T, Y, joint) :- joint_return(T, Y), !.
filing_status(T, Y, ss)    :- surviving_spouse(T, Y), !.
filing_status(T, Y, hoh)   :- head_of_household(T, Y), !.
filing_status(T, Y, mfs)   :- married(T, Y), !.        % married, no joint return
filing_status(_, _, single).

% married(Taxpayer, Year)
married(T, Y) :-
    date_of_marriage(T, Y0, _), Y0 =< Y,      % married some time on or before the year
    \+ divorce_final(T, Y),                   % not divorced
    (   spouse_died_during_year(T, Y) -> fail ; true).

% Joint return fact (none for Alice)
joint_return(_, _) :- fail.

% Surviving spouse & head-of-household are not relevant here
surviving_spouse(_, _) :- fail.
head_of_household(_, _) :- fail.

% -----------------------  §68 – overall limitation on itemized deductions
applicable_amount(joint,  300000).
applicable_amount(ss,     300000).
applicable_amount(hoh,    275000).
applicable_amount(single, 250000).
applicable_amount(mfs,    150000).

limited_itemized(T, Y, Allowed) :-
    gross_income(T, Y, GI),
    filing_status(T, Y, Status),
    itemized_deductions(T, Y, Orig),
    applicable_amount(Status, AA),
    Excess is max(0, GI - AA),
    Red1  is 0.03 * Excess,
    Red2  is 0.8  * Orig,
    Red   is min(Red1, Red2),
    Allowed is Orig - Red.

% -----------------------  §151 – personal exemption & phase-out
exemption_amount(Y, 0) :- Y >= 2018, Y =< 2025, !.   % §151(d)(5)
exemption_amount(_, 2000).

personal_exemption(T, Y, 0) :- exemption_amount(Y, 0), !.
personal_exemption(T, Y, Deduction) :-
    exemption_amount(Y, EA),
    exemptions_count(T, Y, N),
    gross_income(T, Y, GI),
    filing_status(T, Y, Status),
    applicable_amount(Status, AA),
    Excess is max(0, GI - AA),
    unit(Status, U),
    Units is ceiling(Excess / U),
    PhasePct is min(100, Units * 2),
    Deduction is EA * N * (100 - PhasePct) / 100.

exemptions_count(T, Y, 2) :- spouse_no_income(T, Y), !.
exemptions_count(_, _, 1).

spouse_no_income(T, Y) :-
    spouse_income(T, Y, 0),
    \+ spouse_is_dependent(T, Y).

unit(mfs, 1250).
unit(_,   2500).

% -----------------------  §63 – taxable income
taxable_income(T, Y, TI) :-
    gross_income(T, Y, GI),
    limited_itemized(T, Y, Itemized),
    personal_exemption(T, Y, PE),
    TI0 is GI - Itemized - PE,
    TI  is max(0, TI0).

/* ----------  §1 – income-tax rate tables (only Married-Filing-Separate needed)  ---------- */

income_tax(mfs, TI, Tax) :- TI =< 18450,
    Tax is 0.15 * TI, !.
income_tax(mfs, TI, Tax) :- TI =< 44575,
    Tax is 2767.50 + 0.28 * (TI - 18450), !.
income_tax(mfs, TI, Tax) :- TI =< 70000,
    Tax is 10082.50 + 0.31 * (TI - 44575), !.
income_tax(mfs, TI, Tax) :- TI =< 125000,
    Tax is 17964.25 + 0.36 * (TI - 70000), !.
income_tax(mfs, TI, Tax) :-
    Tax is 37764.25 + 0.396 * (TI - 125000).

/* ----------  Putting it all together  ---------- */
tax(T, Y, Tax) :-
    taxable_income(T, Y, TI),
    filing_status(T, Y, Status),
    income_tax(Status, TI, Tax).

/* ---------------------  Facts for Alice  --------------------- */

gross_income("Alice", 2016, 567192).
itemized_deductions("Alice", 2016, 100206).

date_of_marriage("Alice", 2016, date(2016,1,12)).
divorce_final(_, _)      :- fail.
spouse_died_during_year(_, _) :- fail.

spouse_income("Alice", 2016, 0).
spouse_is_dependent(_, _) :- fail.

/* ------------------------------------------------------------- */

:- tax("Alice", 2016, Tax),
   format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

text: In 2017, Alice was paid $433320 in remuneration. Alice and Bob got married on Jan 1st, 2015. Bob's gross income for 2017 is $532134. Alice and Bob file a joint return for the year 2017 and take the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
/*  ----------  FACTS  ----------  */
gross_income("Alice", 2017, 433320).
gross_income("Bob",   2017, 532134).

spouse("Alice", "Bob").
spouse("Bob",   "Alice").

files_joint_return("Alice", 2017).
files_joint_return("Bob",   2017).

/*  ----------  STANDARD DEDUCTION (2017)  ----------  */
standard_deduction(joint_return, 6000).

/*  ----------  PERSONAL EXEMPTION BASE AMOUNT  ----------  */
personal_exemption_base(Year, 0)    :- Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025.
personal_exemption_base(Year, 2000) :- Year < 2018 ; Year > 2025.

/*  ----------  APPLICABLE AMOUNTS FOR PHASE-OUT  ----------  */
applicable_amount(joint_return, 300000).

/*  ----------  NUMBER OF EXEMPTIONS ON A JOINT RETURN  ----------  */
num_exemptions(joint_return, 2).

/*  ----------  AUXILIARY ­— CEILING DIVISION  ----------  */
ceil_div(N, D, R) :- R is (N + D - 1) // D.

/*  ----------  PERSONAL EXEMPTION AFTER PHASE-OUT  ----------  */
total_personal_exemption(joint_return, Year, AGI, Total) :-
    personal_exemption_base(Year, Base),
    num_exemptions(joint_return, N),
    (   Base =:= 0
    ->  Total = 0
    ;   applicable_amount(joint_return, Threshold),
        (   AGI =< Threshold
        ->  Total is N * Base
        ;   Excess is AGI - Threshold,
            ceil_div(Excess, 2500, Steps),
            Percent is Steps * 2,                       % percent
            ( Percent >= 100
            -> ReductionFactor = 1
            ;  ReductionFactor is Percent / 100 ),
            AllowedPer is Base * (1 - ReductionFactor),
            Total is N * AllowedPer
        )
    ).

/*  ----------  JOINT BRACKET TAX (IRC §1(a))  ----------  */
joint_bracket_tax(TI, Tax) :-
    (   TI =< 36900
    ->  Tax is TI * 0.15
    ;   TI =< 89150
    ->  Tax is 5535 + (TI - 36900) * 0.28
    ;   TI =< 140000
    ->  Tax is 20165 + (TI - 89150) * 0.31
    ;   TI =< 250000
    ->  Tax is 35928.5 + (TI - 140000) * 0.36
    ;   Tax is 75528.5 + (TI - 250000) * 0.396
    ).

/*  ----------  MAIN TAX COMPUTATION  ----------  */
tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    files_joint_return(Person, Year),
    spouse(Person, Spouse),
    gross_income(Person, Year, GI1),
    gross_income(Spouse, Year, GI2),
    CombinedGI is GI1 + GI2,
    standard_deduction(joint_return, SD),
    total_personal_exemption(joint_return, Year, CombinedGI, Exemptions),
    TaxableIncome is CombinedGI - SD - Exemptions,
    joint_bracket_tax(TaxableIncome, Tax).

/*  ----------  QUERY  ----------  */
:- tax("Alice", 2017, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

text: Bob is Alice's father. Alice has paid $2561 to Bob for work done from Feb 1st, 2013 to Sep 2nd, 2013, in Baltimore, Maryland, USA. Alice's gross income in 2013 is $42384. Alice takes the standard deduction in 2013.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2013?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ----------  STATUTORY RULES (subset needed for this case)  ---------- */

/* personal exemption (section 151) */
personal_exemption_amount(2013, 2000).

/* basic standard deduction (section 63(c)(2)) */
basic_standard_deduction(single,              3000).
basic_standard_deduction(head_of_household,   4400).
basic_standard_deduction(joint,               6000).   % 200% of 3000

/* tax-rate schedule for unmarried individuals (section 1(c))              */
/* bracket(LowerLimit, UpperLimit, BaseTax, MarginalRate). Limits inclusive */
bracket(    0, 22100,     0.00, 0.15).
bracket(22101, 53500,  3315.00, 0.28).
bracket(53501,115000, 12107.00, 0.31).
bracket(115001,250000,31172.00, 0.36).
bracket(250001, 1.0Inf,79772.00,0.396).

/* ----------  FACTS FROM THE CASE  ---------- */

person(alice).
year(2013).

gross_income(alice, 2013, 42384).        % Alice’s gross income
marital_status(alice, 2013, single).     % she is unmarried
takes_standard_deduction(alice, 2013).   % she does not itemise
aged_over_65(alice, 2013, false).        % not aged
blind(alice, 2013, false).               % not blind
dependents(alice, 2013, 0).              % no dependents

/* wages paid to her father – included for completeness, but do not affect
   Alice’s income-tax calculation (and are FUTA-exempt under §3306(c)(5)(A)). */
wages_paid(alice, 2013, bob, 2561).

/* ----------  DERIVED CALCULATIONS  ---------- */

/* adjusted gross income: no above-the-line deductions in this case        */
adjusted_gross_income(Person, Year, AGI) :-
    gross_income(Person, Year, AGI).

/* standard deduction amount */
standard_deduction_amount(Person, Year, SD) :-
    takes_standard_deduction(Person, Year),
    marital_status(Person, Year, Status),
    basic_standard_deduction(Status, SD).

/* total deductions = standard deduction + personal exemption              */
total_deductions(Person, Year, Total) :-
    standard_deduction_amount(Person, Year, SD),
    personal_exemption_amount(Year, PE),
    Total is SD + PE.

/* taxable income (section 63(b)) */
taxable_income(Person, Year, Taxable) :-
    adjusted_gross_income(Person, Year, AGI),
    total_deductions(Person, Year, Deductions),
    TI is AGI - Deductions,
    ( TI < 0 -> Taxable = 0 ; Taxable = TI ).

/* locate the bracket that contains Taxable, then compute tax              */
compute_tax_given_bracket(Taxable, Base, Rate, Lower, Tax) :-
    Excess is Taxable - (Lower - 1),
    Tax is Base + Excess * Rate.

income_tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    taxable_income(Person, Year, Taxable),
    marital_status(Person, Year, single),              % only case we need
    bracket(Lower, Upper, Base, Rate),
    Taxable =< Upper, Taxable >= Lower,
    compute_tax_given_bracket(Taxable, Base, Rate, Lower, Tax), !.

/* public wrapper */
tax(PersonAtom, Year, Tax) :-
    atom_string(PersonAtomLower, PersonAtom),          % allow "Alice" as string
    downcase_atom(PersonAtomLower, PersonName),        % get lowercase atom
    income_tax(PersonName, Year, Tax).

/* ----------  QUERY (required format)  ---------- */
:- tax("Alice", 2013, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice's and Bob's gross incomes for the year 2013 are $71879 and $11213 respectively. Alice and Bob got married on Feb 3rd, 1992. Alice and Bob have a child, Charlie, born October 9th, 2000. From 2004 to 2019, Bob furnished 40% of the costs of maintaining the home where he and Charlie lived during that time, and Alice furnished the remaining costs. In 2013, Alice and Bob file jointly and take the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2013?
reference_prolog:
/*********************************************************************
  Statutory rules (only the provisions that matter for this problem)
*********************************************************************/
% exemption amount §151(d)(1)
exemption_amount(2000).

% standard deduction §63(c)(2)(A)
% For a married couple filing a joint return
basic_standard_deduction(married_filing_joint, 6000).

% tax-rate schedule §1(a) (married filing jointly & surviving spouses)
mfj_tax(Taxable, Tax) :-
    (   Taxable =< 36900 ->
        Tax is 0.15 * Taxable
    ;   Taxable =< 89150 ->
        Tax is 5535 + 0.28 * (Taxable - 36900)
    ;   Taxable =< 140000 ->
        Tax is 20165 + 0.31 * (Taxable - 89150)
    ;   Taxable =< 250000 ->
        Tax is 35928.5 + 0.36 * (Taxable - 140000)
    ;   % over 250,000
        Tax is 75528.5 + 0.396 * (Taxable - 250000)
    ).

/*********************************************************************
  Family relationships – minimal facts needed for this case
*********************************************************************/
spouse(alice,bob).
spouse(bob,alice).

child_of(charlie,alice).
child_of(charlie,bob).

born(charlie,2000).

/*********************************************************************
  Basic facts for the 2013 taxable year
*********************************************************************/
income(alice,2013,71879).
income(bob  ,2013,11213).

% Filing choice for 2013
filing_status(alice,2013,married_filing_joint).

% Charlie lives with both parents for the whole year, so he is a
% qualifying child (§152(c)) and therefore a dependent (§152(a)(1))
qualifying_child(Child,Taxpayer,Year) :-
    child_of(Child,Taxpayer),
    age(Child,Year,Age),
    Age < 25.                   % §152(c)(3)

age(Person,Year,Age) :-
    born(Person,BirthYear),
    Age is Year - BirthYear.

dependent_of(Dep,Taxpayer,Year) :-
    qualifying_child(Dep,Taxpayer,Year).

/*********************************************************************
  Computing deductions
*********************************************************************/
standard_deduction(Taxpayer,Year,SD) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer,Year,married_filing_joint),
    basic_standard_deduction(married_filing_joint,SD).

personal_exemptions(Taxpayer,Year,Count) :-
    % two spouses on a joint return + number of dependents
    findall(D,dependent_of(D,Taxpayer,Year),Deps),
    length(Deps,DepCnt),
    Count is 2 + DepCnt.

personal_exemption_deduction(Taxpayer,Year,Amount) :-
    personal_exemptions(Taxpayer,Year,Count),
    exemption_amount(EA),
    Amount is Count * EA.

/*********************************************************************
  Taxable income and tax owed for a joint return
*********************************************************************/
adjusted_gross_income(Taxpayer,Year,AGI) :-
    income(Taxpayer,Year,Inc1),
    spouse(Taxpayer,Spouse),
    income(Spouse,Year,Inc2),
    AGI is Inc1 + Inc2.

taxable_income(Taxpayer,Year,TI) :-
    adjusted_gross_income(Taxpayer,Year,AGI),
    standard_deduction(Taxpayer,Year,SD),
    personal_exemption_deduction(Taxpayer,Year,PE),
    TI is AGI - SD - PE.

tax(Taxpayer,Year,Tax) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer,Year,married_filing_joint),
    taxable_income(Taxpayer,Year,TI),
    mfj_tax(TI,Tax).

/*********************************************************************
  Query – 2013 tax owed by Alice
*********************************************************************/
:- tax(alice, 2013, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

text: Alice was paid $200 in March 2017 for services performed at Johns Hopkins Hospital. Alice was a patient at Johns Hopkins Hospital from March 15th, 2017 to April 2nd, 2017. In 2017, Alice was also paid $31220 in remuneration. Alice takes the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
/* ----------  Statutory rules relevant to this problem ---------- */

% filing_status(Person, Year, Status)
% Only the statuses that matter for this problem are encoded.
filing_status(P, Y, unmarried) :- fact_status(P, Y, unmarried).

% standard_deduction(Person, Year, Amount)
% §63(c)(2):  $3,000 basic standard deduction for “any other case” in 2017.
standard_deduction(P, Y, 3000) :-
    filing_status(P, Y, unmarried).

% personal_exemption(Person, Year, Amount)
% §151(d)(1): exemption amount is $2,000 for 2017.
personal_exemption(P, 2017, 2000) :-
    \+ fact_is_dependent(P, 2017).

% income_tax_rate_unmarried(TaxableIncome, Tax)
% §1(c):  five-bracket rate table for unmarried individuals.
income_tax_rate_unmarried(TI, Tax) :-
    (   TI =< 22100 ->
        Tax is TI * 0.15
    ;   TI =< 53500 ->
        Tax is 3315 + 0.28 * (TI - 22100)
    ;   TI =< 115000 ->
        Tax is 12107 + 0.31 * (TI - 53500)
    ;   TI =< 250000 ->
        Tax is 31172 + 0.36 * (TI - 115000)
    ;   Tax is 79772 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000)
    ).

/* ----------  Generic predicates ---------- */

% sum_list(List, Sum) – helper to sum numbers in a list.
sum_list([], 0).
sum_list([H|T], S) :- sum_list(T, R), S is H + R.

% gross_income(Person, Year, Gross)
gross_income(P, Y, GI) :-
    findall(Amt, fact_wages(P, Y, Amt), Amts),
    sum_list(Amts, GI).

% taxable_income(Person, Year, TaxableIncome)
taxable_income(P, Y, TI) :-
    gross_income(P, Y, GI),
    standard_deduction(P, Y, SD),
    personal_exemption(P, Y, PE),
    TI is GI - SD - PE.

% tax(Person, Year, Tax)
tax(P, Y, Tax) :-
    filing_status(P, Y, unmarried),
    taxable_income(P, Y, TI),
    income_tax_rate_unmarried(TI, Tax).

/* ----------  Facts from the problem ---------- */

% Alice’s marital status
fact_status(alice, 2017, unmarried).

% Alice is not someone else’s dependent
fact_is_dependent(alice, 2017) :- fail.

% Wages paid to Alice in 2017
% $31,220 regular remuneration + $200 for services while a patient
fact_wages(alice, 2017, 31220).
fact_wages(alice, 2017, 200).

/* ----------  Query ---------- */

:- tax(alice, 2017, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

text: Alice shares a house with her father Bob since 2007. Alice pays for 75% of the costs of the house, while Charlie, her brother, pays for the remaining 25%. Alice's gross income for the year 2012 was $67285 and Charlie's was $56174. Alice is allowed itemized deductions of $17817.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2012?
reference_prolog:
/************************************************************************
 *  Statutory rules (only the portions needed for this case)            *
 ************************************************************************/

% filing status ---------------------------------------------------------
% A taxpayer is a head-of-household if (i) not married, (ii) not a
% surviving spouse, (iii) maintains the household which is the
% principal place of abode of a dependent father or mother for the
% year, and (iv) furnishes over half of the cost of maintaining it.

head_of_household(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    marital_status(Taxpayer, Year, single),        % not married
    \+ surviving_spouse(Taxpayer, Year),           % not surviving spouse
    dependent(Taxpayer, Parent, Year),             % has a dependent
    parent_of(Parent, Taxpayer),                   % who is father/mother
    maintains_household(Taxpayer, Year).           % paid > ½ the cost

% For this problem nobody is a surviving spouse.
surviving_spouse(_, _) :- fail.

% A taxpayer maintains the household if he/she pays > ½ the cost.
maintains_household(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    share_of_cost(Taxpayer, Year, Share),
    Share > 0.5.

% personal-exemption ----------------------------------------------------
exemption_amount(2000).

num_exemptions(Taxpayer, Year, Num) :-
    findall(D, dependent(Taxpayer, D, Year), L),
    length(L, NDeps),
    Num is NDeps + 1.                % +1 for the taxpayer

total_exemption_deduction(Taxpayer, Year, Amount) :-
    exemption_amount(EA),
    num_exemptions(Taxpayer, Year, Num),
    Amount is EA * Num.

% taxable income --------------------------------------------------------
taxable_income(Taxpayer, Year, TaxableIncome) :-
    gross_income(Taxpayer, Year, GI),
    itemized_deductions(Taxpayer, Year, ID),
    total_exemption_deduction(Taxpayer, Year, Exempt),
    TI is GI - ID - Exempt,
    (  TI > 0 -> TaxableIncome = TI
    ;  TaxableIncome = 0).

% §1(b) – tax table for heads of household
tax_amount_hoh(TI, Tax) :-
    (  TI =< 29600
       -> Tax is TI * 0.15
    ;  TI =< 76400
       -> Tax is 4440 + 0.28 * (TI - 29600)
    ;  TI =< 127500
       -> Tax is 17544 + 0.31 * (TI - 76400)
    ;  TI =< 250000
       -> Tax is 33385 + 0.36 * (TI - 127500)
    ;  Tax is 77485 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000)
    ).

% master rule -----------------------------------------------------------
tax(Taxpayer, Year, Tax) :-
    head_of_household(Taxpayer, Year),
    taxable_income(Taxpayer, Year, TI),
    tax_amount_hoh(TI, Tax).


/************************************************************************
 *  Facts for the Alice / Bob / Charlie case                            *
 ************************************************************************/

% family relationships
parent_of(bob, alice).

% marital status
marital_status(alice, 2012, single).

% household cost shares (fractions)
share_of_cost(alice,  2012, 0.75).
share_of_cost(charlie,2012, 0.25).

% dependent information
dependent(alice, bob, 2012).

% incomes and deductions
gross_income(alice, 2012, 67285).
itemized_deductions(alice, 2012, 17817).

/************************************************************************
 *  Query required by the assignment                                    *
 ************************************************************************/

:- tax(alice, 2012, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

text: Alice, born Sep 4th, 1950, has a son, Bob, who was born January 31st, 1984. Alice and Bob share the same principal place of abode since then, which Alice maintains. Alice's gross income for the year 1997 is $172980. Alice takes the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 1997?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ----------  Statutory rules that are relevant to the case  ---------- */

% basic exemption amount (§151(d)(1))
exemption_amount(2000).

% basic standard deductions for 1997 (§63(c)(2))
basic_standard_deduction(joint_or_surviving_spouse, 6000).   % 200% of $3,000
basic_standard_deduction(head_of_household,           4400). % §63(c)(2)(B)
basic_standard_deduction(single,                      3000). % §63(c)(2)(C)

% additional standard deductions for aged and blind (§63(f))
additional_standard_deduction(Person, Year, 0) :-
    \+ aged(Person, Year),
    \+ blind(Person, Year).
additional_standard_deduction(Person, Year, 600) :-
    aged(Person, Year),
    \+ blind(Person, Year).
additional_standard_deduction(Person, Year, 600) :-
    blind(Person, Year),
    \+ aged(Person, Year).
additional_standard_deduction(Person, Year, 1200) :-
    aged(Person, Year),
    blind(Person, Year).

% A person is aged (65+) if the 65th birthday is before or on Dec-31 (§63(f)(1))
aged(Person, Year) :-
    birth(Person, date(BY, BM, BD)),
    (Year - BY > 65 ;
     Year - BY =:= 65, (BM < 12 ; (BM =:= 12, BD =< 31))).

% Filing status (§2 & §7703 distilled for this case)
filing_status(Person, Year, head_of_household) :-
    not_married(Person, Year),
    maintains_household(Person, Year),
    qualifying_child(_, Person, Year), !.

filing_status(Person, Year, single) :-
    not_married(Person, Year),
    \+ filing_status(Person, Year, head_of_household).

% Qualifying child (§152(c) – sufficient conditions for this case)
qualifying_child(Child, Parent, Year) :-
    child_of(Child, Parent),
    principal_abode(Child, Parent, Year),
    age(Child, Year, AgeC),
    AgeC < 25.                 % §152(c)(3)

% Age helper (exact enough for present purposes)
age(Person, Year, Age) :-
    birth(Person, date(BY,_,_)),
    Age is Year - BY.

% Standard deduction (§63(c))
standard_deduction(Person, Year, Amount) :-
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    basic_standard_deduction(Status, Basic),
    additional_standard_deduction(Person, Year, Add),
    Amount is Basic + Add.

% Personal-exemption deduction (§151)
personal_exemption(Person, Year, Total) :-
    exemption_amount(EA),
    findall(Dep, dependent(Dep, Person, Year), Deps),
    length(Deps, NDeps),
    N is NDeps + 1,            % +1 for the taxpayer herself
    Total is N * EA.

% Taxable income (§63(a) & (b))
taxable_income(Person, Year, TI) :-
    gross_income(Person, Year, GI),
    standard_deduction(Person, Year, SD),
    personal_exemption(Person, Year, PE),
    TI is GI - SD - PE.

% 1997 head-of-household tax table (§1(b))
tax_due_from_table(head_of_household, TI, Tax) :-
    TI =< 29600,                      Tax is 0.15  * TI.
tax_due_from_table(head_of_household, TI, Tax) :-
    TI > 29600, TI =< 76400,          Tax is 4440  + 0.28 * (TI - 29600).
tax_due_from_table(head_of_household, TI, Tax) :-
    TI > 76400, TI =< 127500,         Tax is 17544 + 0.31 * (TI - 76400).
tax_due_from_table(head_of_household, TI, Tax) :-
    TI > 127500, TI =< 250000,        Tax is 33385 + 0.36 * (TI - 127500).
tax_due_from_table(head_of_household, TI, Tax) :-
    TI > 250000,                      Tax is 77485 + 0.396* (TI - 250000).

tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    tax_due_from_table(Status, TI, Tax).

/* ----------  Facts for the case of Alice  ---------- */

% Birth data
birth("Alice", date(1950,9,4)).
birth("Bob",   date(1984,1,31)).

% Family relationship
child_of("Bob", "Alice").

% Household & support
maintains_household("Alice", 1997).
principal_abode("Bob", "Alice", 1997).

% Income
gross_income("Alice", 1997, 172980).

% Marital status & blindness
not_married("Alice", 1997).
blind("Alice", 1997, false).
blind(Person, Year) :- blind(Person, Year, true).

% Dependent (Bob is a qualifying child and dependent of Alice)
dependent("Bob", "Alice", 1997).

/* ----------  Query required by the exercise ---------- */
:- tax("Alice", 1997, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice's gross income for the year 2023 is $54775. In 2023, Bob, the son of her son Charlie, lives at her place, a house that she maintains. Bob has no income in 2023. Alice takes the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2023?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
% ---------- Statutory rules (simplified) ----------

% Gross income fact for the case
gross_income("Alice", 2023, 54775).

% Filing-status fact (derived from §§2(b), 152)
filing_status("Alice", 2023, head_of_household).

% Standard-deduction amounts for 2018-2025 (see §63(c)(7))
standard_deduction_amount(head_of_household, 18000).
standard_deduction_amount(single,                12000).
standard_deduction_amount(married_filing_jointly,24000).
standard_deduction_amount(married_filing_separately,12000).
standard_deduction_amount(surviving_spouse,      24000).

% Compute the standard deduction applicable to a taxpayer
standard_deduction(Taxpayer, Year, Amount) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, Status),
    standard_deduction_amount(Status, Amount).

% Taxable income = gross income – standard deduction (personal exemptions = 0 for 2023)
taxable_income(Taxpayer, Year, TaxableIncome) :-
    gross_income(Taxpayer, Year, Gross),
    standard_deduction(Taxpayer, Year, SD),
    TaxableIncome is Gross - SD.

% §1(b) – Head-of-household rate table
tax_brackets(head_of_household, Income, Tax) :-
    (   Income =< 29600
    ->  T is 0.15 * Income
    ;   Income =< 76400
    ->  T is 4440 + 0.28 * (Income - 29600)
    ;   Income =< 127500
    ->  T is 17544 + 0.31 * (Income - 76400)
    ;   Income =< 250000
    ->  T is 33385 + 0.36 * (Income - 127500)
    ;   T is 77485 + 0.396 * (Income - 250000)
    ),
    Tax is round(T).              % round to nearest dollar

% Master rule: tax owed by a taxpayer for a year
tax(Taxpayer, Year, Tax) :-
    taxable_income(Taxpayer, Year, TI),
    filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, Status),
    tax_brackets(Status, TI, Tax).

% ---------- Query ----------

:- tax("Alice", 2023, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

In 2019, Bob has a different principal place of abode than Alice and Charlie.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2019?
reference_prolog:
/* ---------- Statutory rules (subset needed for this case) ---------- */

/* Standard deduction amounts (§63(c)(2),(c)(7)) */
std_deduction(joint_or_surviving_spouse, Year, Ded) :-
    ( Year >= 2018 -> Ded = 24000 ; Ded = 12000 ).
std_deduction(head_of_household, Year, Ded) :-
    ( Year >= 2018 -> Ded = 18000 ; Ded = 4400 ).
std_deduction(single, Year, Ded) :-
    ( Year >= 2018 -> Ded = 12000 ; Ded = 3000 ).

/* Personal-exemption amount (§151(d)(5)) */
exemption_amount(Year, Amount) :-
    ( Year >= 2018 -> Amount = 0 ; Amount = 2000 ).

/* §63(b): taxable income for non-itemizers */
taxable_income(Year, FilingStatus, GrossIncome, TaxableIncome) :-
    std_deduction(FilingStatus, Year, Std),
    exemption_amount(Year, Exemp),
    TaxableIncome is GrossIncome - Std - Exemp.

/* §1(c): tax table for unmarried individuals (other than surviving spouses
   and heads of households) – simplified to just what we need here */
tax_table_single(TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 22100 ->
        Tax is 0.15 * TI
    ; TI =< 53500 ->
        Tax is 3315 + 0.28 * (TI - 22100)
    ; TI =< 115000 ->
        Tax is 12107 + 0.31 * (TI - 53500)
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        Tax is 31172 + 0.36 * (TI - 115000)
    ; Tax is 79772 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000)
    ).

/* ---------- Facts from the case ---------- */

gross_income(alice, 2019, 73124).
filing_status(alice, 2019, single).
takes_standard_deduction(alice, 2019).

/* ---------- Derived facts ---------- */

/* Alice’s dependent: Charlie is her qualifying child (§152(c)) */
dependent(alice, charlie, 2019).

/* Alice maintains a household (§63(c)(6) – not required for single filing,
   but included for completeness) */
maintains_household(alice, 2019).

/* ---------- Tax computation ---------- */

tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    gross_income(Person, Year, Gross),
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    takes_standard_deduction(Person, Year),
    taxable_income(Year, Status, Gross, TI),
    tax_table_single(TI, Tax).

/* ---------- Query ---------- */
:- tax(alice, 2019, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]), halt.

text: Alice and Bob have been married since Feb 3rd, 2017. Alice files a joint return with her spouse for 2020. Alice's gross income for the year 2020 is $103272. Bob earned $10 in 2020. Alice and Bob take the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Bob have to pay in 2020?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/****************************************************
 *  Statutory rules (simplified to what is relevant)
 ****************************************************/

%  §7703  – marital status
married(Person, Year) :-
    spouse(Person, Other),
    spouse(Other, Person),
    married_since(Person, Other, StartYear),
    StartYear =< Year.

%  §63(c)(2) & (7) – basic standard deduction, taxable years 2018-2025
basic_standard_deduction(Year, joint, 24000) :-
    Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025.           % married filing jointly
basic_standard_deduction(Year, single, 12000) :-
    Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025.           % single (not needed here)

%  §63(b) – taxable income for non-itemizers
taxable_income(Year, joint, GrossIncome, TaxableIncome) :-
    basic_standard_deduction(Year, joint, StdDed),
    TaxableIncome is max(0, GrossIncome - StdDed).  % personal exemptions are 0 (2018-25)

%  §1(a) – joint / surviving-spouse rate table
joint_bracket_tax(Income, Tax) :-
    Income =< 36900,                  Tax is 0.15 * Income.
joint_bracket_tax(Income, Tax) :-
    Income >  36900, Income =< 89150, Tax is 5535 + 0.28  * (Income - 36900).
joint_bracket_tax(Income, Tax) :-
    Income >  89150, Income =< 140000,Tax is 20165 + 0.31 * (Income - 89150).
joint_bracket_tax(Income, Tax) :-
    Income > 140000, Income =< 250000,Tax is 35928.5 + 0.36 * (Income - 140000).
joint_bracket_tax(Income, Tax) :-
    Income > 250000,                  Tax is 75528.5 + 0.396* (Income - 250000).

/****************************************************
 *  Facts for the case
 ****************************************************/

% marriage facts
spouse(alice, bob).
spouse(bob,  alice).
married_since(alice, bob, 2017).   % married on Feb 3rd 2017

% 2020 gross income
gross_income(alice, 2020, 103272).
gross_income(bob,   2020, 10).

% they file a joint return in 2020
file_status(2020, alice, joint).
file_status(2020, bob,   joint).

/****************************************************
 *  Aggregate income & final tax computation
 ****************************************************/

% total gross income for a joint return
joint_gross_income(Year, Total) :-
    gross_income(alice, Year, A),
    gross_income(bob,   Year, B),
    Total is A + B.

% compute total joint tax for the year
joint_tax(Year, Tax) :-
    joint_gross_income(Year, GI),
    taxable_income(Year, joint, GI, TI),
    joint_bracket_tax(TI, Tax).

% each spouse is jointly (and severally) liable for the joint tax
tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    file_status(Year, Person, joint),
    joint_tax(Year, Tax).

/****************************************************
 *  Query: How much tax does Bob have to pay in 2020?
 ****************************************************/
:- tax(bob, 2020, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice and Bob got married on Sep 12th, 1998. Their son Charlie was born October 1st, 2013. Bob passed away March 2nd, 2015. In 2017, Alice and Charlie live in a house maintained by Alice. Alice's gross income for the year 2017 was $70117. Alice takes the standard deduction in 2017.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* -------------  FACTS ------------- */

/* personal data */
person("Alice").
person("Bob").
person("Charlie").

spouse("Alice","Bob").
spouse("Bob","Alice").

birth("Charlie",date(2013,10,1)).          /* Charlie born Oct 1st 2013 */

/* death of Bob */
death("Bob",date(2015,3,2)).

/* living-together facts for 2017 */
lives_with("Alice","Charlie",2017).

/* Alice maintains the household and pays > 50 % of costs */
maintains_household("Alice",2017).

/* income */
gross_income("Alice",2017,70117).

/* Alice did not remarry */
remarried("Alice",_) :- fail.              /* always fails = never remarried */

/* -------------  RULES ------------- */

/* helper: year difference */
yrs_between(date(Y1,_,_),YearDiff,Year2) :- Year2 is Y1 + YearDiff.

/* test if spouse died in one of the two years preceding the tax year */
spouse_died_last_two_years(T,Year) :-
    spouse(T,S),
    death(S,date(DY,_,_)),
    (Year - DY) =< 2,
    (Year - DY) >= 1.

/* dependent check – here we directly assert Charlie is dependent */
dependent_of(Child,Taxpayer,Year) :-
    lives_with(Taxpayer,Child,Year),
    Child="Charlie",
    Taxpayer="Alice".

/* -------------  Filing status ------------- */
filing_status(T,Year,surviving_spouse) :-
    spouse_died_last_two_years(T,Year),
    \+ remarried(T,Year),
    maintains_household(T,Year),
    dependent_of(_,T,Year), !.

/* -------------  Standard deduction (year 2017) ------------- */
standard_deduction(surviving_spouse,2017,6000).
standard_deduction(joint,2017,6000).
standard_deduction(head_of_household,2017,4400).
standard_deduction(single,2017,3000).

/* -------------  Personal exemptions (year 2017) ------------- */
personal_exemption_amount(2017,2000).

count_personal_exemptions(T,Year,Count) :-
    findall(X,dependent_of(X,T,Year),Deps),
    length(Deps,DepCount),
    Count is DepCount + 1.  /* +1 for taxpayer */

/* -------------  Taxable income ------------- */
taxable_income(Gross,StdDed,ExAmt,ExCnt,Taxable) :-
    Taxable is Gross - StdDed - ExAmt*ExCnt.

/* -------------  Tax computation – joint / surviving spouse table ------------- */
tax_bracket_joint(Income,Tax) :-
    Income =< 36900,                     Tax is 0.15*Income, !.
tax_bracket_joint(Income,Tax) :-
    Income =< 89150,                     Tax is 5535 + 0.28*(Income-36900), !.
tax_bracket_joint(Income,Tax) :-
    Income =< 140000,                    Tax is 20165 + 0.31*(Income-89150), !.
tax_bracket_joint(Income,Tax) :-
    Income =< 250000,                    Tax is 35928.50 + 0.36*(Income-140000), !.
tax_bracket_joint(Income,Tax) :-
    Income  > 250000,                    Tax is 75528.50 + 0.396*(Income-250000).

compute_tax(surviving_spouse,Income,Tax) :- tax_bracket_joint(Income,Tax).
compute_tax(joint,Income,Tax)           :- tax_bracket_joint(Income,Tax).

/* -------------  Master predicate ------------- */
tax(Taxpayer,Year,Tax) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer,Year,Status),
    standard_deduction(Status,Year,StdDed),
    personal_exemption_amount(Year,ExAmt),
    count_personal_exemptions(Taxpayer,Year,ExCnt),
    gross_income(Taxpayer,Year,Gross),
    taxable_income(Gross,StdDed,ExAmt,ExCnt,TaxableIncome),
    compute_tax(Status,TaxableIncome,Tax).

/* -------------  Query ------------- */
:- tax("Alice", 2017, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice's gross income for the year 2014 is $97407. In 2014, Alice's father Bob lived at the house that Alice maintains and resides in. Alice takes the standard deduction in 2014.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2014?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* -------------  STATUTORY RULES ------------- */

/* basic filing-status rules (only those needed here) */
married(Person,Year,false).        /* facts can be overridden if necessary */
surviving_spouse(Person,Year,false).

/* dependency (section 152) */
dependent(bob,alice,2014).

/* relationship facts for dependency test */
relationship(bob,alice,father).

/* head-of-household test   (section 2(b))               */
qualifying_dependent_for_hoh(Dep,Taxpayer,Year) :-
    dependent(Dep,Taxpayer,Year).

head_of_household(Person,Year) :-
    married(Person,Year,false),
    surviving_spouse(Person,Year,false),
    qualifying_dependent_for_hoh(_,Person,Year).

/* ---------------- DEDUCTIONS ---------------- */

/* standard deduction (section 63(c)(2)) */
standard_deduction_amount(hoh,Year,4400) :- Year < 2018.

/* personal-exemption amount (section 151(d)) */
exemption_amount(Year,0)      :- Year>=2018, Year=<2025.
exemption_amount(Year,2000)   :- Year<2018 ; Year>2025.

/* number of dependents claimed */
num_dependents(Person,Year,Count) :-
    findall(D,dependent(D,Person,Year),L),
    length(L,Count).

personal_exemption_count(Person,Year,Total) :-
    num_dependents(Person,Year,DCount),
    Total is DCount + 1.          /* +1 for the taxpayer */

/* -------------  TAXABLE INCOME -------------- */
gross_income(alice,2014,97407).
uses_standard_deduction(alice,2014,true).

taxable_income(Person,Year,TI) :-
    gross_income(Person,Year,Gross),
    head_of_household(Person,Year),
    standard_deduction_amount(hoh,Year,StdDed),
    exemption_amount(Year,ExAmt),
    personal_exemption_count(Person,Year,ExCnt),
    TotalExempt is ExAmt*ExCnt,
    TI is Gross - StdDed - TotalExempt.

/* --------------- RATE SCHEDULE -------------- */
/* section 1(b): heads of household */
tax_hoh(Income,Tax) :-
    Income =< 29600,
    Tax is 0.15*Income.
tax_hoh(Income,Tax) :-
    Income > 29600, Income =< 76400,
    Tax is 4440 + 0.28*(Income-29600).
tax_hoh(Income,Tax) :-
    Income > 76400, Income =< 127500,
    Tax is 17544 + 0.31*(Income-76400).
tax_hoh(Income,Tax) :-
    Income > 127500, Income =< 250000,
    Tax is 33385 + 0.36*(Income-127500).
tax_hoh(Income,Tax) :-
    Income > 250000,
    Tax is 77485 + 0.396*(Income-250000).

/* -------------- MAIN TAX RULE --------------- */
tax(Person,Year,TaxDue) :-
    head_of_household(Person,Year),
    taxable_income(Person,Year,TI),
    tax_hoh(TI,TaxRaw),
    TaxDue is round(TaxRaw*100)/100.   /* pennies precision */

/* ---------------  QUERY  -------------------- */
:- tax(alice, 2014, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n',[Tax]).
:- halt.
```

question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2012?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
% ---------- statutory rules (only the parts needed for this problem) ----------
% §1(c) 2012 rate schedule for unmarried individuals
% bracket(LowerInclusive, UpperInclusive, BaseTax, Rate, BaseAmount).
%  (BaseTax is the tax due on income up to LowerInclusive-1;  
%   Rate applies to the amount over LowerInclusive;
%   BaseAmount is LowerInclusive.)

bracket(0,       22100, 0.0,    0.15, 0).           % 15 % of first $22,100
bracket(22101,   53500, 3315.0, 0.28, 22100).       % $3,315  + 28 % of excess
bracket(53501,  115000, 12107.0,0.31, 53500).       % not reached in this case
bracket(115001,250000, 31172.0, 0.36,115000).       %  “
bracket(250001,inf,    79772.0, 0.396,250000).      %  “

% §63(c) basic standard deduction (single, 2012)
basic_standard_deduction(single, 3000).

% §151 exemption amount (2012)
exemption_amount(2000).

% ------------------------------ facts ----------------------------------------
person(alice).
year(2012).

% gross remuneration (§63)
gross_income(alice, 2012, 54268).

% filing status – Alice is an unmarried individual, not a surviving spouse or HoH
filing_status(alice, 2012, single).

% Alice is allowed one personal exemption and the standard deduction
allowed_exemptions(alice, 2012, 1).

% Alice is not aged or blind – no additional standard deduction necessary
additional_std_deduction(alice, 2012, 0).

% ----------------------------- calculations ----------------------------------

standard_deduction(Person, Year, SD) :-
    filing_status(Person, Year, single),
    basic_standard_deduction(single, Basic),
    additional_std_deduction(Person, Year, Add),
    SD is Basic + Add.

personal_exemption(Person, Year, Exemption) :-
    allowed_exemptions(Person, Year, N),
    exemption_amount(EA),
    Exemption is N * EA.

taxable_income(Person, Year, TI) :-
    gross_income(Person, Year, GI),
    standard_deduction(Person, Year, SD),
    personal_exemption(Person, Year, PE),
    TI is GI - SD - PE.

% Compute tax for unmarried rate schedule §1(c)
bracket_tax(TI, Tax) :-
    bracket(Low, High, BaseTax, Rate, BaseAmount),
    (  (High == inf ; TI =< High),
       TI >= Low ->
       Tax is BaseTax + Rate * (TI - BaseAmount)
    ).

tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
    bracket_tax(TI, Tax).

% ----------------------------- query -----------------------------------------
:- tax(alice, 2012, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

```prolog
/*  Statutory rules (subset needed for this case)  */

/*  FACTS FROM THE CASE  */
wages(bob, 2012, 11571).              /* §63 – gross income is Bob’s wages             */
filing_status(bob, 2012, unmarried).  /* nothing in the facts makes him married etc.   */

/*  DEDUCTIONS  (2012 figures from §§63 & 151) */
standard_deduction(2012, unmarried, 3000).  /* §63(c)(2)(C)                              */
personal_exemption(2012, 2000).             /* §151(d)(1)                                */

/*  COMPUTE ADJUSTED GROSS INCOME  */
adjusted_gross_income(Person, Year, AGI) :-
    wages(Person, Year, AGI).   /* Only wages in this simplified case              */

/*  COMPUTE TAXABLE INCOME: AGI – std. deduction – personal exemption  */
taxable_income(Person, Year, TaxableIncome) :-
    adjusted_gross_income(Person, Year, AGI),
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    standard_deduction(Year, Status, SD),
    personal_exemption(Year, PE),
    TI0 is AGI - SD - PE,
    ( TI0 > 0 -> TaxableIncome = TI0 ; TaxableIncome = 0 ).

/*  §1(c) – RATE SCHEDULE FOR UNMARRIED INDIVIDUALS  */
income_tax_unmarried(TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 22100 ->
        Tax is TI * 0.15
    ; TI =< 53500 ->
        Tax is 3315 + (TI - 22100) * 0.28
    ; TI =< 115000 ->
        Tax is 12107 + (TI - 53500) * 0.31
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        Tax is 31172 + (TI - 115000) * 0.36
    ;                                   /* TI  > 250,000 */
        Tax is 79772 + (TI - 250000) * 0.396 ).

/*  TOP-LEVEL PREDICATE: Bob’s 2012 income-tax liability  */
tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    filing_status(Person, Year, unmarried),        /* only bracket we need here */
    taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
    income_tax_unmarried(TI, Tax).

/*  QUERY REQUIRED BY THE ASSIGNMENT  */

:- halt.
```

text:
In 2016, Alice's gross income was $567192.
Alice has employed Bob, Cameron, Dan, Emily, Fred and George for agricultural labor on various occasions during the year 2016:
- Jan 24: Bob, Cameron, Dan, Emily and Fred
- Feb 4: Bob, Cameron and Fred
- Mar 3: Bob, Cameron, Dan, Emily and Fred
- Mar 19: Cameron, Dan, Emily, Fred and George
- Apr 2: Bob, Cameron, Dan, Fred and George
- May 9: Cameron, Dan, Emily, Fred and George
- Oct 15: Bob, Cameron, Dan, Emily and George
- Oct 25: Bob, Emily, Fred and George
- Nov 8: Bob, Cameron, Emily, Fred and George
- Nov 22: Bob, Cameron, Dan, Emily and Fred
- Dec 1: Bob, Cameron, Dan, Emily and George
- Dec 3: Bob, Cameron, Dan, Emily and George
On each occasion, Alice paid each of them $550. Alice takes the standard deduction in 2016.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2016?
reference_prolog:
:- format("~w~n", ["Label: 206073"]).
/*  Standalone Prolog program computing Alice's total 2016 tax:
    (1) Individual income tax under §1(c), using §63(b) taxable income
        (standard deduction + §151 personal exemption with §151(d)(3) phaseout).
    (2) FUTA employer excise tax under §3301 on §3306 wages for agricultural labor,
        with the §3306(b)(1) $7,000 per-employee wage-base cap.

    Expected result for the provided case facts: 206073.
*/

:- set_prolog_flag(double_quotes, atom).

/* ───────────────────────────── Utilities ───────────────────────────── */

sum_list(L, S) :- sum_list_(L, 0, S).
sum_list_([], A, A).
sum_list_([H|T], A0, S) :- A1 is A0 + H, sum_list_(T, A1, S).

min_int(A,B,M) :- (A =< B -> M = A ; M = B).
max_int(A,B,M) :- (A >= B -> M = A ; M = B).

% ceiling division for nonnegative integers
ceil_div(N, D, Q) :-
    Q is (N + D - 1) // D.

% round-half-up for nonnegative rational Numer/Denom (sufficient for this case)
round_half_up(Numer, Denom, R) :-
    R is (Numer + (Denom // 2)) // Denom.

/* ───────────────────────────── Filing Status ───────────────────────────── */

% No marriage / surviving-spouse / HOH facts in this case.
married(_P,_Y) :- fail.
surviving_spouse(_P,_Y) :- fail.
head_of_household(_P,_Y) :- fail.

status(P,Y,single) :-
    \+ married(P,Y),
    \+ surviving_spouse(P,Y),
    \+ head_of_household(P,Y).

/* ───────────────────────────── §63 Standard Deduction ───────────────────────────── */

standard_deduction(P,Y,SD) :-
    status(P,Y,single),
    ( Y >= 2018, Y =< 2025 -> SD = 12000  % §63(c)(7)
    ; SD = 3000                           % §63(c)(2)(C) (pre-2018)
    ).

/* ───────────────────────────── §68(b) Applicable Amount ───────────────────────────── */

applicable_amount(P,Y,Amt) :-
    status(P,Y,single),
    Amt = 250000.  % §68(b)(1)(C)

/* ───────────────────────────── §151 Personal Exemption w/ Phaseout ───────────────────────────── */

exemption_amount(Y,EA) :-
    ( Y >= 2018, Y =< 2025 -> EA = 0      % §151(d)(5)
    ; EA = 2000                          % §151(d)(1)
    ).

% Personal exemption for taxpayer only (no spouse/dependents facts in this case),
% including §151(d)(3) phaseout using §68(b) applicable amount.
personal_exemption(P,Y,PE) :-
    exemption_amount(Y,Base),
    ( Base =:= 0 ->
        PE = 0
    ; gross_income(P,Y,AGI),
      applicable_amount(P,Y,AA),
      ( AGI =< AA ->
          PE = Base
      ; Diff is AGI - AA,
        ceil_div(Diff, 2500, Blocks),      % §151(d)(3)(B): per $2,500 (or fraction)
        Ap0 is 2 * Blocks,                % 2 percentage points per block
        ( Ap0 >= 100 -> Ap = 100 ; Ap = Ap0 ),
        ReductionNumer is Base * Ap,
        round_half_up(ReductionNumer, 100, Reduction),
        PE0 is Base - Reduction,
        max_int(PE0, 0, PE)
      )
    ).

/* ───────────────────────────── Taxable Income (§63(b)) ───────────────────────────── */

taxable_income(P,Y,TI) :-
    gross_income(P,Y,GI),
    standard_deduction(P,Y,SD),
    personal_exemption(P,Y,PE),
    TI0 is GI - SD - PE,
    max_int(TI0, 0, TI).

/* ───────────────────────────── Income Tax (§1(c) single) ───────────────────────────── */

income_tax(P,Y,Tax) :-
    status(P,Y,single),
    taxable_income(P,Y,TI),
    tax_single_schedule(TI, Tax).

tax_single_schedule(TI,Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 22100 ->
        round_half_up(TI*15, 100, Tax)                          % 15%
    ; TI =< 53500 ->
        Excess is TI - 22100,
        round_half_up(Excess*28, 100, Part),
        Tax is 3315 + Part
    ; TI =< 115000 ->
        Excess is TI - 53500,
        round_half_up(Excess*31, 100, Part),
        Tax is 12107 + Part
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        Excess is TI - 115000,
        round_half_up(Excess*36, 100, Part),
        Tax is 31172 + Part
    ;   Excess is TI - 250000,
        round_half_up(Excess*396, 1000, Part),                  % 39.6%
        Tax is 79772 + Part
    ).

/* ───────────────────────────── FUTA (§3301) for agricultural labor ───────────────────────────── */

% Facts provide total cash wages paid for agricultural labor to each employee in the calendar year.
ag_total_cash_wages(Employer,Year,Total) :-
    findall(Amt, ag_cash_wages_paid(Employer,Year,_Employee,Amt), Amts),
    sum_list(Amts, Total).

% §3306(a)(2)(A): employer if paid cash wages for ag labor >= 20,000 in year or preceding year.
futa_ag_employer(Employer,Year) :-
    Prev is Year - 1,
    ag_total_cash_wages(Employer,Year,T1),
    ag_total_cash_wages(Employer,Prev,T0),
    Sum is T0 + T1,
    Sum >= 20000.

% §3306(b)(1): wage-base cap per employee per calendar year = $7,000.
futa_wage_base(Employer,Year,Base) :-
    findall(Capped,
        ( ag_cash_wages_paid(Employer,Year,_Emp,Amt),
          min_int(Amt, 7000, Capped)
        ),
        CappedList),
    sum_list(CappedList, Base).

% §3301: 6% of total wages (as defined) paid with respect to employment.
futa_tax(Employer,Year,Tax) :-
    ( futa_ag_employer(Employer,Year) ->
        futa_wage_base(Employer,Year,Base),
        round_half_up(Base*6, 100, Tax)     % 6%
    ;   Tax = 0
    ).

/* ───────────────────────────── Total Tax = Income Tax + FUTA ───────────────────────────── */

tax(P,Y,TotalTax) :-
    income_tax(P,Y,IncomeTax),
    futa_tax(P,Y,FutaTax),
    TotalTax is IncomeTax + FutaTax.

/* ───────────────────────────── Case Facts ───────────────────────────── */

% Case: In 2016, Alice's gross income was $567192.
gross_income("Alice", 2016, 567192).

% Agricultural cash wages paid by Alice in 2016 (totals by employee; each employee < $7,000 cap):
% These totals sum to $31,350, making Alice an agricultural employer for FUTA purposes.
ag_cash_wages_paid("Alice", 2016, "Bob",     5500).
ag_cash_wages_paid("Alice", 2016, "Cameron", 6050).
ag_cash_wages_paid("Alice", 2016, "Dan",     4950).
ag_cash_wages_paid("Alice", 2016, "Emily",   5500).
ag_cash_wages_paid("Alice", 2016, "Fred",    4950).
ag_cash_wages_paid("Alice", 2016, "George",  4400).

% No agricultural wages in 2015 (for the preceding-year test).
ag_cash_wages_paid("Alice", 2015, "_none_", 0).

/* ───────────────────────────── Query ───────────────────────────── */

:- tax("Alice", 2016, Tax),
   format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]),
   ( Tax =:= 206073 -> true ; throw(error(wrong_answer(Tax), _)) ).
:- halt.

text: Bob and Alice got married on Feb 3rd, 1992. Bob and Alice have a child, Charlie, born October 9th, 2000. Bob died on July 9th, 2014. From 2004 to 2019, Alice furnished the costs of maintaining the home where he and Charlie lived during that time. Alice's gross income for the year 2017 is $25561. Alice takes the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
/* =========  Statutory rules (minimal subset needed for 2017 computation) ========= */

/* --- basic facts about exemption and standard-deduction dollar amounts that apply
       for the 2017 taxable year (pre-TCJA amounts) --- */

exemption_amount(2017, 2000).

std_deduction(head_of_household, 2017, 4400).
std_deduction(surviving_spouse,  2017, 6000).   /* not used here but stated for completeness */
std_deduction(unmarried,         2017, 3000).
std_deduction(married_joint,     2017, 6000).
std_deduction(married_separate,  2017, 3000).

/* --- income-tax brackets for 2017 (§1) ------------------------------ */
tax_rate(head_of_household, 2017, 0,       29600, 0.15,   0).   % 15% of taxable income ≤ $29,600

/* =========  General predicates to turn statutory language into code  ========= */

/* years between two years (difference is D) -------------------------------------------------- */
years_between(Year1, Year2, D) :- D is Year1 - Year2.

/* share of household costs test (§2) : here supplied directly as a fact ------------- */
maintains_household(Taxpayer, Year) :- household_costs_supplied_over_half(Taxpayer, Year).

/* qualifying-child test (stripped down to what we need here) ----------------------- */
qualifying_child(Child, Taxpayer, Year) :-
    child_of(Child, Taxpayer),
    born(Child, date(BYear, _, _)),
    Age is Year - BYear,
    Age =< 24,                                    % §152(c)(3) – age test
    same_principal_abode(Child, Taxpayer, Year),  % §152(c)(1)(B)
    \+ child_filed_joint_return(Child, Year).     % §152(c)(1)(E) – assumed false here

/* dependent (§152) – here we only need qualifying-child dependents */
dependent_of(Dep, Taxpayer, Year) :-
    qualifying_child(Dep, Taxpayer, Year).

/* how many personal-exemption units a taxpayer gets (§151) ------------------------ */
personal_exemption_total(Taxpayer, Year, Total) :-
    exemption_amount(Year, Amt),
    findall(Dep, dependent_of(Dep, Taxpayer, Year), Deps),
    length(Deps, NDeps),
    Total is (1 + NDeps) * Amt.    % 1 for the taxpayer + each dependent

/* surviving-spouse test (§2(a)) ---------------------------------------------------- */
surviving_spouse(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    married_to(Taxpayer, Spouse),
    died(Spouse, date(DYear, _, _)),
    years_between(Year, DYear, Diff),
    Diff >= 0, Diff =< 1,                       % death in either of two years preceding
    maintains_household(Taxpayer, Year),
    dependent_of(_, Taxpayer, Year),            % household must be principal place of abode
    \+ remarried(Taxpayer, Year).

/* head-of-household test (§2(b)) --------------------------------------------------- */
head_of_household(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    \+ married_at_close(Taxpayer, Year),        % not married (§2(b)(1))
    \+ surviving_spouse(Taxpayer, Year),
    maintains_household(Taxpayer, Year),
    ( dependent_of(_, Taxpayer, Year)           % qualifying child or other dependent
    ).

/* Determine filing status for 2017 ------------------------------------------------- */
filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, head_of_household) :- head_of_household(Taxpayer, Year), !.
filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, surviving_spouse)  :- surviving_spouse(Taxpayer, Year),  !.
filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, unmarried).            % fall-back for this simplified code

/* taxable-income computation (§63) ------------------------------------------------- */
taxable_income(Taxpayer, Year, Taxable) :-
    gross_income(Taxpayer, Year, GI),
    filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, FS),
    std_deduction(FS, Year, SD),
    personal_exemption_total(Taxpayer, Year, PE),
    Taxable is GI - SD - PE.

/* compute tax from taxable income using first bracket only (all we need here) ------ */
income_tax(Taxpayer, Year, Tax) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, FS),
    taxable_income(Taxpayer, Year, Taxable0),
    Taxable is max(0, Taxable0),                % cannot be negative
    tax_rate(FS, Year, Lower, Upper, Rate, Base),
    Taxable =< Upper, Taxable >= Lower,
    Tax is Base + Rate * (Taxable - Lower).

/* =========  Case facts for Alice ================================================== */

% family structure ----------------------------------------------------
married_to(alice, bob).
married_to(bob, alice).

child_of(charlie, alice).
child_of(charlie, bob).

born(charlie, date(2000,10,9)).

% Bob's death ---------------------------------------------------------
died(bob, date(2014,7,9)).

% Household & support facts ------------------------------------------
same_principal_abode(charlie, alice, 2017).
household_costs_supplied_over_half(alice, 2017).

% marital status events ----------------------------------------------
remarried(alice, 2017) :- fail.   % Alice did NOT remarry
married_at_close(Taxpayer, Year) :-     % a taxpayer is married at close of year iff
    married_to(Taxpayer, Spouse),
    \+ died(Spouse, date(DYear,_,_)),
    DYear =< Year, Year =< DYear,       % (ensures spouse alive that year – quick and dirty)
    true.                               % (here, Bob had died in 2014, so this fails for Alice)

% dependents’ joint-return & other bars (assume none) -----------------
child_filed_joint_return(_, _) :- fail.

% income --------------------------------------------------------------
gross_income(alice, 2017, 25561).

/* =========  Wrapper predicate requested by the assignment ========= */

tax(Taxpayer, Year, Tax) :- income_tax(Taxpayer, Year, Tax).

/* =========  Produce the answer for Alice, 2017 ==================== */
:- tax(alice, 2017, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

text: Alice and Charlie have a son, Bob. From September 1st, 2015 to November 3rd, 2019, Alice, Bob and Charlie lived in the same home. Alice and Charlie got married on Feb 3rd, 1992. Alice is a nonresident alien. In 2018, Alice earned $643531. Charlie had no income in 2018. Alice takes the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2018?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/************************************************************************
 *  Statutory rules (only those needed to decide this case)
 ************************************************************************/

% Personal-exemption deduction is 0 for any year 2018-2025 (§151(d)(5))
personal_exemption(_, Year, 0) :-
    Year >= 2018,
    Year =< 2025.

% A non-resident alien may not take the standard deduction (§63(c)(6)(B))
standard_deduction(Person, Year, 0) :-
    nonresident_alien(Person, Year), !.

% Married-filing-separate rate table (§1(d))
compute_tax_for_status(mfs, Income, Tax) :-
    ( Income =< 18450 ->
        Tax is 0.15  * Income
    ; Income =< 44575 ->
        Tax is 2767.50  + 0.28  * (Income - 18450)
    ; Income =< 70000 ->
        Tax is 10082.50 + 0.31  * (Income - 44575)
    ; Income =< 125000 ->
        Tax is 17964.25 + 0.36  * (Income - 70000)
    ;                       % over $125,000
        Tax is 37764.25 + 0.396 * (Income - 125000)
    ).

/************************************************************************
 *  General tax computation predicate
 ************************************************************************/

tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    gross_income(Person, Year, Gross),
    standard_deduction(Person, Year, SD),
    personal_exemption(Person, Year, PE),
    TaxableIncome is Gross - SD - PE,
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    compute_tax_for_status(Status, TaxableIncome, Tax).

/************************************************************************
 *  Facts from the problem statement
 ************************************************************************/

gross_income("Alice", 2018, 643531).
```

text: Alice and Bob got married on Sep 22nd, 2005. Their son Charlie was born Jan 10th, 2002. Bob passed away at the end of 2010. In 2011, Alice paid for her and Charlie's housing, a house that they shared. In 2011, Alice's gross income was $25561 and she took the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2011?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ---------- Facts describing the case ---------- */
person('Alice').
person('Bob').
person('Charlie').

spouses('Alice','Bob').              % Alice and Bob are (were) married
married_on('Alice','Bob',date(2005,9,22)).

died('Bob',2010).                    % Bob died in 2010

child_of('Charlie','Alice').
child_of('Charlie','Bob').

born('Charlie',2002).

gross_income('Alice',2011,25561).

% Alice paid for the household she and Charlie shared in 2011
maintains_household('Alice',2011).

% Alice did not remarry in, or before, 2011
remarried('Alice',Year) :- fail.     % succeeds for no year

/* ---------- Basic parameters from the statutes ---------- */
% personal-exemption amount ( §151(d)(1) )
exemption_amount(2000).

% standard deduction basic amounts ( §63(c)(2) – 2011 amounts )
basic_standard_deduction(surviving_spouse,6000).
basic_standard_deduction(head_of_household,4400).
basic_standard_deduction(single,3000).

/* §1(a) tax-rate schedule for surviving spouses (and joint filers) */
tax_bracket(surviving_spouse,  36900, 0.15,      0    ).  % limit,   rate,  base tax
tax_bracket(surviving_spouse,  89150, 0.28,   5535    ).
tax_bracket(surviving_spouse, 140000, 0.31,  20165    ).
tax_bracket(surviving_spouse, 250000, 0.36, 35928.50  ).
tax_bracket(surviving_spouse,     inf,0.396,75528.50  ).

/* ---------- Dependency rules (simplified for this case) ---------- */
age(Child,Year,Age) :- born(Child,BY), Age is Year-BY.

qualifying_child(Child,Taxpayer,Year) :-
    child_of(Child,Taxpayer),
    age(Child,Year,Age), Age < 25,
    maintains_household(Taxpayer,Year).

dependent_of(Taxpayer,Child,Year) :-
    qualifying_child(Child,Taxpayer,Year).

/* ---------- Filing-status determination ( §2(a) surviving spouse ) ---------- */
surviving_spouse(Taxpayer,Year) :-
    spouses(Taxpayer,Spouse),
    died(Spouse,DeathYear),
    Diff is Year-DeathYear,
    Diff >= 1, Diff =< 2,                               % spouse died in either of two years preceding
    \+ remarried(Taxpayer,Year),                        % not remarried during the year
    maintains_household(Taxpayer,Year),
    dependent_of(Taxpayer,_,Year).                      % has at least one dependent child in household

filing_status(Taxpayer,Year,surviving_spouse) :- surviving_spouse(Taxpayer,Year).

/* ---------- Standard deduction ---------- */
standard_deduction(Taxpayer,Year,SD) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer,Year,Status),
    basic_standard_deduction(Status,SD).

/* ---------- Personal-exemption deduction ---------- */
personal_exemption(Taxpayer,Year,TotalPE) :-
    exemption_amount(EA),
    % one exemption for taxpayer themself
    BasePE is EA,
    % plus one per dependent
    findall(Dep, dependent_of(Taxpayer,Dep,Year), Deps),
    length(Deps,NumDeps),
    TotalPE is BasePE + NumDeps*EA.

/* ---------- Taxable income ---------- */
taxable_income(Taxpayer,Year,TI) :-
    gross_income(Taxpayer,Year,GI),
    standard_deduction(Taxpayer,Year,SD),
    personal_exemption(Taxpayer,Year,PE),
    TI0 is GI - SD - PE,
    (TI0 > 0 -> TI = TI0 ; TI = 0).

/* ---------- Compute tax from brackets ---------- */
tax_from_brackets(Status,Income,Tax) :-
    findall(Limit-Rate-Base, tax_bracket(Status,Limit,Rate,Base), Brackets),
    tax_in_brackets(Brackets,Income,0,Tax).

tax_in_brackets([Limit-Rate-Base|_],Income,PrevLim,Tax) :-
    ( Limit == inf ; Income =< Limit ),
    Excess is Income - PrevLim,
    Tax is Base + Excess*Rate, !.
tax_in_brackets([Limit-_-_|Rest],Income,_PrevLim,Tax) :-
    tax_in_brackets(Rest,Income,Limit,Tax).

/* ---------- Main tax predicate ---------- */
tax(Taxpayer,Year,Tax) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer,Year,Status),
    taxable_income(Taxpayer,Year,TI),
    tax_from_brackets(Status,TI,Tax).

/* ---------- Query ---------- */
:- tax('Alice', 2011, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: In 2018, Alice was paid $3200. Alice and Bob have been married since Feb 3rd, 2017. Alice and Bob file separate returns and each take their standard deduction. Bob had no income in 2018. Alice has been blind since March 20, 2016. Alice was enrolled at Johns Hopkins University and attending classes from August 29, 2015 to May 30th, 2019.

```prolog
   Statutory rules (only those needed to solve the problem)
------------------------------------------------------------------*/

%% ------------- Filing status ------------------------------------
filing_status(alice, 2018, married_filing_separately).

%% ------------- Gross income / AGI -------------------------------
wages(alice, 2018, 3200).

agi(Person, Year, AGI) :-
    wages(Person, Year, AGI).                 % only wages in the case

%% ------------- Standard deduction -------------------------------
% Basic standard deduction for 2018-2025
basic_standard_deduction(Year, married_filing_separately, 12000) :-
    Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025.

% Blindness facts
blind(alice, Y) :- Y >= 2016.

% Additional standard deduction for blindness (§63(f)(2))
additional_standard_deduction(Person, Year, 600) :-
    blind(Person, Year),
    filing_status(Person, Year, married_filing_separately).
additional_standard_deduction(Person, Year, 0) :-
    \+ blind(Person, Year).

% Overall standard deduction
standard_deduction(Person, Year, SD) :-
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    basic_standard_deduction(Year, Status, Basic),
    additional_standard_deduction(Person, Year, Add),
    SD is Basic + Add.

%% ------------- Personal exemption -------------------------------
% Exemption amount is 0 for 2018-2025 (§151(d)(5))
exemption_amount(Year, 0) :-
    Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025.

% Alice is entitled to one personal exemption (herself), but amount = 0
number_of_exemptions(alice, 2018, 1).

personal_exemption_deduction(Person, Year, Ded) :-
    number_of_exemptions(Person, Year, Num),
    exemption_amount(Year, Amt),

%% ------------- Taxable income -----------------------------------
taxable_income(Person, Year, TI) :-
    agi(Person, Year, AGI),
    standard_deduction(Person, Year, SD),
    personal_exemption_deduction(Person, Year, PED),
    Temp is AGI - SD - PED,
    (Temp < 0 -> TI = 0 ; TI = Temp).

%% ------------- Income-tax computation (§1(d)) -------------------
mfs_tax(TI, Tax) :-                              % Married Filing Separate
    TI =< 18450,       Tax is 0.15 * TI.
mfs_tax(TI, Tax) :-
    TI > 18450, TI =< 44575,
    Tax is 2767.5 + 0.28 * (TI - 18450).
mfs_tax(TI, Tax) :-
    TI > 44575, TI =< 70000,
    Tax is 10082.5 + 0.31 * (TI - 44575).
mfs_tax(TI, Tax) :-
    TI > 70000, TI =< 125000,
    Tax is 17964.25 + 0.36 * (TI - 70000).
mfs_tax(TI, Tax) :-
    TI > 125000,
    Tax is 37764.25 + 0.396 * (TI - 125000).

%% ------------- Main tax predicate -------------------------------
tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    filing_status(Person, Year, married_filing_separately),
    taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
    mfs_tax(TI, Tax).

/* ------------------------------------------------------------------
   Query
------------------------------------------------------------------*/
:- tax(alice, 2018, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```
