text: Alice and Bob got married on April 5th, 2012. Alice and Bob have a son, Charlie, who was born on September 16th, 2017. Alice and Charlie live in a home for which Alice furnished 40% of the maintenance costs, and Bob the remaining 60%, since September 16th, 2017. Alice and Bob file jointly from 2017 to 2019. Alice and Bob's gross incomes in 2018 were $36991 and $41990 respectively. Alice and Bob take the standard deduction. From 2017 to 2019, Bob lived separately.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2018?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ---------- Statutory rules (relevant extracts only) ---------- */

/*  Standard deduction amounts – §63(c)(2) & (7)  */
standard_deduction(Year, married_filing_joint, 24000) :-
    Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025.     /* §63(c)(7) raises joint amount to $24,000 */

/*  Married-filing-joint rate schedule – §1(a)  */
mfj_bracket(0,      36900, 0.15, 0).                        % 15% up to $36,900
mfj_bracket(36900,  89150, 0.28, 5535).                    % 28% over 36,900
mfj_bracket(89150, 140000, 0.31, 20165).                   % 31% over 89,150
mfj_bracket(140000,250000, 0.36, 35928.50).                % 36% over 140,000
mfj_bracket(250000,inf,   0.396, 75528.50).                % 39.6% over 250,000

/*  Compute tax for married-filing-joint taxable income  */
mfj_tax(TI, Tax) :-
    mfj_bracket(Low, High, Rate, Base),
    TI > Low,
    ( High == inf ; TI =< High ),
    Tax is Base + Rate*(TI - Low),
    !.

/* ---------- Case-specific facts ---------- */

income("Alice", 2018, 36991).
income("Bob",   2018, 41990).

/*  Alice and Bob file a joint return for 2018  */
filing_status("Alice", 2018, married_filing_joint).
spouse("Alice", 2018, "Bob").

 /* ---------- Derived calculations ---------- */

adjusted_gross_income(Tp, Yr, AGI) :-
    filing_status(Tp, Yr, married_filing_joint),
    spouse(Tp, Yr, Sp),
    income(Tp, Yr, Inc1),
    income(Sp, Yr, Inc2),
    AGI is Inc1 + Inc2.

taxable_income(Tp, Yr, TI) :-
    adjusted_gross_income(Tp, Yr, AGI),
    filing_status(Tp, Yr, married_filing_joint),
    standard_deduction(Yr, married_filing_joint, SD),
    TI is AGI - SD.

tax(Tp, Yr, Tax) :-
    taxable_income(Tp, Yr, TI),
    mfj_tax(TI, Tax).

/* ---------- Query ---------- */
:- tax("Alice", 2018, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice and Bob got married on Feb 3rd, 1992. Alice died on July 9th, 2014. From 2015 to 2019, Bob furnished the costs of maintaining the home where he and his friend Charlie lived during that time, while Charlie had no income and was not the qualifying child of any taxpayer. Bob earned $304598 every year from 2015 to 2019.
question: How much tax does Bob have to pay in 2018?
reference_prolog:
/* -------------  Facts from the case ------------- */
income(bob, 2018, 304598).

spouse_of(bob, alice, date(1992,2,3)).
spouse_death(alice, 2014).

% Bob and Charlie share a home 2015-2019, Bob pays the costs
maintains_household(bob, charlie, Year) :-
    member(Year,[2015,2016,2017,2018,2019]).

% Charlie is Bob's dependent in those same years (§152(d)(2)(H))
dependent_of(bob, charlie, Year) :-
    maintains_household(bob, charlie, Year).

qualifies_by_H(bob, charlie, Year) :-  % qualifies only under §152(d)(2)(H)
    dependent_of(bob, charlie, Year).

/* -------------  Status rules (simplified to what we need) ------------- */
spouse_alive(Person, Year) :-
    spouse_of(Person, Spouse, _),
    ( \+ spouse_death(Spouse, _) ;  (spouse_death(Spouse,DY), DY > Year) ).

married(Person, Year) :- spouse_alive(Person, Year).      % §7703(a)

% No dependent child ⇒ never a surviving spouse for Bob
surviving_spouse(Person, Year) :-
    spouse_of(Person, Spouse, _),
    spouse_death(Spouse, DY),
    (Year-DY =:= 1 ; Year-DY =:= 2),
    fail.   % (no qualifying child)                       §2(a)

% Head-of-household disallowed when the only dependent is via §152(d)(2)(H)
head_of_household(Person, Year) :-
    \+ married(Person, Year),
    \+ surviving_spouse(Person, Year),
    maintains_household(Person, Dep, Year),
    dependent_of(Person, Dep, Year),
    \+ qualifies_by_H(Person, Dep, Year).                 % §2(b)(3)(B)

status(Person, Year, married_joint)       :- married(Person, Year).
status(Person, Year, surviving_spouse)    :- surviving_spouse(Person, Year).
status(Person, Year, head_of_household)   :- head_of_household(Person, Year).
status(Person, Year, single) :-            % §1(c)
    \+ status(Person, Year, married_joint),
    \+ status(Person, Year, surviving_spouse),
    \+ status(Person, Year, head_of_household).

/* -------------  Deductions (2018–2025 rules) ------------- */
standard_deduction_by_status(married_joint,  _, 24000).
standard_deduction_by_status(surviving_spouse,_, 24000).
standard_deduction_by_status(head_of_household,_, 18000).
standard_deduction_by_status(single,         _, 12000).   % §63(c)(7)

standard_deduction(Person, Year, SD) :-
    Year>=2018, Year=<2025,
    status(Person, Year, Status),
    standard_deduction_by_status(Status, Year, SD).

personal_exemption(_, Year, 0) :-        % §151(d)(5)
    Year>=2018, Year=<2025.

/* -------------  Taxable income ------------- */
taxable_income(Person, Year, TI) :-
    income(Person, Year, GI),
    standard_deduction(Person, Year, SD),
    personal_exemption(Person, Year, PE),
    TI is GI - SD - PE.

/* -------------  §1(c) single rate schedule ------------- */
single_tax(TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 22100 ->                 Tax is 0.15 * TI
    ; TI =< 53500 ->                 Tax is 3315   + 0.28 * (TI-22100)
    ; TI =< 115000 ->                Tax is 12107  + 0.31 * (TI-53500)
    ; TI =< 250000 ->                Tax is 31172  + 0.36 * (TI-115000)
    ;                                 Tax is 79772 + 0.396* (TI-250000)
    ).

/* -------------  Overall tax ------------- */
tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    status(Person, Year, single),         % Bob is single in 2018
    taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
    single_tax(TI, Tax).

/* -------------  Query required by the task ------------- */
:- tax(bob, 2018, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

text:
Alice got married on June 2nd, 2006. Alice files a joint return with her spouse for 2017. Alice's and her spouse's gross income for the year 2017 is $684642. They take the standard deduction in 2017.
Alice has employed Bob, Cameron, Dan, Emily, Fred and George for agricultural labor on various occasions during the year 2017:
- Jan 24: Bob, Cameron, Dan, Emily and Fred
- Feb 4: Bob, Cameron and Fred
- Mar 3: Bob, Cameron, Dan, Emily and Fred
- Mar 19: Cameron, Dan, Emily, Fred and George
- Apr 2: Bob, Cameron, Dan, Fred and George
- May 9: Cameron, Dan, Emily, Fred and George
- Oct 15: Bob, Cameron, Dan, Emily and George
- Oct 25: Bob, Emily, Fred and George
- Nov 8: Bob, Cameron, Emily, Fred and George
- Nov 22: Bob, Cameron, Dan, Emily and Fred
- Dec 1: Bob, Cameron, Dan, Emily and George
- Dec 3: Bob, Cameron, Dan, Emily and George
Alice has paid each $632 on each occasion.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
:- format("~w~n", ["Label: 247432"]).
/*  Standalone Prolog program computing Alice's total 2017 tax.
    Includes:
      - Income tax under §1(a) (joint return)
      - Personal-exemption phaseout under §151(d)(3) using §68(b) threshold
      - FUTA excise tax under §3301 / §3306 on agricultural wages

    Expected result for this case: 247432
*/

:- set_prolog_flag(double_quotes, atom).

/* ───────────────────────────── Utilities ───────────────────────────── */

sum_list([], 0).
sum_list([H|T], S) :- sum_list(T, S0), S is H + S0.

day_year(Day, Year) :-
    split_string(Day, "-", "", [YS,_,_]),
    atom_number(YS, Year).

ceiling_div(Int, Div, Ceil) :-
    Ceil is (Int + Div - 1) // Div.

/* ───────────────────────── Filing status (minimal) ───────────────────────── */

spouse_of(alice, spouse).

joint_return(alice, spouse, 2017).

status(P, Y, married_joint) :-
    joint_return(P, _, Y).

/* ───────────────────────── Gross income / AGI ───────────────────────── */

% Case gives combined gross income for Alice and spouse
gross_income_joint(alice, 2017, 684642).

agi(P, Y, AGI) :-
    status(P, Y, married_joint),
    gross_income_joint(alice, Y, AGI).

/* ───────────────────────── §63 Standard deduction (2017) ───────────────────────── */

standard_deduction(married_joint, Y, 6000) :- Y < 2018.

/* ───────────────────────── §68(b) applicable amount (needed for §151 phaseout) ───────────────────────── */

applicable_amount(married_joint, _Y, 300000).

/* ───────────────────────── §151 Personal exemptions with phaseout ───────────────────────── */

exemption_amount(Y, 0) :- Y >= 2018, Y =< 2025, !.
exemption_amount(_Y, 2000).

% reduced_exemption_amount(Person, Year, ReducedEach)
reduced_exemption_amount(P, Y, Reduced) :-
    exemption_amount(Y, Base),
    ( Base =:= 0 ->
        Reduced = 0
    ; status(P, Y, S),
      agi(P, Y, AGI),
      applicable_amount(S, Y, AA),
      ( AGI =< AA ->
          Reduced = Base
      ; Diff is AGI - AA,
        ceiling_div(Diff, 2500, CeilSteps),
        Ap0 is 2 * CeilSteps,
        Ap is min(Ap0, 100),
        Reduction is round((Base * Ap) / 100),
        Reduced is max(Base - Reduction, 0)
      )
    ).

personal_exemptions(P, Y, TotalPE) :-
    status(P, Y, married_joint),
    reduced_exemption_amount(P, Y, Each),
    TotalPE is 2 * Each.   % taxpayer + spouse on joint return

/* ───────────────────────── Taxable income (standard deduction path) ───────────────────────── */

taxable_income(P, Y, TI) :-
    agi(P, Y, AGI),
    status(P, Y, S),
    standard_deduction(S, Y, SD),
    personal_exemptions(P, Y, PE),
    TI0 is AGI - SD - PE,
    TI is max(TI0, 0).

/* ───────────────────────── §1(a) Income tax (joint) ───────────────────────── */

income_tax_joint(TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 36900 ->
        Tax is round(0.15 * TI)
    ; TI =< 89150 ->
        Tax is round(5535 + 0.28 * (TI - 36900))
    ; TI =< 140000 ->
        Tax is round(20165 + 0.31 * (TI - 89150))
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        Tax is round(35928.50 + 0.36 * (TI - 140000))
    ;   Tax is round(75528.50 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000))
    ).

income_tax(P, Y, Tax) :-
    status(P, Y, married_joint),
    taxable_income(P, Y, TI),
    income_tax_joint(TI, Tax).

/* ───────────────────────── §3301 / §3306 FUTA (agricultural labor) ───────────────────────── */

% Agricultural work schedule in 2017 (each listed worker is paid $632 cash that day)
ag_day("2017-01-24", [bob,cameron,dan,emily,fred]).
ag_day("2017-02-04", [bob,cameron,fred]).
ag_day("2017-03-03", [bob,cameron,dan,emily,fred]).
ag_day("2017-03-19", [cameron,dan,emily,fred,george]).
ag_day("2017-04-02", [bob,cameron,dan,fred,george]).
ag_day("2017-05-09", [cameron,dan,emily,fred,george]).
ag_day("2017-10-15", [bob,cameron,dan,emily,george]).
ag_day("2017-10-25", [bob,emily,fred,george]).
ag_day("2017-11-08", [bob,cameron,emily,fred,george]).
ag_day("2017-11-22", [bob,cameron,dan,emily,fred]).
ag_day("2017-12-01", [bob,cameron,dan,emily,george]).
ag_day("2017-12-03", [bob,cameron,dan,emily,george]).

% Cash agricultural wage payment fact generator
ag_wage_payment(Employer, Employee, Day, Amount) :-
    Employer = alice,
    Amount = 632,
    ag_day(Day, Emps),
    member(Employee, Emps).

% §3306(a)(2)(A): agricultural employer if paid >= 20000 in (year or preceding year)
ag_employer_under_3306(Employer, Year) :-
    PYear is Year - 1,
    findall(Amt,
        ( ag_wage_payment(Employer, _E, Day, Amt),
          day_year(Day, Y0),
          (Y0 =:= Year ; Y0 =:= PYear)
        ),
        Amts),
    sum_list(Amts, Total),
    Total >= 20000.

cap7000(Sum, Capped) :- Capped is min(7000, Sum).

futa_tax(Employer, Year, Tax) :-
    ag_employer_under_3306(Employer, Year),
    % collect employees paid in Year
    findall(E,
        ( ag_wage_payment(Employer, E, Day, _),
          day_year(Day, Year)
        ),
        Emps0),
    sort(Emps0, Emps),
    % compute capped wages per employee for Year
    findall(Capped,
        ( member(E, Emps),
          findall(Amt,
              ( ag_wage_payment(Employer, E, Day, Amt),
                day_year(Day, Year)
              ),
              AmtsE),
          sum_list(AmtsE, SumE),
          cap7000(SumE, Capped)
        ),
        CappedWageList),
    sum_list(CappedWageList, TotalCappedWages),
    Tax is round(0.06 * TotalCappedWages).

/* ───────────────────────── Total tax (income + FUTA) ───────────────────────── */

tax(P, Y, TotalTax) :-
    income_tax(P, Y, IncomeTax),
    ( futa_tax(P, Y, FUTA) -> true ; FUTA = 0 ),
    TotalTax is IncomeTax + FUTA.

tax("Alice", Y, Tax) :- tax(alice, Y, Tax).

/* ───────────────────────── Query ───────────────────────── */

:- tax("Alice", 2017, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

text: Alice has paid $300 to Bob for domestic service done in her home from Feb 1st, 2010 to Sep 2nd, 2010, in Baltimore, Maryland, USA. In 2010, Alice was paid $33408. Alice is allowed itemized deductions of $680, $2102, $1993 and $4807.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2010?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ----------  Facts for the particular case  ---------- */
income(alice, 2010, 33408).                         % gross (and AGI, no adjustments)
itemized_deductions(alice, 2010, [680,2102,1993,4807]).
married(alice, 2010, no).
dependent_of_someone_else(alice, 2010, no).        % she is not another taxpayer’s dependent
surviving_spouse(alice, 2010, no).
head_of_household(alice, 2010, no).

/* ----------  Generic rules drawn from the statutes  ---------- */

/* Sum a list of numbers */
sum_list([],0).
sum_list([H|T],Sum) :- sum_list(T,S1), Sum is H+S1.

/* ------------------------------------------------------------------
   Filing-status determination – here we only need “single” (unmarried,
   not surviving spouse, not HoH).  Extendable for other statuses.
   ------------------------------------------------------------------ */
filing_status(Person, Year, single) :-
    married(Person, Year, no),
    surviving_spouse(Person, Year, no),
    head_of_household(Person, Year, no).

/* ----------  Standard deduction (§63(c)(2))  ---------- */
standard_deduction(single, 3000).      % 2010 amounts (not 2018-25 inflation)

/* ----------  Personal exemption (§151)  ---------- */
personal_exemption(Person, Year, 0) :-
    dependent_of_someone_else(Person, Year, yes), !.
personal_exemption(_Person, _Year, 2000).          % 2010 amount

/* ----------  Chosen deduction set (itemize vs. standard) ---------- */
chosen_deductions(Person, Year, DeductionAmount) :-
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    itemized_deductions(Person, Year, List), sum_list(List, Itemized),
    standard_deduction(Status, StdDed),
    ( Itemized > StdDed ->
        DeductionAmount = Itemized
    ; DeductionAmount = StdDed).

/* ----------  Taxable income (§63)  -------------------- */
taxable_income(Person, Year, Taxable) :-
    income(Person, Year, AGI),
    chosen_deductions(Person, Year, Deductions),
    personal_exemption(Person, Year, Exempt),
    Taxable is max(0, AGI - Deductions - Exempt).

/* ----------  Income-tax computation (§1)  -------------- */
income_tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    filing_status(Person, Year, single),
    taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
    ( TI =< 22100 ->
        Tax is TI * 0.15
    ; TI =< 53500 ->
        Tax is 3315 + 0.28 * (TI - 22100)
    ; TI =< 115000 ->
        Tax is 12107 + 0.31 * (TI - 53500)
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        Tax is 31172 + 0.36 * (TI - 115000)
    ;                          % over 250,000
        Tax is 79772 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000)
    ).

/* ----------  FUTA (employer) tax (§3301–§3306)  --------
   Alice paid only $300 (<$1,000) for domestic service,
   so she is **not** an “employer”; FUTA tax = 0.
   ------------------------------------------------------- */
futa_tax(_Person, _Year, 0).

/* ----------  Total federal tax owed  ------------------ */
tax(Person, Year, TotalTax) :-
    income_tax(Person, Year, IncomeTax),
    futa_tax(Person, Year, Futa),
    TotalTax is IncomeTax + Futa.

/* ---------------  Query demanded by the task  -------------------- */
:- tax(alice, 2010, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: Bob is Alice's father. Alice's gross income in 2015 is $311510. Bob has no income in 2015. Alice takes the standard deduction in 2015.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2015?
reference_prolog:
% Facts from the case
father(bob, alice).
gross_income(alice, 2015, 311510).
gross_income(bob, 2015, 0).
takes_standard_deduction(alice, 2015).

% Relationship for qualifying relative
relationship(taxpayer, Dep) :- father(Dep, taxpayer).
relationship(Taxpayer, Dep) :- father(Dep, Taxpayer).

% Qualifying child (not applicable here due to lack of facts)
qualifying_child(_, _, _) :- fail.

% Qualifying relative
qualifying_relative(Taxpayer, Dep, Year) :-
    relationship(Taxpayer, Dep),
    gross_income(Dep, Year, Income),
    Income =:= 0,
    \+ qualifying_child(Taxpayer, Dep, Year).

% Dependent
dependent(Taxpayer, Dep, Year) :-
    qualifying_child(Taxpayer, Dep, Year).
dependent(Taxpayer, Dep, Year) :-
    qualifying_relative(Taxpayer, Dep, Year).

% Count dependents
count_dependents(Taxpayer, Year, Count) :-
    findall(Dep, dependent(Taxpayer, Dep, Year), List),
    sort(List, Set),
    length(Set, Count).

% Filing status rules
married(_, _) :- fail.
surviving_spouse(_, _) :- fail.
head_of_household(_, _) :- fail.

filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, head) :-
    head_of_household(Taxpayer, Year), !.
filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, surviving) :-
    surviving_spouse(Taxpayer, Year), !.
filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, joint) :-
    married(Taxpayer, Year), !.
filing_status(_, _, single).

% Adjusted gross income
agi(Taxpayer, Year, AGI) :-
    gross_income(Taxpayer, Year, AGI).

% Exemption amount
exemption_amount(Year, Amount) :-
    (Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025 -> Amount = 0 ; Amount = 2000).

% Applicable amount for phaseout
applicable_amount(joint, 300000).
applicable_amount(surviving, 300000).
applicable_amount(head, 275000).
applicable_amount(single, 250000).
applicable_amount(separate, 150000).

step(separate, 1250).
step(_, 2500).

applicable_percentage(Status, AGI, Percent) :-
    applicable_amount(Status, Amt),
    (AGI =< Amt ->
        Percent = 0
    ;
        Excess is AGI - Amt,
        step(Status, Step),
        N is ceiling(Excess / Step),
        P0 is N * 2,
        (P0 > 100 -> Percent = 100 ; Percent = P0)
    ).

% Exemption deduction
exemption_count(Taxpayer, Year, Count) :-
    count_dependents(Taxpayer, Year, DepCount),
    Count is 1 + DepCount.

exemption_deduction(Taxpayer, Year, Total) :-
    exemption_amount(Year, Base),
    (Base =:= 0 -> Total = 0 ;
        agi(Taxpayer, Year, AGI),
        filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, Status),
        applicable_percentage(Status, AGI, Percent),
        Reduced is Base * (1 - Percent / 100),
        exemption_count(Taxpayer, Year, Count),
        Total is Reduced * Count).

% Standard deduction
basic_standard_deduction(Status, Year, Basic) :-
    (Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025 ->
        (Status = head -> Basic = 18000 ;
         Status = joint -> Basic = 24000 ;
         Status = surviving -> Basic = 24000 ;
         Basic = 12000)
    ;
        (Status = head -> Basic = 4400 ;
         Status = joint -> Basic = 6000 ;
         Status = surviving -> Basic = 6000 ;
         Basic = 3000)
    ).

standard_deduction(Taxpayer, Year, Amount) :-
    takes_standard_deduction(Taxpayer, Year),
    filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, Status),
    basic_standard_deduction(Status, Year, Basic),
    Amount is Basic.

% Taxable income
taxable_income(Taxpayer, Year, TI) :-
    agi(Taxpayer, Year, AGI),
    standard_deduction(Taxpayer, Year, SD),
    exemption_deduction(Taxpayer, Year, EX),
    TI is AGI - SD - EX.

% Tax brackets
tax_amount(single, TI, Tax) :-
    (TI =< 22100 -> Tax is 0.15 * TI ;
     TI =< 53500 -> Tax is 3315 + 0.28 * (TI - 22100) ;
     TI =< 115000 -> Tax is 12107 + 0.31 * (TI - 53500) ;
     TI =< 250000 -> Tax is 31172 + 0.36 * (TI - 115000) ;
     Tax is 79772 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000)).

tax_amount(head, TI, Tax) :-
    (TI =< 29600 -> Tax is 0.15 * TI ;
     TI =< 76400 -> Tax is 4440 + 0.28 * (TI - 29600) ;
     TI =< 127500 -> Tax is 17544 + 0.31 * (TI - 76400) ;
     TI =< 250000 -> Tax is 33385 + 0.36 * (TI - 127500) ;
     Tax is 77485 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000)).

% Final tax computation
tax(Taxpayer, Year, TaxRounded) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, Status),
    taxable_income(Taxpayer, Year, TI),
    tax_amount(Status, TI, Tax),
    TaxRounded is round(Tax * 100) / 100.

:- tax(alice, 2015, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

text: Alice's gross income for the year 2010 is $210204. Alice has paid $45252 to Bob for work done in the year 2010. In 2010, Alice has also paid $9832 into a retirement fund for Bob, and $5322 into health insurance for Charlie, who is Alice's father and has retired in 2009. Bob has no income in 2010. Alice takes the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2010?
reference_prolog:
/* =========  STATUTORY RULES  ========= */

% ---------- BASIC MONETARY AMOUNTS ----------
exemption_amount(2000).

standard_basic(joint,          6000).   % not used here, shown for completeness
standard_basic(surviving,      6000).
standard_basic(head_of_house,  4400).
standard_basic(single,         3000).
standard_basic(separate,       3000).

% ---------- INCOME-TAX BRACKETS FOR 2010 ----------
% tax_bracket(Status, LowerLimit, UpperLimitOrInf, BaseTax, MarginalRate).
tax_bracket(single, 0,       22100,     0,        0.15).
tax_bracket(single, 22100,   53500,     3315,     0.28).
tax_bracket(single, 53500,   115000,    12107,    0.31).
tax_bracket(single, 115000,  250000,    31172,    0.36).
tax_bracket(single, 250000,  inf,       79772,    0.396).

% ---------- FUTA ----------
futa_rate(0.06).
futa_wage_cap(7000).

/* =========  FACTS FROM THE CASE  ========= */

person(alice).
tax_year(2010).

% Gross income
gross_income(alice,2010, 210204).

% Payments to employee Bob
wages(alice,bob,2010,   45252).      % cash wages
wages(alice,bob,2010,   9832).       % retirement contribution – included in wages

% Health insurance for Charlie (father) – not wages
health_insurance_paid(alice,charlie,2010,5322).

% Information about dependents
dependent(alice,charlie,2010).   % Father Charlie qualifies
dependent_count(Person,Year,Count) :-
    findall(D, dependent(Person,D,Year), L),
    length(L,Count).

% Filing status (assume unmarried and not head-of-household)
filing_status(alice,2010,single).

/* =========  COMPUTATIONS  ========= */

% ---------- FUTA ----------
futa_wages(Person,Year,TotalCapWages) :-
    % sum capped wages over each employee
    setof(E, Amt^(wages(Person,E,Year,Amt)), Employees),
    findall(Cap,
            (member(E,Employees),
             findall(A, wages(Person,E,Year,A), List),
             sum_list(List,TotalEmp),
             futa_wage_cap(CapLimit),
             (TotalEmp >= CapLimit -> Cap = CapLimit ; Cap = TotalEmp)),
            Caps),
    sum_list(Caps,TotalCapWages).

futa_tax(Person,Year,Tax) :-
    futa_wages(Person,Year,WageBase),
    futa_rate(Rate),
    Tax is WageBase * Rate.

% ---------- STANDARD DEDUCTION ----------
standard_deduction(Person,Year,SD) :-
    filing_status(Person,Year,Status),
    standard_basic(Status,SD).

% ---------- PERSONAL EXEMPTIONS ----------
personal_exemptions(Person,Year,PE) :-
    dependent_count(Person,Year,DepCnt),
    exemption_amount(ExAmt),
    % +1 for taxpayer herself
    TotalExemptions is DepCnt + 1,
    PE is TotalExemptions * ExAmt.

% ---------- TAXABLE INCOME ----------
taxable_income(Person,Year,TI) :-
    gross_income(Person,Year,GI),
    standard_deduction(Person,Year,SD),
    personal_exemptions(Person,Year,PE),
    TI is GI - SD - PE.

% ---------- INCOME TAX ----------
income_tax(Person,Year,Tax) :-
    taxable_income(Person,Year,TI),
    filing_status(Person,Year,Status),
    compute_bracket_tax(Status,TI,Tax).

compute_bracket_tax(Status,TI,Tax) :-
    tax_bracket(Status,Lower,Upper,Base,Rate),
    ( (Upper == inf, TI > Lower)
    ; (TI =< Upper, TI > Lower) ),
    Excess is TI - Lower,
    Tax is Base + Rate * Excess, !.

% ---------- TOTAL TAX ----------
total_tax(Person,Year,TotalTax) :-
    income_tax(Person,Year,IncomeTax),
    futa_tax(Person,Year,FutaTax),
    TotalTax is IncomeTax + FutaTax.

tax(Person,Year,Tax) :- total_tax(Person,Year,Tax).

/* =========  QUERY  ========= */
:- tax(alice, 2010, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

text: Alice and Bob got married on Aug 6th, 2010. Their son Charlie was born February 2nd, 2012. Alice and Bob were legally separated under a decree of divorce on March 2nd, 2015. In 2017, Alice and Charlie live in a house maintained by Alice. Alice's gross income for the year 2017 is $9560. Alice takes the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
/*******************************
 *  Statutory rules (selected) *
 *******************************/

% standard_deduction(Status, Year, Amount)
standard_deduction(hoh, Year, 4400) :- Year =:= 2017.      % §63(c)(2)(B)

% exemption_amount(Year, Amount)
exemption_amount(2017, 2000).                              % §151(d)(1)

% tax_rate(Status, TaxableIncome, Tax)  (§1 rate tables)
tax_rate(hoh, TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 29600 ->
        Tax is TI * 0.15
    ; TI =< 76400 ->
        Tax is 4440  + 0.28 * (TI - 29600)
    ; TI =< 127500 ->
        Tax is 17544 + 0.31 * (TI - 76400)
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        Tax is 33385 + 0.36 * (TI - 127500)
    ;   Tax is 77485 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000)
    ).

/*******************************
 *       Case-specific facts   *
 *******************************/
person(alice).
person(charlie).

year(2017).

% income(Person, Year, GrossIncome)
income(alice, 2017, 9560).

% filing_status(Person, Year, Status)
% Alice qualifies as Head of Household under §2(b)
filing_status(alice, 2017, hoh).

% dependents(Taxpayer, Year, ListOfDependents)
dependents(alice, 2017, [charlie]).

/*******************************
 *       Derived amounts       *
 *******************************/

% personal_exemption_total(Person, Year, Amount)
personal_exemption_total(P, Y, Total) :-
    exemption_amount(Y, EA),
    dependents(P, Y, Deps),
    length(Deps, NDeps),
    Count is NDeps + 1,          % taxpayer herself + dependents
    Total is EA * Count.

% standard_deduction for given person/year
standard_deduction_person(P, Y, SD) :-
    filing_status(P, Y, Status),
    standard_deduction(Status, Y, SD).

% taxable_income(Person, Year, TaxableIncome)
taxable_income(P, Y, TI) :-
    income(P, Y, GI),
    standard_deduction_person(P, Y, SD),
    personal_exemption_total(P, Y, PE),
    TI0 is GI - SD - PE,
    (TI0 < 0 -> TI = 0 ; TI = TI0).

% top-level tax predicate
tax(P, Y, Tax) :-
    taxable_income(P, Y, TI),
    filing_status(P, Y, Status),
    tax_rate(Status, TI, Tax).

/*******************************
 *            Query            *
 *******************************/
:- tax(alice, 2017, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2003?

/* ---------- Statutory rules (minimal fragment needed for this case) ---------- */

/* standard_deduction(FilingStatus, Amount) */
standard_deduction(joint, 6000).

/* exemption_amount(Amount) */
exemption_amount(2000).

/* exemptions(Person, Spouse, Year, TotalExemptions) ––
   On a joint return, each spouse gets one personal exemption (§151). */
exemptions(_Person, _Spouse, _Year, Total) :-
    exemption_amount(EA),
    Total is 2 * EA.

/* taxable_income(AGI, StandardDeduction, Exemptions, Taxable) */
taxable_income(AGI, SD, EX, TI) :-
    Temp is AGI - SD - EX,
    ( Temp > 0 -> TI = Temp ; TI = 0 ).

/* income_tax_joint(TaxableIncome, Tax) –– §1(a) brackets */
income_tax_joint(TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 36900 ->
        Tax is TI * 0.15
    ; TI =< 89150 ->
        Excess is TI - 36900,
        Tax is 5535 + Excess * 0.28
    ; TI =< 140000 ->
        Excess is TI - 89150,
        Tax is 20165 + Excess * 0.31
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        Excess is TI - 140000,
        Tax is 35928.50 + Excess * 0.36
    ;   Excess is TI - 250000,
        Tax is 75528.50 + Excess * 0.396
    ).

/* ---------- Facts from the case ---------- */
income("Alice", 2003, 54313).
income("Bob",   2003,     0).

spouse("Alice", "Bob").
spouse("Bob",   "Alice").

files_joint_return("Alice", 2003).   /* they filed a joint return */

/* ---------- Main tax predicate for this specific filing situation ---------- */
tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    files_joint_return(Person, Year),
    spouse(Person, Spouse),
    income(Person, Year, Inc1),
    income(Spouse, Year, Inc2),
    AGI is Inc1 + Inc2,
    standard_deduction(joint, SD),
    exemptions(Person, Spouse, Year, EX),
    taxable_income(AGI, SD, EX, TI),
    income_tax_joint(TI, Tax).

/* ---------- Query ---------- */
:- tax("Alice", 2003, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

text: Alice has paid $4512 to Bob for work done from Feb 1st, 2005 to Sep 2nd, 2005. In 2005, Alice was paid $133200. Alice is allowed itemized deductions of $2939 and $8744.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2005?
reference_prolog:
/* -------------  Statutory rules ------------- */

/* exemption amount (§151(d)(1)) */
exemption_amount(Year, 2000) :- Year < 2018.          /* years before 2018, simplified for this case */

/* personal exemptions allowed to Alice */
personal_exemptions(alice, 2005, 1).

/* ---------- income-tax rate brackets (§1) --------- */
/* bracket(Status, LowerInclusive , UpperExclusive , BaseTax , MargRate).   */
/* “unmarried individuals (other than surviving spouses and heads of households)” (§1(c)) */
bracket(unmarried,    0,       22100,      0,        0.15).
bracket(unmarried,    22100,   53500,      3315,     0.28).
bracket(unmarried,    53500,   115000,     12107,    0.31).
bracket(unmarried,    115000,  250000,     31172,    0.36).
bracket(unmarried,    250000,  inf,        79772,    0.396).

/* --------------- FUTA (§3301, §3306) --------------- */
futa_cap(7000).           /* §3306(b)(1) – wages above 7,000 ignored        */
futa_rate(0.06).          /* §3301                                         */

futa_taxable_wages(W, TW) :- futa_cap(Cap), ( W < Cap -> TW = W ; TW = Cap ).

/* ------------- Facts for the case ------------- */
gross_income(alice, 2005, 133200).

itemized_deduction(alice, 2005, 2939).
itemized_deduction(alice, 2005, 8744).

/* Wages Alice paid to employee Bob */
wages_paid(alice, 2005, bob, 4512).

/* ---------- Helper predicates ---------- */

/* total itemized deductions */
total_itemized(Person, Year, Total) :-
    findall(D, itemized_deduction(Person, Year, D), L),
    sum_list(L, Total).

/* total exemptions */
total_exemptions(Person, Year, Total) :-
    personal_exemptions(Person, Year, N),
    exemption_amount(Year, EA),
    Total is N * EA.

/* taxable income (§63) : GI – itemized – exemptions */
taxable_income(Person, Year, TI) :-
    gross_income(Person, Year, GI),
    total_itemized(Person, Year, ID),
    total_exemptions(Person, Year, EX),
    TI is GI - ID - EX.

/* filing status – for this case we assume unmarried (§1(c)) */
filing_status(alice, 2005, unmarried).

/* income-tax owed under §1 */
income_tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    bracket(Status, Lower, Upper, Base, Rate),
    (  (Upper == inf , TI >= Lower)
    ;  (Upper \== inf, TI >= Lower, TI < Upper)
    ),
    Tax is Base + Rate * (TI - Lower).

/* total FUTA taxable wages for all employees */
total_futa_wages(Person, Year, Total) :-
    findall(TW,
            ( wages_paid(Person, Year, _Emp, W),
              futa_taxable_wages(W, TW)
            ),
            L),
    sum_list(L, Total).

futa_tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    total_futa_wages(Person, Year, TW),
    futa_rate(R),
    Tax is TW * R.

/* --------------- Overall tax --------------- */
tax(Person, Year, TotalTax) :-
    income_tax(Person, Year, IncomeTax),
    futa_tax(Person, Year, FutaTax),
    TotalTax is IncomeTax + FutaTax.

/* --------------- Query --------------- */
:- tax(alice, 2005, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

text: Alice has a son, Bob, who in 2015 lives with her at the house she maintains as her home. Alice was paid $73200 in 2015 as an employee of Bertha's Mussels in Baltimore, Maryland, USA. Alice takes the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2015?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ----------  STATUTORY RULES (only the parts needed here) ---------- */

/* exemption amount (§151(d)(1)) */
exemption_amount(Year, 2000) :- Year < 2018.        % special zero rule starts 2018

/* standard deduction (§63(c)(2),(7)) */
standard_deduction(Year, head_of_household, 4400)  :- Year < 2018.
standard_deduction(Year, head_of_household, 18000) :- Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025.

/* 2015 head-of-household tax brackets (§1(b)) 
   bracket(Status, LowerInclusive, UpperInclusiveOrInf, MarginalRate, BaseTax) */
bracket(head_of_household,        0,   29600, 0.15,     0).
bracket(head_of_household,    29600,   76400, 0.28,  4440).
bracket(head_of_household,    76400,  127500, 0.31, 17544).
bracket(head_of_household,   127500,  250000, 0.36, 33385).
bracket(head_of_household,   250000,      inf, 0.396,77485).

/* ----------  FACTS FROM THE CASE ---------- */

wages("Alice", 2015, 73200).

% Filing-status facts
unmarried("Alice", 2015).
qualifying_child("Alice", 2015, "Bob").   % Bob lived with Alice >½ year
status("Alice", 2015, head_of_household). % derived from facts above

/* Dependents */
dependent("Alice", 2015, "Bob").

% Every taxpayer is always his/her own exemption
exempt_person("Alice", 2015, "Alice").
exempt_person(TaxPayer, Year, Dep) :- dependent(TaxPayer, Year, Dep).

/* ----------  GENERIC COMPUTATIONS ---------- */

agi(Person, Year, AGI) :- wages(Person, Year, AGI).

number_of_exemptions(Person, Year, N) :-
    findall(Who, exempt_person(Person, Year, Who), List),
    length(List, N).

personal_exemption_total(Person, Year, Total) :-
    exemption_amount(Year, EA),
    number_of_exemptions(Person, Year, N),
    Total is EA * N.

standard_deduction_total(Person, Year, Deduction) :-
    status(Person, Year, Status),
    standard_deduction(Year, Status, Deduction).

taxable_income(Person, Year, TI) :-
    agi(Person, Year, AGI),
    standard_deduction_total(Person, Year, SD),
    personal_exemption_total(Person, Year, PET),
    TI0 is AGI - SD - PET,
    TI  is max(0, TI0).

/* locate correct bracket and compute tax */
tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    status(Person, Year, Status),
    taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
    bracket(Status, Lower, Upper, Rate, Base),
    TI > Lower,
    (Upper == inf ; TI =< Upper),
    TaxFloat is Base + Rate * (TI - Lower),
    Tax is round(TaxFloat).   % cents-free whole-dollar tax for this problem

/* ----------  QUERY ---------- */

:- tax("Alice", 2015, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```

text: In 2017, Alice was paid $75845. Alice has a son, Bob. From September 1st, 2015 to November 3rd, 2019, Alice and Bob lived in the same home, which Alice maintained. In 2017, Alice takes the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ---------- Statutory constants ---------- */

/* filing-status(Taxpayer, Year, Status).  We will derive this from facts  */
status_bracket_amount(head_of_household,
        [ bracket(0,      29600, 0.15, 0),
          bracket(29600,  76400, 0.28, 4440),
          bracket(76400, 127500, 0.31, 17544),
          bracket(127500,250000, 0.36, 33385),
          bracket(250000,  inf, 0.396, 77485)
        ]).

/* standard_deduction(Status, Year, Amount).  (Only 2017 rules needed) */
standard_deduction(head_of_household, 2017, 4400).
standard_deduction(unmarried,        2017, 3000).

/* personal exemption amount for 2017 */
personal_exemption_amount(2017, 2000).

/* ---------- Case facts for 2017 ---------- */
income("Alice", 2017, 75845).

/* Household & dependency facts */
principal_abode_same_household("Alice", "Bob", 2017).
qualifying_child("Alice", "Bob", 2017).

/* Alice’s marital state */
marital_status("Alice", 2017, unmarried).

/* Alice maintained more than half the cost of the household in 2017 */
maintains_household_cost_half("Alice", 2017).

/* ---------- Derived information ---------- */

/* Alice is head of household if: unmarried, has qualifying child living with
   her >½ year, and she paid >½ the cost of keeping up the home.             */
filing_status(T, Y, head_of_household) :-
    marital_status(T, Y, unmarried),
    qualifying_child(T, _, Y),
    principal_abode_same_household(T, _, Y),
    maintains_household_cost_half(T, Y).

/* Number of personal exemptions = 1 for taxpayer + each dependent. */
num_personal_exemptions(T, Y, N) :-
    findall(Dep, qualifying_child(T, Dep, Y), L),
    length(L, DepN),
    N is DepN + 1.                /* +1 for taxpayer herself */

/* Total exemption deduction */
total_exemption_deduction(T, Y, Deduction) :-
    num_personal_exemptions(T, Y, N),
    personal_exemption_amount(Y, Amt),
    Deduction is N * Amt.

/* Standard deduction amount according to filing status */
standard_deduction_amount(T, Y, Amount) :-
    filing_status(T, Y, Status),
    standard_deduction(Status, Y, Amount).

/* Taxable income = gross income − standard deduction − personal exemptions */
taxable_income(T, Y, Taxable) :-
    income(T, Y, Gross),
    standard_deduction_amount(T, Y, SD),
    total_exemption_deduction(T, Y, Exempt),
    Taxable is Gross - SD - Exempt.

/* Compute tax from taxable income using bracket schedule for status */
tax_from_brackets(Status, TaxableIncome, Tax) :-
    status_bracket_amount(Status, Brackets),
    compute_tax(Brackets, TaxableIncome, Tax).

compute_tax([bracket(Low, High, Rate, Base)|_], Income, Tax) :-
    (   (High == inf ; Income =< High),
        Income >= Low
    ->  Tax is Base + (Income - Low) * Rate
    ).
compute_tax([bracket(_, High, _, _) | Rest], Income, Tax) :-
    Income > High,
    compute_tax(Rest, Income, Tax).

/* Overall tax predicate */
tax(T, Y, Tax) :-
    filing_status(T, Y, Status),
    taxable_income(T, Y, Taxable),
    tax_from_brackets(Status, Taxable, Tax).

/* ---------- Query ---------- */
:- tax("Alice", 2017, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.
```
